spells.description: As Light Muscle Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Regenerate Muscle
spells.description: Target regenerates any one muscle (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Strength III
spells.description: As Strength I, except the bonus is +15 (resulting in +30 for melee OB).

spells.name: Light Muscle Healing True
spells.description: As Light Muscle Healing, except the recovery period is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Joining True ‡ *
spells.description: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Strength True
spells.description: As Strength I, except the bonus is +20 (resulting in +40 for melee OB).

spells.name: Severe Muscle Healing True
spells.description: As Light Muscle Healing True, except an injury incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Mass Regenerate Muscle
spells.description: Target regenerates all tendons and muscles in his body (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Mass Muscle Healing True
spells.description: All injuries to muscle ROLEMASTER or tendons are cleaned, protected, and bound as effectiveUNIFIED ly as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed,  the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and Spell Law the recovery period is reduced to rounds rather than days. 77 6.2 - Closed Channeling NERVE & ORGAN LAW

spells.name: Diagnosis
spells.description: Diagnoses target’s wounds, injuries, and diseases allowing a +20 bonus to all Medicine maneuvers made by the caster to treat the target’s diagnosed condition(s). Caster can take a round to advise someone else on treating the target, providing a +10 bonus to treat the target.

spells.name: Infertility
spells.description: Target becomes completely infertile for the spell’s duration.

spells.name: Light Organ Relief
spells.description: An injury to an organ (e.g. eye, ear, liver but not the brain or nerves) incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.

spells.name: Distraction
spells.description: Target is at -30 for all actions.

spells.name: Limb Preservation
spells.description: Allows caster to prevent deterioration of one limb (even while severed); requires use of the Blood, Bone, and Muscle Limb Preservation spells as well. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Medium Organ
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, but an UNIFIED injury incurring a penalty up to -40. This can be used to cure  most causes of infertility (if the requisite organs are still presSpell Law ent).

spells.name: Sense Enhancement
spells.description: One of target’s natural senses (e.g. vision, hearing, touch) is honed and perfected for the duration. Perception maneuvers based exclusively on that sense gain a +20 bonus, while Perception that involves that sense and others 78 gain a +10 bonus.

spells.name: Restart Heart
spells.description: Target’s heart will start beating after having stopped. This does not repair any heart disease or damage that caused the initial stoppage.

spells.name: Light Nerve Relief
spells.description: An injury to the brain or nerves incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.

spells.name: Piercing Gaze
spells.description: Target can see twice as far through fog, mist, water, or other semi-transparent substances that would otherwise reduce their range of vision.

spells.name: Severe Organ
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, but an injury incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Joining ‡ *
spells.description: Caster may reattach one severed limb. The Joining spell from the Bone, Blood, and Muscle spell lists must be cast for the limb to be functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

spells.name: Light Organ
spells.description: An injury to an organ incurring a penalty up to -20 is treated as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Coma Recovery
spells.description: Target comes out of a coma or unconsciousness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure. Process takes 1-10 hours and target is at -50 to Endurance rolls for 1-10 days.

spells.name: Medium Organ Healing
spells.description: As Light Organ Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.

spells.name: Light Nerve
spells.description: An injury to the brain or nerves incurring a penalty up to -20 is treated as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives +50 bonus on their recovery roll, and recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Sense Restoration
spells.description: Target regains function in one sensory organ, so long as that sensory organ has not been completely destroyed (in which case Organ Regeneration is required). Recovery time is instantaneous.

spells.name: Severe Organ
spells.description: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Paralysis Cures
spells.description: Caster can cure any paralysis problem. The recovery time is 1-10 days. Treat as -100 injury for progress of recovery.

spells.name: Medium Nerve
spells.description: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to -40.

spells.name: Joining True ‡ *
spells.description: As Joining, except recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Severe Nerve
spells.description: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Organ Regeneration
spells.description: Target will regenerate any one organ (external or internal) other than the brain or nerves. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Paralysis Cures
spells.description: As Paralysis Cures, except recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Brain Regeneration
spells.description: Target will regenerate his brain. Spell must be cast before target dies. Target will be in a “healing coma” while brain grows back, and memories may be lost depending on the severity of the damage. Recovery time is 1-10 days. 6.2 - Closed Channeling SYMBOLIC WAYS

spells.name: Analyze Symbol
spells.description: Caster learns what spell is emplaced within one Symbol within 50'. The Symbol will not be triggered (unless specifically created to be triggered by an Analyze Symbol spell).

spells.name: Spell Prime
spells.description: This spell “primes” the next spell cast by the caster – that spell is treated as if it were instantaneous. The PP cost of this spell is equal to the highest level that the caster wants to have primed. For example, if the caster wants any fourth (or lower) level spell to be treated as instantaneous, this spell will cost 4 PP. In addition, when casting this spell, the caster must pray for one minute per PP spent (e.g., if the caster spends 4 PP on this spell, he must pray for 4 minutes before the spell is completed.) The effects of this spell end when the caster casts his next spell (even if the next spell does not benefit from this spell due to being of too high a level or already an instantaneous spell).

spells.name: Unsymbol I
spells.description: Caster can remove one Symbol I (an emplaced 1st level spell). The Symbol’s RR is based on the level of the spell in the Symbol spell (the target level) and the level of the Unsymbol spell (the attack level).

spells.name: Symbol I
spells.description: Caster can emplace one 1st level spell on an inanimate, non-mobile slab of stone (block, piece, etc.) weighing at least 2,000 pounds. Only one Symbol may be placed on each continuous slab. The stone may not be moved appreciably without destroying the Symbol. Within one minute, the spell to be emplaced must also be cast (within 10'). A Symbol can be triggered (once per day) by one of the following (decided by caster): time period, specified movements, specified sounds, touch, reading, violent actions in the area, etc. The triggering range is normally the range of the emplaced spell or 10' (whichever is greater). The effective level of the embedded spell (for the purposes of RRs, Duration, etc.) is the level of the highest level spell the Symbol can hold. For example, a first level spell placed in a 6.2 Closed Symbol using Symbol I is cast at 1st level (regardless of the level of Channeling the original caster), while a first level spell place using Symbol VII is cast at 7th level. If the spell is an elemental attack, the OB is normally +0. However, the attack can be focused on a location (as opposed to simply the person who triggers the symbol); in this case, the OB is +50. The Symbol is usually visible, but is only required to be visible if reading is the trigger.

spells.name: Recharge Symbol
spells.description: This spell must be cast on a Symbol that has already been triggered this day. Once this spell is cast on the Symbol, the Symbol is recharged and can be triggered again. A given caster cannot recharge the same Symbol more than once per day, but one caster may recharge any number of Symbols and a Symbol may be recharged by multiple casters.

spells.name: Unsymbol II
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-II may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol II
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-2nd level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Unsymbol V
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-V may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol III
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-3rd level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Analyze Symbol
spells.description: As Analyze Symbol, except caster learns what spells are emplaced in all Symbols within a 50'R.

spells.name: Symbol V
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-5th level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Unsymbol VI
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-VI may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol VI
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-6th level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Unsymbol VII
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-VII may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol VII
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-7th level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Unsymbol VIII
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-VIII may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol VIII
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-8th level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Unsymbol X
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-X may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol IX
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-9th level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Symbol X
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-10th level spell may be emplaced.

spells.name: Lord Research
spells.description: This spell allows for the creation of random magic items. See Treasure Law, Section 4.4.

spells.name: Lord Unsymbol
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except a Symbol I-X or a Lord ROLEMASTER Symbol may be removed. UNIFIED

spells.name: Lord Symbol
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a 1st-20th level spell may be  emplaced. Spell Law

spells.name: Unsymbol True
spells.description: As Unsymbol I, except any Symbol may be removed.

spells.name: Symbol True
spells.description: As Symbol I, except a spell with a level up to half that of the caster (e.g., a 50th level caster can emplace a 25th level spell) may be emplaced. 79 6.3 - Cleric Base CHANNELS

spells.name: Create Holy Water
spells.description: When this spell is cast, up to four fluid ounces of clean, clear water are transformed into holy water. This spell takes one hour to cast. If the water is not placed into a clean receptacle, its holy nature is ruined.

spells.name: Calming
spells.description: Caster points his arm (elbow locked and fist clenched) at the target and channels raw power from his power source (usually a deity). The target of the spell may take no aggressive/offensive action, and will only fight if attacked.

spells.name: Channels I *
spells.description: When this spell is cast, the 1st level spell that the caster begins to cast (or prepare) the next round will be “channeled” to another spell user of the Channeling realm. The “channeled” spell is transmitted to the other spell user when it would normally take effect, and must be used ROLEMASTER by the receiving spell user immediately or delayed as described in Section UNIFIED 4.8 (treating the receiving spell user as the caster of the spell). All normal  restrictions on the transmitted spell apply. The caster of the Channels spell Spell Law must be able to see the receiver or know exactly where the receiver is (direction and distance or specific place). The receiver must know exactly when the spell is coming (but need not know what the spell sent is). For example, if a Cleric was at a certain altar at a certain time (midnight of full moon) ready to receive a spell; then another spell user (or deity) who knew this could use Channels I to send a spell to that Cleric. Because the spell 80 is passed through the aura of the receiving character, the spell is cast at the level of the receiving character (including any level-based parameters).

spells.name: Power Channel
spells.description: As Channels I, except instead of sending a spell, the caster may send up to 1 PP/level to the other spell user (who need not use them right away). If the PP sent put the receiver over his normal PP maximum, excess PP are lost and the receiving individual suffers one hit of damage per PP in excess of his maximum.

spells.name: Stunning
spells.description: As Calming, except target is stunned (-50).

spells.name: Channels III *
spells.description: As Channels I, except a 1st-3rd level spell may be transmitted.

spells.name: Power Channel
spells.description: As Channels I, except the caster can send up to 2 PP/level.

spells.name: Golden Slumbers
spells.description: As Stunning, except target falls into a deep sleep. The spell duration of this sleep is magical (and the target cannot be awakened in any normal fashion). After this magical sleep, the target may be awakened normally, but if undisturbed, the target will sleep for a normal length sleep period (eight hours for humans).

spells.name: Create Holy Water True
spells.description: As Create Holy Water, except that the holy water is simply created without the need for clean water to transform.

spells.name: Channels V *
spells.description: As Channels I, except a 1st-5th level spell may be transmitted.

spells.name: Blinding Channel
spells.description: As Stunning, except target is blinded for 1 minute per 5 failure.

spells.name: Holy Smite
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Target takes a B Holy critical.

spells.name: Channels VIII *
spells.description: As Channels I, except a 1st-8th level spell may be transmitted.

spells.name: Absolution
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Target’s “soul” is torn from the target’s body (or other physical location if the soul is otherwise housed); it remains in whatever place “souls” go for 1 week/10 failure. The soul can be returned early only through Lifegiving or Reuniting. Target is unconscious until soul is restored; even subconscious activity (i.e., healing, etc.) suffers a -75 penalty.

spells.name: Holy Shout *
spells.description: All beings not of the caster’s own “alignment” (religious persuasion) are affected. All targets failing to resist by 1-40 are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; those failing by more than 40 are unconscious for 1 rnd/5 failure.

spells.name: Item Returning
spells.description: Target item (weighing no more than 10 pounds) will return to a designated location. This spell has two possible durations (determined at the time of casting). The caster may send (i.e., teleport) the target item to a designated location at the time of casting (with an instantaneous duration), or the caster can designate that the target item will return to the caster when the possessor of the item has completed a specific task (specified at the time of casting).

spells.name: Channels X *
spells.description: As Channels I, except a 1st-10th level spell may be transmitted.

spells.name: Sanctuary *
spells.description: Caster is returned to a predefined place (this is a type of teleportation). Caster must have lived in the place for 30 days (meditating 16 hours each day) to define it as the caster’s “sanctuary”. Only one such place may be defined at any one time; to change it, the new place must be defined as above.

spells.name: Item Rereturning
spells.description: As Item Returning, except object will stay at the designated location (set at the time of casting) for up to 1 round per level. At the end of this time, the object will go back to where it “returned” from.

spells.name: Absolution Pure
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] As Absolution, except the “soul” can only be brought back through Lifegiving or Reuniting.

spells.name: Sanctuary's Return
spells.description: For up to 1 round/level after having used Sanctuary, the caster may cast this spell and be returned to within 20' of the point at which the Sanctuary spell was cast.

spells.name: Lord Channels
spells.description: As Channels I, except a 1st-20th level spell may be transmitted.

spells.name: Holy Smite True
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Target takes an E Holy critical.

spells.name: Channels True *
spells.description: As Channels I, except there is no limit on the level of spell that may be transmitted.

spells.name: Holy Bridge
spells.description: Opens a direct channel to the caster’s deity; results depend on the deity’s personality, motives, and powers. Any spell the deity can cast may be transmitted through the gate. 6.3 - Cleric Base COMMUNAL WAYS

spells.name: Guess *
spells.description: When faced with a choice about which the caster has little or no information (e.g., which corridor leads to the wine cellar), the caster may cast this spell and the Gamemaster will determine which choice the caster actually makes, biasing the choice favorably by 25% (e.g., if there are two choices, roll 1-100: correct on 26-100, incorrect on 01-25). The caster must have a limited number of options (i.e., finite and countable). After the spell is cast, the caster may not cast another Guess for the same information until his experience level has increased.

spells.name: Faithful Tongue
spells.description: Caster can speak and understand the language(s) of any supernatural entities favorable to his religion with the same fluency as he has in his native language.

spells.name: Intuitions I
spells.description: Caster gains a vision of what will probably happen in the next minute if the caster takes a specified action.

spells.name: Dream I
spells.description: Caster has a dream relating to a topic decided upon just before retiring; limited to once per night. The caster must sleep for a normal sleep cycle (minimum of four hours) for this spell to work.

spells.name: Intuitions III
spells.description: As Intuitions I, except the caster gets to gaze 3 minutes into the future.

spells.name: Death’s Tale
spells.description: Caster gets a vision of the events surrounding the death of any 1 dead being through the eyes of the deceased, including a view of the killer, if any. Caster must be within 10' of the corpse. Corpse must have died within a number of years equal to the caster’s level.

spells.name: Channel Opening
spells.description: Caster can open a channel with any 1 known being the caster is acquainted with. This spell will enable the caster to learn the specific location (direction and distance only) of the other being, making it possible for the caster to use a Channels spell (see Channels list). Note that this spell does not allow any sort of communication, although the target will know that a Channel has been opened to him.

spells.name: Intuitions V
spells.description: As Intuitions I, except caster gets to gaze 5 minutes into the future.

spells.name: Holy Hands
spells.description: Caster gains a +50 to his Attunement skill as applied to magic items tied to his religion (i.e., those based in Channeling magic and usable by the caster).

spells.name: Dream III
spells.description: As Dream I, except the limit is 3 dreams/night on 6.3 Cleric different topics. Base

spells.name: Intuitions X
spells.description: As Intuitions I, except caster gets to gaze 10 minutes into the future.

spells.name: Commune I
spells.description: Caster receives a “yes” or “no” to a question on a single topic; usable but once a day. This answer normally comes from the caster’s deity or some supernatural subordinate of that deity.

spells.name: Magic Basin I
spells.description: The target of this spell is a small pool of clear, clean water. The water must be calm (and remain so for the duration of the spell). The caster may designate a topic and a vision will appear in the pool that relates to the topic. This vision normally takes 5-50 rounds. This vision may be seen by anyone looking into the pool.

spells.name: Share Thoughts
spells.description: Caster can mentally communicate with one other person, who must be a member of the same religion.

spells.name: Channel Opening
spells.description: As Channel Opening I, except range is 10 miles per level.

spells.name: Commune II
spells.description: As Commune I, except caster may ask two questions.

spells.name: Dream V
spells.description: As Dream I, except the limit is five dreams/night on different topics.

spells.name: Magic Basin II
spells.description: As Magic Basin I, except up to two topics may be designated (5-50 rounds are required for each vision).

spells.name: Death’s Tale True
spells.description: Caster gets a vision of the events surrounding the death of any 1 dead being within 10 feet. Caster gets an understanding of the reason(s) the deceased died, exactly who the killer was (if any), and who was ultimately responsible (if anyone). The vision of who is ultimately responsible will be a quick montage of the connections between the killer and the person ultimately responsible. Corpse may be of any age.

spells.name: Commune True
spells.description: As Commune I, except caster can receive a more detailed answer (e.g., a name or short phrase) to a question involving only one concept.

spells.name: Intuitions True
spells.description: As Intuitions I, except caster may gaze into the future for a number of minutes equal to the caster’s level.

spells.name: Channel Opening
spells.description: As Channel Opening I, except caster can open a channel to anyone the caster chooses, regardless of whether or not the caster is acquainted with the being. Caster must specify the type of being sought (e.g., a priest of a specific deity, a warrior with blue eyes, etc.), range is 1 mile per level. Channel will open to the nearest being matching the description, if any.

spells.name: Channel Opening
spells.description: As Channel Opening I, except range is 100 miles per level.

spells.name: Magic Basin
spells.description: As Magic Basin I, except up to one topic for every 10 levels (or fraction thereof) of the caster may be designated, and the visions take less objective time (although they will seem as subjectively long to the viewer), requiring only 1 round per vision. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED

spells.name: High Prayer
spells.description: Caster receives detailed information on a single  topic question. This information comes directly from the caster’s Spell Law deity. 81 6.3 - Cleric Base LIFE MASTERY

spells.name: Life Awareness
spells.description: Caster can detect presence and number of living entities (Tiny animals or larger) within the area of effect and can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round. Caster will be aware of mundane life, such as insects, very small animals and plants, but not of their exact number, only general types.

spells.name: Preservation I
spells.description: Caster can preserve a dead body, preventing decomposition. Preservation can only be used on bodies of living beings that are or were capable of life, it cannot be used to preserve other organic matter.

spells.name: Heal Life Essence
spells.description: The cast can reduce a stat penalty due to Life Drain by 1. A target can only receive one Heal Life Essence spell per day, and can only have a specific stat affected once per week.

spells.name: Lifekeeping I
spells.description: Caster prevents the soul of a target from leaving its body (thus preventing actual death), although the body will continue to take damage (from bleeding or other effects) for the duration. If the body is not capable of sustaining ROLEMASTER life at the end of the duration, then the soul will depart. This UNIFIED will prevent Absolution of any kind, unless the Absolution  makes a successful RR against this spell. Spell Law

spells.name: Infuse Life II
spells.description: Target is infused with a part of the caster’s life force. Caster takes a -2 to a stat bonus of his choice, and the target gets a +2 to any stat bonus (which does not have to be the same stat bonus as the caster reduces). The target receives the benefits for the duration of the spell, but the caster’s penalty 82 can only be removed via Heal Life Essence. Each stat can only be under the benefit of a single Infuse Life at a time, although the caster could have several penalties due to multiple Infuse Life spells being cast.

spells.name: Resurrection
spells.description: Target is temporarily revived by returning the soul to the body for a brief time, regardless of the condition of the body (if body is terribly damaged the target may not be able to take advantage of his brief return to the living).

spells.name: Preservation II
spells.description: As Preservation I, except for duration.

spells.name: Analyze Life
spells.description: Caster can analyze a life form he can see or has previously detected with Life Awareness. He understands the nature of the life form (race), its approximate level, and if its life is currently in danger. Caster can analyze one being each round for the duration of the spell (requires Concentration).

spells.name: Sacrifice *
spells.description: Caster can restore life to a recently dead body (within 1 rnd/lvl) by giving up his own life. The target’s soul is returned to his body with no ill effects (although he is still subject to any wounds he had). The caster’s soul departs, however this spell also acts as a Preservation II spell on the caster’s body. Duration is for the Preservation effect; the other effects are permanent.

spells.name: Heal Life Essence
spells.description: As Heal Life Essence I except that a stat penalty can be reduced by 2.

spells.name: Lifekeeping II
spells.description: As Lifekeeping I, except for duration.

spells.name: Infuse Life IV
spells.description: As Infuse Life I, except the caster’s stat bonus is reduced by 4 and the target’s stat bonus is increased by

spells.name: Preservation III
spells.description: As Preservation I, except for duration.

spells.name: Lifegiving I
spells.description: Caster can return the soul of a being (that has not been dead for more than 1 min/lvl) to its body. If the body is not capable of sustaining life, then the soul will depart again. The target gets a -15 to all stats (which can be restored via Heal Life Essence).

spells.name: Heal Life Essence
spells.description: As Heal Life Essence I, except penalty can be reduced by 3.

spells.name: Lifekeeping III
spells.description: As Lifekeeping I, except for duration.

spells.name: Infuse Life VI
spells.description: As Infuse Life I, except caster’s stat bonus is reduced by 6 and the target’s stat bonus increased by 6.

spells.name: Preservation True
spells.description: As Preservation I, except for duration.

spells.name: Lifegiving II
spells.description: As Lifegiving I, except body can have been dead for up to 1 day/level.

spells.name: Heal Life Essence
spells.description: As Heal Life Essence I except penalty can be reduced by 5.

spells.name: Lifekeeping True
spells.description: As Lifekeeping I, except for duration.

spells.name: Infuse Life X
spells.description: As Infuse Life I, except caster’s stat bonus is reduced by 10 and the target’s stat bonus increased by 10.

spells.name: Lifegiving III
spells.description: As Lifegiving I, except body can have been dead for up to 1 month/level.

spells.name: Heal Life Essence
spells.description: As Heal Life Essence I, except the entire stat penalty is removed.

spells.name: Lifegiving True
spells.description: As Lifegiving I, except there is no time limit. 6.3 - Cleric Base PROTECTIONS

spells.name: Prayer I
spells.description: Target gets a +5 bonus to RRs and maneuver rolls. The duration of this spell is 10 minutes per level if cast on a non-mobile target. If cast on a mobile target, the spell will last as long as the caster concentrates. If cast on a non-mobile target that is moved, the spell is dispelled. If the caster is the target, the duration is one minute per level and concentration is not required.

spells.name: Bless I
spells.description: As Prayer I, except bonus applies to DB (Defensive Bonus) and maneuver rolls.

spells.name: Resistance I
spells.description: As Prayer I, except the bonus applies to RRs and DB.

spells.name: Heat Resistance *
spells.description: Target is protected from natural heat up to 80°C (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). For temperatures above 80°C, subtract 60°C to determine the effective temperature for the target. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. heat spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental fire/heat attacks. If the caster is not the target, concentration is required. Duration is as for Prayer I.

spells.name: Cold Resistance *
spells.description: Target is protected from natural cold down to -10°C (treat as if target were in 20°C temperature). For temperatures below 20°C, add 30°C to determine the effective temperature for the target. Target also receives +20 to all RRs vs. cold spells/attacks and a +20 to DB vs. elemental cold/ice attacks. If the caster is not the target, concentration is required. Duration is as for Prayer I.

spells.name: Sacred Protection
spells.description: Target is protected from physical attacks as if armored. Target is granted the benefits of AT 4, but is not encumbered in any way, as he is not actually armored.

spells.name: Resist Spell
spells.description: Target gains a +30 bonus to his RRs against a specific spell (spells whose name differs only by Roman numeral are considered the same spell for this purpose, but a “Lord” or 6.3 Cleric “True” version is not). Base

spells.name: Temperature Sphere
spells.description: All who are within the sphere are allowed the benefits of either a Heat Resistance or a Cold Resistance spell (must be the same for all targets). The sphere is not mobile.

spells.name: Sacred Protection
spells.description: As Sacred Protection I, except AT is 6.

spells.name: Protective Sphere
spells.description: All within the radius are allowed the benefits of Prayer I or Bless I or Resistance I (must be the same for all targets). The sphere is not mobile.

spells.name: Prayer III
spells.description: As Prayer I, except total bonus is +15 and the spell may be divided among 1-3 targets (1 target would get a +15 bonus, 3 targets could each get +5 bonuses, etc.).

spells.name: Bless III
spells.description: As Bless I, except total bonus is +15 and spell may be divided among 1-3 targets as in Prayer III.

spells.name: Resistance III
spells.description: As Resistance I, except total bonus is +15 and the spell may be divided among 1-3 targets as in Prayer III.

spells.name: Sacred Protection
spells.description: As Sacred Protection I, except AT is

spells.name: Temperature Sphere
spells.description: As Temperature Sphere I, except radius is 20'.

spells.name: Greater Protective
spells.description: As Protective Sphere I, except radius is 30'.

spells.name: Protective Sphere
spells.description: As Protective Sphere I, except all within the radius are allowed the benefits of Prayer III or Bless III or Resistance III (i.e., each protected individual gains a +15 bonus).

spells.name: Prayer V
spells.description: As Prayer I, except total bonus is +25 and the spell may be divided among 1-5 targets (1 target would get a +25 bonus, two targets could get a +13 bonus for one and a +12 bonus for the other, etc.)

spells.name: Sacred Protection
spells.description: As Sacred Protection I, except AT is 10.

spells.name: Bless V
spells.description: As Bless I, except total bonus is +25 and the spell may be divided among 1-5 targets as in Prayer V.

spells.name: Resistance V
spells.description: As Resistance I, except total bonus is +25 and the spell may be divided among 1-5 targets as in Prayer V.

spells.name: Protective Sphere
spells.description: As Protective Sphere I, except all within the radius are allowed the benefits of Prayer V or Bless V or Resistance V (i.e., each protected individual gains a +25 bonus).

spells.name: Temperature Sphere
spells.description: As Temperature Sphere I, except radius is 10' per level and the effect is as both Heat Resistance and Cold Resistance, depending on what temperatures occur to protect against. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Mass Sacred Protection
spells.description: As Sacred Protection I, except UNIFIED  multiple targets may be given the equivalent of any armor from AT 2 to AT 10, with the restriction that the total ATs provided Spell Law must be no greater than the level of the caster (e.g., a 40th level caster could provide AT 10 to four targets or AT 5 to eight targets or AT 8 to three targets and AT 4 to four targets).

spells.name: Protections True
spells.description: Target gets a +30 bonus to RRs, maneuver rolls, and DB. 83 6.3 - Cleric Base REPULSIONS

spells.name: Repel Undead V
spells.description: Causes up to 5 Undead to flee or disintegrate. Up to five “points” of Undead may be affected: a “Class I” Undead counts as 1 point, a “Class II” Undead counts as 2 points, etc. “Animated Dead” are treated as “Class I” Undead but are not entitled to an RR (they automatically disintegrate if the spell does not fail). Otherwise, each target is entitled to an RR: if it fails by 1-50, it will flee (targets that cannot flee remain motionless) for 1 round per 1 failure. If it fails by more than 50, it disintegrates. The RR of any undead creature can be modified by concentrating the effects of the spell on that target: for each additional point of the spell allocated to a target (beyond those normally needed to affect it), that target’s RR is modified by -5. Thus Repel Undead V could affect 5 Class I Undead with no RR modifier or 1 Class 1 Undead with an RR modifier of -20 or 1 Class II Undead with an RR modifier of -5 and 2 Class I Undead with no RR modifier or any other combination of targets providing no more than 5 points were allocated. Once an undead has made an RR against a given caster’s Repel Undead spell, it cannot be affected by a Repel Undead spell from that caster again until one of the following happens: the caster gains a level, the undead is “killed” and has to reconstitute itself, or the ROLEMASTER caster converts to a new power source (i.e., changes religion). UNIFIED – When the caster is a target of a spell from  2. Cancel Channeling * the Realm of Channeling, the “incoming” spell must first make an Spell Law RR before the caster makes an RR, using the level of this spell as the attacking level and the level of the incoming spell as the defending level. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is 84 considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.

spells.name: Cancel Channeling *
spells.description: When the caster is a target of a spell from the Realm of Channeling, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR, using the level of this spell as the attacking level and the level of the incoming spell as the defending level. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster with this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.

spells.name: Anti-Channels I
spells.description: Cancels a Channels I spell being transmitted or received within range. Note that the Channels I spell gets no RR.

spells.name: Neutralize Curse
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Nullifies a curse for the duration of this spell. The curse is not dispelled and takes effect again once the duration of this spell expires.

spells.name: Anti-Channels III
spells.description: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels III.

spells.name: Repel Undead IX
spells.description: As Repel Undead V, except 9 points of undead may be affected.

spells.name: Uncurse
spells.description: Dispels a curse if the curse fails an RR. The curse’s level is the level of the caster of the curse. If the curse is not dispelled, the caster cannot try again until he has gone up another level of experience.

spells.name: Dispel Channeling
spells.description: Any active spell from the realm of Channeling that is on the target must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.

spells.name: Neutralize Curse
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] As Neutralize Curse I, except curse is nullified for one hour per level.

spells.name: Anti-Channels V
spells.description: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels V.

spells.name: Repel Undead XII
spells.description: As Repel Undead V, except 12 points of Undead may be affected.

spells.name: Anti-Channels
spells.description: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels VIII.

spells.name: Lesser Banishment
spells.description: An entity from another plane (typically a demon) is sent back to whence it came. This spell will affect entities of up to Type (or Grade for elementals) II or up to 10th level if they do not have a “Type”.

spells.name: Dispel Channeling
spells.description: As Dispel Channeling I, except affects up to five targets.

spells.name: Anti-Channels X
spells.description: As Anti-Channels I, except affects up to a Channels X.

spells.name: Repel Undead XV
spells.description: As Repel Undead V, except 15 points of Undead may be affected.

spells.name: Greater Banishment
spells.description: As Lesser Banishment, except affects entities up to Type (or Grade) IV or 20th level if they do not have a “Type”.

spells.name: Neutralize Curse
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] As Neutralize Curse I, except curse is nullified for 1 day/level.

spells.name: Dispel Channeling
spells.description: As Dispel Channeling I, except affects up to ten targets.

spells.name: Lord Repel Undead
spells.description: As Repel Undead V, except this spell disintegrates any one Undead of Class V or lower automatically or automatically sends a Class VI fleeing for 1 round per caster level.

spells.name: Uncurse True
spells.description: [RR Mod: -50] As Uncurse, except for the RR modification.

spells.name: Anti-Channels
spells.description: As Anti-Channels I except any Channeled spell can be canceled. Spells channeled with the aid of a Holy Bridge (Cleric Base list, Channels) are not affected.

spells.name: Repel Undead
spells.description: As Repel Undead V, except a number of points of Undead equal to the caster’s level may be affected and if 20 points are applied to any one Undead, the effect is that of Lord Repel Undead on that target.

spells.name: True Banishment
spells.description: As Lesser Banishment, except affects any entity short of a god.

spells.name: Rechanneling
spells.description: Once per round, the caster can intercept one channeled spell that is being transmitted or received within this spell’s range and cast that spell himself. The person sending the channeled spell gets an RR (the defending level is that of the caster sending the channeled spell). Alternatively, the caster can intercept channeled PP for his own use. Interception takes no activity. Casting an intercepted spell takes the normal time. 6.3 - Cleric Base SUMMONS

spells.name: Summons I
spells.description: Caster can instantly summon a first level non-intelligent (i.e., possessing the Animalistic or Mindless flaw) living creature, which appears somewhere within the spell’s range. The caster can specify the general type of creature, but exactly what the creature is should be determined randomly (e.g., the caster could specify “four-legged, hoofed” and could get a zebra, horse, camel, etc.). If the creature can understand the caster’s communication, the creature can be controlled by normal communication. Otherwise, control is maintained through concentration of the caster. If the creature summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in its own perception), the duration for this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the creature is ever put into a dangerous situation, the duration immediately drops to 1 round per level. At the end of the spell’s duration, the summoned creature will disappear back whence it came.

spells.name: Summons II
spells.description: As Summons I, except the caster chooses one of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options to the higher level Summons spells, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level). 6.3 Cleric

spells.name: Summons III
spells.description: As Summons II, except two options are chosen. Base

spells.name: Summons IV
spells.description: As Summons II, except three options are chosen.

spells.name: Summons V
spells.description: As Summons II, except four options are chosen.

spells.name: Summons VI
spells.description: As Summons II, except five options are chosen.

spells.name: Summons VII
spells.description: As Summons II, except six options are chosen.

spells.name: Summons VIII
spells.description: As Summons II, except seven options are chosen.

spells.name: Summons X
spells.description: As Summons II, except nine options are chosen.

spells.name: Demonic Gate I
spells.description: Caster summons a Type I Demon that appears over the course of 2 rounds; the caster can control it by concentrating. After the caster stops concentrating, the demon will fade out over the course of 2 rounds, attacking the closest being until gone.

spells.name: Summons XV
spells.description: As Summons II, except fourteen options are chosen.

spells.name: Summon Guard
spells.description: Caster can summon (teleport to his location) a single “guard”, who must be a member of an intelligent race, a follower of the caster’s own religion, and currently doing service to the summoner or the summoner’s religious institution. Guard must also be within range of the spell when it is cast or it will fail.

spells.name: Lord Summons
spells.description: As Summons II, except nineteen options are chosen.

spells.name: Divine Gate I
spells.description: As Demonic Gate I, except summons a Type I extraplanar being of a non-hostile nature. Control is not possible, though the summoned being will be helpful and will usually do as the caster requests if communication is possible.

spells.name: Demonic Gate II
spells.description: As Demonic Gate I, except a Type II Demon is summoned.

spells.name: Summon Guards
spells.description: As Summon Guard, except one guard can be summoned for every five full levels of the caster (e.g., a 16th level caster could summon three guards).

spells.name: Waiting Summons
spells.description: As Summons X, except it can be delayed up to 1 day/level or until triggered by one of the following (decided by the caster at the time of casting): specified movements, sounds, touch, or actions. Trigger must be such as the caster would be able to sense it if still at the location of the spell effect. At the time of casting, the caster may specify a single task for the creature(s) to perform when it/they arrive(s).

spells.name: Summon Army
spells.description: Caster sends out a telepathic summons heard by all the faithful within range to gather for a holy war. Response is voluntary, except for the Paladins of the faith, who must make an RR if they wish to resist. Use of this spell tends to be strictly monitored by the Cleric’s superiors, both mortal and divine, so it is best used only when there is a real need.

spells.name: Divine Gate II
spells.description: As Divine Gate I, except a Type II entity is summoned.

spells.name: Demonic Gate
spells.description: As Demonic Gate I, except a Type III Demon can be summoned.

spells.name: Mass Summons
spells.description: As Summons II, except one option per caster level is chosen.

spells.name: Summons True
spells.description: As Summons I, except caster can summon a speROLEMASTER cific type of creature of a level up to half the caster’s level for more UNIFIED lengthy service. 

spells.name: Divine Gate III
spells.description: As Divine Gate I, except a Type III entity is sumSpell Law moned.

spells.name: Demonic Gate
spells.description: As Demonic Gate I, except a Type IV Demon can be summoned.

spells.name: Gate
spells.description: Caster can pull any being through a “gate” to the caster’s location; if the being is intelligent, it must be willing to come. An intel85 ligent being will know the religious affiliation of the gating person and a vague idea of the reason and the significance for the “gate”. 6.4 - Druid Base ANIMAL MASTERY

spells.name: Animal Sleep I
spells.description: Puts any animal into a magical sleep.

spells.name: Animal Restoration
spells.description: Caster may heal any non-fatal damage in any animal of Small size or smaller.

spells.name: Animal Tongues
spells.description: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any one animal species.

spells.name: Animal Sleep III
spells.description: As Animal Sleep I, except up to 3 animals may be made to sleep.

spells.name: Animal Mastery
spells.description: Allows caster to control the actions of any one animal.

spells.name: Animal Location
spells.description: Caster can locate members of any one species of animal within the area of effect or he can find out what ROLEMASTER species are in the area. UNIFIED 

spells.name: Befriending
spells.description: All animals within 10' will act friendly toward Spell Law caster. This does not control the animals.

spells.name: Animal Restoration
spells.description: Caster may heal any non-fatal damage in any animal of Medium size or smaller or heal fatal damage in an animal of Small size or smaller.

spells.name: Animal Empathy
spells.description: Caster can understand and/or visualize the thoughts and emotions of any one animal. 86

spells.name: Animal Summons
spells.description: Caster magically summons a 1st level animal from the surrounding area. The caster can specify the species of creature or simply a general type, but not a particular individual. If the animal can understand the caster (e.g., through use of Animal Tongues), the creature can be controlled through normal communications. Otherwise, control is maintained only through concentration by the caster. If the animal summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in the animal’s perception), the duration of this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the animal is ever put into a dangerous situation, the duration immediately drops to 1 round per level.

spells.name: Animal Mastery
spells.description: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control up to three animals.

spells.name: Animal Summons
spells.description: As Animal Summons I, except the caster chooses two of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level).

spells.name: Animal Call
spells.description: Caster can “call” any one known, individual animal; if it is physically possible, the animal will come to the caster.

spells.name: Animal Reviving
spells.description: This spell will return any animal of no more than Small size from the dead. The animal cannot have been dead for more days than its own level plus the caster’s level. If this spell is ever cast unsuccessfully on an animal (i.e., if an attempt to cast this spell ends in spell failure), then that animal can never be revived. The animal will be at -100 to all actions for a number of days equal to 20 days per day dead.

spells.name: Call of the Wild
spells.description: When cast upon any domesticated animal, that animal will return to its “wild” state. The animal will have no difficulty in adapting to the harsh requirements of the wilderness (and have no desire to return to domestication). Most animals will be willing targets of this spell, but those with a strong tie to a particular human may prefer domestication.

spells.name: Animal Mastery
spells.description: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control up to five animals.

spells.name: Animal Restoration
spells.description: Caster may heal any non-fatal damage in any animal of any size or heal fatal damage in an Animal of Medium size or smaller.

spells.name: Animal Summons
spells.description: As Animal Summons III, except four options can be chosen.

spells.name: Animal Reviving
spells.description: As Animal Reviving I, except animal may be of up to Medium size and recovery period is only one day per day spent dead.

spells.name: Animal Summons
spells.description: As Animal Summons III, except nine options can be chosen.

spells.name: Species Mastery
spells.description: As Animal Mastery I, except caster can control all animals of a given species within 10'R per level.

spells.name: Animal Reviving
spells.description: As Animal Reviving I, except animal may be of up to Big size and recovery period is only one day per day spent dead for a Big animal, with no recovery period for smaller animals.

spells.name: Animal Restoration
spells.description: Caster may heal any damage in any animal of any size.

spells.name: Animal Reviving
spells.description: As Animal Reviving I, except animal may be of any size and recovery period is only one day per day spent dead for a Large or larger animal, with no recovery period for smaller animals.

spells.name: Animal Summons
spells.description: As Animal Summons III, except caster can select one option for every level of experience. 6.4 - Druid Base HERB MASTERY

spells.name: Herb Lore
spells.description: Caster can understand the nature, origin, value, and uses of any magical herb. If a plant is not a magical herb, no information can be gained other than that fact.

spells.name: Herb Enhancement
spells.description: Allows caster to enhance the efficacy of any one herb (growing or dead) with healing or durational effects. For healing herbs, recovery rolls receive an extra +25 bonus. For herbs with a durational effect, the duration is doubled (i.e., x2). This spell may be employed only once per herb.

spells.name: Herb Finding I
spells.description: Allows caster to locate any one specific variety of herb that is sought, regardless of physical obstacles. This spell gives exact direction, distance, and quantity. If multiple patches of the herb are within range, the caster is aware of all of them.

spells.name: Instant Herbal
spells.description: Allows caster to prepare one dose of any herb so that it will be instantly effective. When the herb is subsequently used, its benefits/dangers will be immediately felt. The herb will keep for 1 day per level of the caster.

spells.name: Herb Mastery II
spells.description: Caster can double the potency of any one herb (growing or dead). This has the effect of halving the quantity of herb needed for a full dose, i.e. if a normal dose requires one full leaf to be effective, following this spell, only half a leaf would be required. This spell may be employed only once per herb. May not be combined with Herb Enhancement.

spells.name: Herb Finding III
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except that the range is 300'.

spells.name: Herb Production
spells.description: Caster can grow an herb to sudden maturity by planting the proper seed; the herb is sterile and the growth takes 1-10 rnds.

spells.name: Herb Enhancement
spells.description: As Herb Enhancement II, except healing herbs receive a +50 bonus and herbs with a durational effect have the duration trebled (x3). This spell may be employed only once per herb and may not be combined with Herb Enhancement II.

spells.name: Herb Finding V
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except range is 500'. 6.4 Druid

spells.name: Herb Cleansing
spells.description: Allows caster to remove any harmful poiBase sons, by-products, or side effects from all herbs within range. This includes eliminating any addictive properties.

spells.name: Herb Mastery III
spells.description: As Herb Mastery II, except potency is 3x.

spells.name: Mass Herb Finding
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except locates all herbs in area of effect.

spells.name: Far Herb Finding
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except range is 1 mile. Location is known within 10'R.

spells.name: Herb Preservation
spells.description: Preserves an herb indefinitely. If another spell is then cast on the herb, this spell will be cancelled and normal deterioration of the herb will begin.

spells.name: Herb Mastery IV
spells.description: As Herb Mastery II, except potency is 4x

spells.name: Mass Herb Finding
spells.description: As Herb Finding III, except locates all herbs in area of effect.

spells.name: Preserved Instant Herbal Cures
spells.description: As Instant Herbal Cures, except herb will keep for 1 week per level of the caster.

spells.name: Far Herb Finding
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except range is 5 miles. Location is known within 25'R.

spells.name: Mass Herb Finding
spells.description: As Herb Finding V, except locates all herbs in area of effect.

spells.name: Lord Herb Finding
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except range is 20 miles; location is known within 100'R.

spells.name: Mass Herb Enhancement
spells.description: As Herb Enhancement III, except that 1 herb per caster level can be affected. All herbs must be of the same type.

spells.name: Herb Mastery True
spells.description: As Herb Mastery II, except that the potency of 1 herb, growing or dead, can be increased by 100% x lvl/5 of caster (e.g., a 30th Ivl caster could increase potency of any herb by 600%, i.e., 6x, so one normal “dose” of a herb could be split into six effective portions).

spells.name: Instant Herbal Cures True
spells.description: As Instant Herbal Cures, except herb will keep until used.

spells.name: Mass Instant Herbal Cures
spells.description: As Preserved Instant Herbal Cures, except affects up to 1 herb/level.

spells.name: Herb Finding True
spells.description: As Herb Finding I, except that caster can locate any 1 herb variety within a range of 1 mile/lvl of caster (e.g., 50 miles for a 50th level caster); location is known within 100'R. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 87 6.4 - Druid Base NATURE’S LORE

spells.name: Trap Awareness
spells.description: Caster has a 75% chance of detecting each individual trap in a 5'R. Caster can concentrate on a 5'R area each round. There must be plants or animals in the area of effect or no information will be gained. Spiders and insects are animals, so many indoor environments will still allow the use of this spell. If a trap is missed, the spell will not find it no matter how many times the caster scans the same area.

spells.name: Nature’s Awareness
spells.description: Allows caster to monitor animate activity in the area (e.g., the caster will be aware of movements, subtle and overt). Caster cannot move while concentrating. Caster does not receive a picture of what is moving, just an awareness of the movement. This will allow distinguishing general size and shape of a moving object. Someone staying still, but breathing, will be detected, but only a very general idea of size would be determinable.

spells.name: Pathfinding II
spells.description: Caster learns the location(s) of any “path(s)” within 200'. The spell gives the nearest point on the path, but not the ROLEMASTER path’s course. (See Section 10.16 for information on interpretation UNIFIED  of “path”.) Spell Law

spells.name: Detect Magic
spells.description: Detects any active spell or active magical item. Caster can concentrate on a different 5'R area each round. (See Section 10.4 for more about detection spells.)

spells.name: Weather Prediction
spells.description: Caster has a 95% chance of correctly predicting time, type, and severity of weather over the next 24 hr period. 88

spells.name: Nature’s Awareness
spells.description: As Nature’s Awareness I, except that the radius is 200'.

spells.name: Weather Prediction
spells.description: As Weather Prediction I, except period is 3 days.

spells.name: Locate Landmark
spells.description: For the duration of this spell, the caster will know the direction and distance to any unique natural feature that the caster is familiar with. The feature must be within range or no information is gained.

spells.name: Waiting Awareness
spells.description: Allows caster to set up a natural “alarm system” in the area of effect; any disturbing activity will cause caster to be alerted. This alert will wake the caster, if he is in a natural sleep.

spells.name: Nature’s Awareness
spells.description: As Nature’s Awareness I, except that radius is 300'.

spells.name: Weather Prediction
spells.description: As Weather Prediction I, except period is 5 days.

spells.name: Memory’s Path
spells.description: Caster can remember an exact route that the caster has previously traveled regardless of whether or not the original journey was made with the caster’s normal sensory abilities active (e.g., a 12th level caster could remember the course of 12 hours of a trip even if blindfolded during that time). To be effective, this spell must be cast within one month of the journey.

spells.name: Waiting Awareness
spells.description: As Waiting Awareness I, except radius is 100'.

spells.name: Locate Landmark
spells.description: As Locate Landmark I, except for range.

spells.name: Nature’s Eye I
spells.description: Allows caster to visualize and sense activity in a known fixed area (100'R).

spells.name: Nature’s Awareness
spells.description: As Nature Awareness I, except radius is 500'.

spells.name: Elemental Communication
spells.description: Caster can communicate with any elemental force within range. The elemental force must be able to communicate (i.e., this spell does not confer communication abilities upon the force, just translation of those it does have). Elemental forces include all elementals, water spirits, forest spirits, etc.

spells.name: Water Visions
spells.description: The target of this spell is a small pool of water. The water must be calm (and remain calm for the duration of the spell). The caster may designate a topic and a vision will appear in the pool that relates to the topic. The vision will take 1-50 rounds.

spells.name: Locate Landmark
spells.description: As Locate Landmark I, except for range.

spells.name: Nature’s Eye II
spells.description: As Nature’s Eye I, except the range is 10 miles and caster can monitor an area of 500'R.

spells.name: Weather Prediction
spells.description: As Weather Prediction I, except period is 1 day/level.

spells.name: Nature’s Eye III
spells.description: As Nature’s Eye I, except range is 100 miles and caster can monitor an area of 500'R.

spells.name: Nature’s Awareness True
spells.description: As Nature’s Awareness I, except radius is 100'/level.

spells.name: Commune with
spells.description: Caster is made aware of the ecological health of the area and anything that is causing significant ecological impact on the area (e.g., the water from the magic spring makes everything here grow fast, poison is leaching out from the pile of mine tailings, or the unicorns prevent disease from spreading through the wildlife).

spells.name: Druidic Eye
spells.description: Allows caster to monitor an area of 100'R that the caster has both been to and become acquainted with (i.e., spent at least 1 day meditating in or 30 days living in). 6.4 - Druid Base NATURE’S PROTECTION

spells.name: Study Form
spells.description: Caster studies and memorizes the form of one type of animal or plant for use with spells later on this list. The caster can only have as many forms of each kingdom studied as he has ranks in this list. Thus, a Druid with 8 ranks in Nature’s Protection may have studied 8 types of animal and 8 types of plant. Caster may choose to forget a studied form at any time to make room for replacements.

spells.name: Resist Elements
spells.description: Protects caster from natural heat up to 176°F (80°C) and natural cold down to 14°F (-10°C), experiencing either as a 68°F (20°C) environment. For temperatures beyond the protected ranges, treat the caster as being in an environment of 20°C modified by one degree per degree beyond the protection limit (e.g., if the temperature were 180°F, the caster would experience it as 72°F). Against cold/ice or heat/fire attacks, the caster gains +20 to DB and RRs. Duration is 1 hour per level if the caster does not move, but if the caster moves, then duration becomes Concentration.

spells.name: Hues
spells.description: Allows caster to take on the physical coloration of any 1 organic object. The caster must be in contact with the object when the spell is cast. In many situations, this will give a +10 to +50 bonus (GM’s discretion) to Stalking attempts.

spells.name: Plant Façade
spells.description: Allows caster to appear as any one type of plant that he has studied. This is purely a visual illusion: caster will still sound, smell, and feel the same, and the caster undergoes no physical change.

spells.name: Silent Moves
spells.description: Caster can move silently, so long as he does not create a sound originating more than 1' from his body. This typically provides a +10 to +50 bonus (GM’s discretion) to Stalking maneuvers when moving.

spells.name: Underwater Breathing
spells.description: Caster can breathe normally under water. Water may be salt or fresh, but no other liquid is breathable by means of this spell.

spells.name: Animal Façade
spells.description: Allows caster to appear as any one type of animal that he has studied. This is purely a visual illusion: caster will still sound, smell, and feel the same, and the caster undergoes 6.4 Druid no physical change. Base

spells.name: Shadow
spells.description: Caster and objects on his person appear to be a shadow and are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus between +25 and +75).

spells.name: Organic Defense
spells.description: Any single attack made against the caster this round suffers a -50 modifier. The weapon or object defended against must be from an organic source (e.g., an animal) or at least a partially organic weapon (e.g., an arrow) and the caster must be able to see the attack.

spells.name: Plant Form I
spells.description: Allows caster to take the form of any one plant he has studied. The caster will look and feel like the plant. The caster’s size can change by up to one size increment. Caster does not gain the physical abilities of the plant other than Size category.

spells.name: Protection From Elements
spells.description: As Resist Elements, except it protects caster from any natural heat or natural cold (treat as if caster were in 68°F [20°C] environment), and DB/RR bonus is +40.

spells.name: Animal Thoughts
spells.description: For the duration of this spell, the caster’s mental patterns will appear to be those of any animal he has studied. The caster cannot move while this spell is active.

spells.name: Organic Defense
spells.description: As Organic Defense I, except the penalty is -100.

spells.name: Animal Form I
spells.description: Allows caster to take the form of any one animal he has studied. The caster will look and feel like the animal. The caster’s size can be varied up to one size increment. Caster does not gain the physical abilities of the animal, other than Size category.

spells.name: Animal Thoughts
spells.description: As Animal Thoughts I, except caster can move (though he must still maintain concentration).

spells.name: Plant Form II
spells.description: As Plant Form I, except caster may also assume the scent and physical abilities of the plant.

spells.name: Animal Form II
spells.description: As Animal Form I, except caster may also assume the movement abilities of the animal.

spells.name: Organic Defense
spells.description: As Organic Defense I, except attack automatically misses.

spells.name: Animal Form III
spells.description: As Animal Form II, except caster also gains the physical attacks of the animal. Caster can vary his size up to two Size increments. Caster gains normal OB of the animal with its natural attacks, gaining the instincts to use them properly.

spells.name: Plant Form True
spells.description: As Plant Form II, except caster can change his form to any other plant he has studied at any time during the duration of the spell and he can vary his size by up to 2 size increments.

spells.name: Animal Form True
spells.description: As Animal Form III, except caster can change his form to any other animal he has studied at any time during the duration of the spell and caster has all physical abilities of the animal.

spells.name: Nature’s Form True
spells.description: As Plant Form True and Animal Form True operating at the same time. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Animal Thoughts
spells.description: As Animal Thoughts II, except caster UNIFIED does not need to concentrate, and duration is 1 minute per level. 

spells.name: Master of Natural
spells.description: As Nature’s Form True, except for Spell Law duration.

spells.name: Organic Defense
spells.description: As Organic Defense III, except one attack may be defended against each round the spell is in effect. 89 6.4 - Druid Base NATURE’S WRATH

spells.name: Nature’s Ire I *
spells.description: The caster’s next attack gains a +5 to its OB. If the caster is attacking in animal form, this bonus is doubled.

spells.name: Nature’s Curse
spells.description: Target will find that animals, both wild and domestic have become wary of him. All maneuvers dealing with handling, influencing, or working with animals suffer a penalty of -25.

spells.name: Entangling Vegetation I
spells.description: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Small Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (grasses, flowers, or larger plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +0.

spells.name: Shock Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack.

spells.name: Nature’s Ire II *
spells.description: As Nature’s Ire I, except bonus is +10. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Wall of Fire
spells.description: Creates an opaque wall of fire (up to 10'x10'x6"). AnyUNIFIED one passing through it takes an A Heat critical (no RR). See Section  10.20 for additional details on Wall spells. Spell Law

spells.name: Nature’s Ire III *
spells.description: As Nature’s Ire I, except bonus is +15.

spells.name: Entangling Vegetation II
spells.description: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Medium Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (vines, tall stalks such as corn/maize, small bushes such as roses, or larger plants). If a Grapple % 90 result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +5.

spells.name: Nature’s Might
spells.description: The caster’s next attack (including spells) does double concussion hit damage.

spells.name: Shock Bolt III
spells.description: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.

spells.name: Contrary Wind
spells.description: Winds gust about the target, always blowing from the direction in which he tries to move and anyone else in the radius will share this effect (no RR). This will prevent a sailed vessel from moving, reduce normal movement by two Paces (e.g., target may no longer Walk, but must at least use a Jog pace, which will produce a Creep). In addition, the target (but not others in the radius) will have a special -20 penalty to Endurance rolls due to moving. Any loose items will be blown away. Any missile fire into or out of the area affected will suffer a -80 modification to OB.

spells.name: Circle Aflame
spells.description: As Wall of Fire, except “wall” is 10' high and forms a 10'R circle (6" thick) with the caster at the center (it is non-mobile).

spells.name: Nature’s Ire V *
spells.description: As Nature’s Ire I, except bonus is +25.

spells.name: Entangling Vegetation III
spells.description: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Big Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (large bushes, mature ivy, or larger plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +10.

spells.name: Shock Bolt V
spells.description: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.

spells.name: Nature’s Ire VI
spells.description: As Nature’s Ire I, except bonus is +30.

spells.name: Hailstorm
spells.description: The area of effect is pelted with heavy hail. Treat as an Ice Bolt attack on everyone in the area of effect with an OB equal to half the caster’s skill in this list and with no range modification. Fumble results on the Ice Bolt attack are ignored.

spells.name: Entangling Vegetation IV
spells.description: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Large Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (generally trees or plentiful vines). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +15.

spells.name: Nature’s Ire VII
spells.description: As Nature’s Ire I, except bonus is +35.

spells.name: Lightning Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; the results are determined on the Lighting Bolt Table as a Medium sized attack.

spells.name: Entangling Vegetation V
spells.description: Plants around the target become highly animated and attempt to take hold of the target. Treat as a size Huge Grapple attack with an OB equal to the caster’s skill in this list. This spell requires the presence of suitable plants (thick growth including large plants). If a Grapple % result occurs, the plants will continue to hold the target until it is freed. For attempts to break free, treat the plants’ Grappling skill as equal to the original OB and their Strength bonus as +25.

spells.name: Lightning Bolt
spells.description: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.

spells.name: Nature’s Ire True
spells.description: As Nature’s Ire I, except bonus is +50 and attack does double concussion hit damage.

spells.name: Hailstorm True
spells.description: As Hailstorm, except for range, radius, and the OB is equal to the caster’s skill in this list.

spells.name: Quake
spells.description: Caster can cause an earthquake; with the caster’s location at the time of casting as the epicenter. The quake can be delayed up to 1 round/level. The severity of the quake on the Richter scale is determined by a roll: (01-20) = 5.5, (21-45) = 6, (46-65) = 6.5, (66-80) = 7.0, (81-90) = 7.5, (91-95) = 8.0, (96-98) = 8.5, (99-100) = 9.0. 6.4 - Druid Base PLANT MASTERY

spells.name: Plant Lore
spells.description: Caster learns nature, value, and history of one plant (that is not a magical herb).

spells.name: Speed Growth I
spells.description: Allows caster to increase speed of growth for any 1 species of plant within the area of effect to ten times the normal rate.

spells.name: Plant Tongues
spells.description: Allows caster to understand and “speak” the language of any 1 plant species.

spells.name: Plant Restore
spells.description: Instantly heals damage to one plant that is not a tree.

spells.name: Rapid Growth I
spells.description: Allows caster to increase speed of growth for any 1 species of plant within the area of effect to a hundred times the normal rate.

spells.name: Plant Location
spells.description: Caster can locate members of any 1 species of plant or find out what species are in the area.

spells.name: Plant Growth II
spells.description: Allows caster to double the size of any 1 plant. This requires 1 day’s growth. When fully mature the plant will achieve twice its normal size.

spells.name: Plant Control I
spells.description: Caster can control the automatic and/or mental processes of any one plant. Caster can also control the plant’s movements, although this does not allow movement beyond normal allowances. If cast daily, this can be used to direct a plant’s growth into specific unusual shapes, so long as these would be naturally possible.

spells.name: Speed Growth II
spells.description: As Speed Growth I, except the range is 100' and the radius is 100'.

spells.name: Plant Growth III
spells.description: As Plant Growth II, except size increase is 3 times.

spells.name: Plant Control III
spells.description: As Plant Control I, except caster can control any three plants.

spells.name: Tree Restore
spells.description: As Plant Restore, except it can affect any plant (including trees).

spells.name: Plant Growth V
spells.description: As Plant Growth II, except size increase is 5 times.

spells.name: Rapid Growth II
spells.description: As Rapid Growth I, except the range is 100' and the radius is 100'R. 6.4 Druid

spells.name: Plant Control V
spells.description: As Plant Control I, except caster can conBase trol any 5 plants.

spells.name: Wall of Thorns
spells.description: Summons a wall of very tough (but pliable) thorny growth. The wall is up to 10'x10'x5'. Anyone moving through the wall receives d10 dagger attacks (each with a +50 OB) per foot of wall moved through. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.

spells.name: Firestop
spells.description: This spell will put out any fires upon living materials that are in the area of effect. Fumble results on the Ice Bolt attack are ignored.

spells.name: Plant Growth X
spells.description: As Plant Growth II, except size increase is 10 times.

spells.name: Awaken Tree
spells.description: This spell requires a tree at least 80 years old as its target. If this spell is cast on a tree every day for a year (not necessarily by the same person each time), then the tree will awaken, becoming a Slowroot (See Creature Law). The newly awakened tree will not automatically be under any control of the caster(s) who wakened it.

spells.name: Plant Growth
spells.description: As Plant Growth II, except caster can increase size of all plants of 1 species within a 100'R by 5x.

spells.name: Rapid Growth
spells.description: As Rapid Growth I, except for range and area of effect.

spells.name: Plant Control
spells.description: As Plant Control I, except caster can control all plants within range (100'R).

spells.name: Wall of Thorns
spells.description: As Wall of Thorns, but dimensions are up to 20'x10'x5' and the duration is permanent (the wall is rooted and living).

spells.name: Plant Animation
spells.description: Caster can animate any 1 plant; the plant can then move slowly (10'/rnd for plants under 10' tall, 20'/rnd if taller), carrying any root system it may possess along.

spells.name: Mass Plant Animation
spells.description: As Plant Animation, except caster can animate up to 1 plant/level. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 91 6.5 - Paladin Base CURSEBREAKING

spells.name: Holy Cancellation
spells.description: When the caster is a target of a spell from an Evil spell list, the “incoming” spell must first make an RR before the caster makes an RR. If the “incoming” spell passes the RR, it may target the caster of this spell normally; otherwise the incoming spell has no effect on this target. Being a “willing target” does not eliminate this effect, but the caster’s own spells do not need to make an RR. Note that the caster is considered to be a target of any spell which includes him in its area of effect or would “detect” him.

spells.name: Holy Cancellation Channeling
spells.description: As Holy Cancellation Evil, except affects spells from the realm of Channeling. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Holy Cancellation Essence
spells.description: As Holy Cancellation Evil, UNIFIED except affects spells from the realm of Essence.  Spell Law

spells.name: Holy Cancellation Mentalism
spells.description: As Holy Cancellation Evil, except affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.

spells.name: Dispel Evil I
spells.description: Any active spell from an Evil spell list that is on the target must make an RR. If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled. 92

spells.name: Neutralize Curse
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -20] Nullifies a curse for the duration of this spell. The curse is not dispelled and takes effect after the duration of this spell.

spells.name: Dispel Channeling
spells.description: As Dispel Evil I, except affects spells from the realm of Channeling.

spells.name: Dispel Essence I
spells.description: As Dispel Evil I, except affects spells from the realm of Essence.

spells.name: Dispel Mentalism
spells.description: As Dispel Evil I, except affects spells from the realm of Mentalism.

spells.name: Curse Tales
spells.description: Caster can determine nature and origin of any one curse, including the name of the one who bequeathed it.

spells.name: Dispel Evil Sphere
spells.description: Any active Evil spell that is (at least partly) within the radius of effect must make an RR (use the spell’s level for the RR). If the spell fails its RR, it is dispelled.

spells.name: Find Curser I
