spells.name: Jog *
spells.description: Caster may move at two times normal walking pace (i.e., Jog pace) as easily as walking. Fatigue and difficulty of maneuvers are as if at Walk pace; spells requiring a “walking pace” may be used (e.g. Shadow Steps), but not those requiring slower than walking pace (e.g., Spider’s Step). Once the caster stops moving, the spell is canceled.

spells.name: Longjump *
spells.description: Caster may jump (from any pace) up to 10' plus 5' per level of the caster horizontally or 5' plus 2' per level of the caster vertically. This spell will allow the caster to always land safely from jumps of this distance or less. The jump itself is a 1 AP action or it may be incorporated into movement at no cost if the caster is moving at a Jog (x2) or greater pace.

spells.name: Soft Landing *
spells.description: This spell may be cast when the caster begins to fall, taking effect immediately, allowing the caster to land safely in a fall up to 20' per level of the caster and to take that distance off the severity of any longer fall. This landing will also be noiseless.

spells.name: Shadow Steps
spells.description: Allows the caster to cloak all of his movements in complete silence, so long as he moves no faster than a walking pace. This provides a +25 bonus to Stalking ROLEMASTER maneuvers and negates any penalties for especially noisy UNIFIED surfaces.  Spell Law

spells.name: Underwater
spells.description: Caster can take action underwater as if on land.

spells.name: Balancing *
spells.description: Adds +50 to any rolls for any moving maneuvers performed at a walking pace or less (e.g., walking a 3" beam). 144

spells.name: Spider’s Step
spells.description: Caster can move (at Creep pace) along any solid surface angled up to 90° so long as at least three point contact (e.g., two feet and a hand) is maintained with the surface.

spells.name: Long Dive *
spells.description: Caster can safely dive through the air up to 50' per level so long as there is deep enough water present at the end of the dive (at least 1' of water per 25' of dive).

spells.name: Traceless Passing
spells.description: Caster can move at a walking pace without leaving tracks or other visible signs of his passing.

spells.name: Spider’s Step True
spells.description: As Spider’s Step, except caster can move along any surface (including ceilings).

spells.name: Float *
spells.description: Caster can float upon the air. While floating, the caster is at the mercy of the prevailing winds and cannot control any aspect of his flight without access to some other motive force (i.e., pulling along a rope or wall, being towed, etc.).

spells.name: Spider’s Walk
spells.description: As Spider’s Step, except caster may move at a normal walking pace and he needs only to maintain a 2-point contact with the wall.

spells.name: Run *
spells.description: As Jog, except pace is three times walking pace.

spells.name: Shadow Landing
spells.description: As Soft Landing, except caster can land safely from any fall 99% of the time, and still takes the normal 20'/level off any fall for the 1% of the time that safety is not complete. While falling, the caster gets his full Stalking bonus and adds +25 to that if not in direct sunlight.

spells.name: Glide I
spells.description: Caster can “glide” through the air. The product of caster’s altitude and horizontal speed cannot exceed 100 (with altitude in feet and speed in feet per round). For example, a caster can glide along at a rate of 5' per round if he were 20' off the ground, or he could glide at the rate of 50' per round if he were 2' off the ground. Note that the product must equal 100 (i.e., the caster cannot choose to have a result less than or more than 100). Elevation may be increased up to 5' or decreased up to 10' each round, with the highest elevation being used to calculate the horizontal speed. There is a minimum elevation of 2'.

spells.name: Airwalking
spells.description: Caster can walk on calm air. This spell only allows for horizontal movement. Vertical movement must be achieved by other means.

spells.name: Secret Speed *
spells.description: Caster can move at any pace or using any means (e.g., swimming, which would normally be less stealthy due to splashing water) without penalty to any Stalking rolls.

spells.name: Sprint *
spells.description: As Jog, except pace is four times walking pace.

spells.name: Glide II
spells.description: As Glide I, except product is 200.

spells.name: Spider’s Walk True
spells.description: As Spider’s Walk, except caster can move along any surface (including ceilings).

spells.name: Glide III
spells.description: As Glide I, except product is 300.

spells.name: Superior Balance
spells.description: As Balance, except bonus is +100.

spells.name: Dash *
spells.description: As Jog, except pace is five times walking pace.

spells.name: Glide True
spells.description: As Glide I, except product is 500.

spells.name: Movement Mastery
spells.description: Caster may use any lower level spell from this list once per round. 7.4 7.4 - - Dabbler Dabbler Base Base SENSES

spells.name: Sly Ears
spells.description: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of hearing. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only hearing, +25 to Perception involving hearing and other senses.

spells.name: Nightvision
spells.description: Caster can see up to 100' as if it were daylight, so long as there is some light.

spells.name: Scent
spells.description: Caster gains an extremely acute sense of smell. This results in +50 to Perception rolls involving only smell, +25 to Perception involving smell and other senses.

spells.name: Sensory Marker
spells.description: One location is “marked” so that the caster can find it again later with a Far Sense spell. The marker is automatically located when the caster casts a Far Sense spell if the marker is within range of the Far Sense spell. A marker can be placed on any inanimate object. The caster can only have as many markers as he has ranks in this list; a Sensory Marker spell can be cancelled even if the caster is no longer within range of the marker.

spells.name: Far Sense I
spells.description: Caster may designate any one of his senses to be used at any “marked” location within range (see Sensory Marker). The caster can use any perception abilities he has at the time (e.g., if he is currently under the effects of Nightvision, he will have Nightvision at the marked location). The remote sensory point may be rotated to face any direction. While he is sensing at the marked location, the caster must remain immobile and concentrate (and does not have that sense available at his own location).

spells.name: Sidevision
spells.description: Caster has a 300° field of vision. The flank bonus for attacks against the caster is lowered to +5 and the rear bonus is lowered to +15.

spells.name: Touch
spells.description: Caster gains extreme tactile sensitivity. This provides a bonus to any action requiring a sense of touch. For example, caster might receive +25 to picking locks, 7.4 Dabbler disarming traps, opening secret doors, etc. and +50 to PerBase Lists ception involving only touch, +25 to Perception involving touch and other senses).

spells.name: Watervision
spells.description: Caster can see 100' in any water (including murky water) as if through air in daylight.

spells.name: Darkvision
spells.description: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.

spells.name: Far Sense II
spells.description: As Far Sense I, except caster may designate up to two senses for use at the marked location.

spells.name: Woodsight
spells.description: Caster can see through wood (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Lightvision
spells.description: Caster can see in any brightness of light (including that which would be blinding or glaring). This spell does not work in the absence of light.

spells.name: Fabricsight
spells.description: Caster can see through any woven organic substance (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Greater Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense I, except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).

spells.name: Long Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense I, except for range.

spells.name: Stonesight
spells.description: Caster can see through stone (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Locate Marker
spells.description: Caster knows the location (distance and direction) of one chosen Sensory Marker relative to the caster’s current location.

spells.name: Greater Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense II, except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).

spells.name: Metalsight
spells.description: Caster can see through metal (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Long Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense II, except for range.

spells.name: Vision Suite
spells.description: As all Vision spells on this list operating at the same time.

spells.name: Senses True
spells.description: Caster has the effects of Sly Ears, Scent, Touch, and all Vision spells simultaneously.

spells.name: Danger Sense
spells.description: Caster has a warning of any impending danger. This will give the caster a second chance to notice any trap he has missed before actually triggering it, prevent any attacker from gaining the bonus for Surprise or succeeding in using Ambush skill against the caster, and should provide similar protection against other sudden danROLEMASTER ger (GM’s discretion). UNIFIED 

spells.name: Far Sense True
spells.description: As Greater Far Sense I, except caster Spell Law can use all senses and the range is improved.

spells.name: Sense Mastery

spells.name: Reflection
spells.description: Makes one surface of an inanimate object up to 10'x10' perfectly reflective in the orientation desired by the caster, e.g. to permit viewing around a corner.

spells.name: Smoke Cloud
spells.description: Creates a cloud of obscuring smoke (caster’s choice of color) which obscures as heavy fog (-50 Perception, visibility reduced to 50').

spells.name: Sound/Light Mirage
spells.description: Creates any simple, immobile visual image/scene in an area of up to 10'R, or creates a set of immobile sounds in an area of up to 10'R.

spells.name: Subtle Eye I
spells.description: Caster gains a +25 Perception bonus to notice that which is hidden by any means (including noticing illusions).

spells.name: Illusion III
spells.description: Creates a simple, immobile image or scene up to an area of 10'R. Two of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, or e) the illusion can cast light. All parts of the area of effect must be in the caster’s field of vision at the time of casting.

spells.name: Darkness
spells.description: Creates an area of darkness up to 2'R/lvl about the point touched. The darkness is equal to the darkest night. (See RMCL Section 15.7) If the point is on a mobile object or being, it will move with the object/being (but any target gets an RR to avoid having it “stick” to them). One of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not ROLEMASTER the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, UNIFIED c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect  can be doubled, e) another image can be created and moved Spell Law independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within range of the spell), f) the phantasm can cast light, or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper senses involved must also be included within the illusion). 146

spells.name: Phantasms II
spells.description: Creates the image of one object or being that will move however the caster wants (as long as the caster concentrates). When the caster stops concentrating, the image remains but stops moving. The caster may resume concentration again later, and make the image move again (presuming that the duration has not expired). The image can be of any size that would fit in a 10'R. One of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (not the tactile or Presence senses), b) the duration can be doubled, c) the range can be doubled, d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled, e) another image can be created and moved independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within range of the spell), f) the phantasm can cast light, or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper senses involved must also be included within the illusion).

spells.name: Extinguish Minor
spells.description: Instantly extinguishes a fire up to the size of a campfire.

spells.name: Waiting Distraction
spells.description: The next spell cast from this list (must be within 1 minute) will have its effect delayed up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster must decide upon the trigger for the spell. The caster can choose one of the following: a) time period, b) specified movements, or c) specified sounds.

spells.name: Ignite Fuel
spells.description: Instantly ignites any one thing intended to burn, e.g. it will light a lantern, torch, firewood, but not other items such as furniture or clothing.

spells.name: Subtle Eye II
spells.description: As Subtle Eye I, except bonus is +50.

spells.name: Illusion V
spells.description: As Illusion II, except four options can be chosen.

spells.name: Phantasms III
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except two options may be chosen.

spells.name: Extinguish Major

spells.name: Mass Extinguish
spells.description: As Extinguish Minor, except all minor fires in the area of effect.

spells.name: Illusion VIII
spells.description: As Illusion II, except seven options can be chosen.

spells.description: As Smoke Cloud, but area of effect is 10'/lvl R.

spells.name: Phantasms IV
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except three options can be chosen.

spells.name: Mass Extinguish
spells.description: Immediately extinguishes all fire within 5’/lvl radius.

spells.name: Mass Ignite
spells.description: As Ignite Fuel, except it will ignite all suitable things of the specified type (or all suitable types) within the radius.

spells.name: Phantasms V
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except four options can be chosen.

spells.name: Illusion X
spells.description: As Illusion II, except nine options can be chosen.

spells.description: As Subtle Eye I, except bonus is +100.

spells.name: Illusion XV

spells.description: As Phantasms II, except nine options can be chosen. 7.4 7.4 - - Dabbler Dabbler Base Base TRADE MASTERY

spells.name: Tradesman’s Lore
spells.description: Caster receives basic idea of item’s purpose, and receives +20 to its use if it is intended to be operated using any Craft or Technical skill.

spells.name: Tune
spells.description: When this spell is cast upon a machine, the machine’s efficiency will return to its original level. For example, if cast upon a rusty lock, the lock would return to its original non-rusty operational level.

spells.name: Phantom Tools I
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to perform one maneuver using a Craft or Technical skill as if the caster had a complete set of the appropriate tools with a bonus of +10.

spells.name: Jam I
spells.description: Target mechanism (trap, lock, door, machine, etc) is jammed, and has only a 7% chance of operating when used/triggered normally. It remains jammed until repaired.

spells.name: Jewel/Metal Assessment
spells.description: Caster can assess the value of jewels and metals within 10% of actual value. This spell allows the caster to calculate different values for all of the cultures with which the caster is familiar. This spell evaluates materials only, not craftsmanship or magical properties.

spells.name: Operate I
spells.description: This spell will operate any non-magical machine in its normal capacity for the duration. After the duration, the machine will operate normally. For example, if this spell were used to turn a wheel, the wheel would spin for the duration of the spell, then slowly spin to a stop. This merely provides basic motive power; any additional controls (directional controls, frequency settings, etc.) must be handled separately.

spells.name: Item Assessment
spells.description: As Jewel/Metal Assessment, except this spell will determine the value of an item, taking into account the craftsmanship of the item.

spells.name: Phantom Tools
spells.description: As Phantom Tools I, except the bonus is +30.

spells.name: Sense Power
spells.description: Caster will be aware of any machine activity or stored power for a machine (e.g. capacitors, battery, springs, weights) within the radius of effect. The caster will not know what the machine is or exactly where, though successful Perception 7.4 Dabbler may give an idea of the number of machines, vague location, and Base Lists overall energy involved. A Very Hard Perception maneuver while moving at a walking pace or slower with this spell active will give the caster enough warning that he is starting to trigger a mechanism for the caster to pull back, avoiding a tripwire or pressure plate activation.

spells.name: Acumen I
spells.description: Caster receives a +25 bonus to Trading skill and to any Social or Lore skill used to locate a buyer or seller.

spells.name: Lesser Item
spells.description: Caster gains a general idea of an item’s abilities (e.g., it provides a bonus to Stealth [but not the amount], it allows casting a spell twice daily [but not which spell], or it does an additional critical in combat [but not what kind or information on the severity of the critical]).

spells.name: Operate II
spells.description: As Operate I, except concentration is not required and the duration is 1 minute per level.

spells.name: Jam II
spells.description: As Jam I, but the mechanism has only a 4% chance of operating when used/triggered.

spells.name: Detect Significance
spells.description: Caster can detect any item in the radius which has cultural or historical significance (e.g. a sacred relic, a item formerly possessed by a great hero), and its magnitude (mild significance, great fame, etc), but not the details. Caster can move the area of effect 5' each round by concentrating.

spells.name: Operate III
spells.description: As Operate II, except duration is 1 hr/level.

spells.name: Phantom Tools V
spells.description: As Phantom Tools I, except the bonus is +50.

spells.name: Acumen II
spells.description: As Acumen I, except the bonus is +50.

spells.name: Origins
spells.description: The caster learns the area of origin of the item, the race of the being who made it, and when it was made (within 100 years).

spells.name: Operate IV
spells.description: As Operate II, except the duration is 1 day per level.

spells.name: Area Jamming
spells.description: As Jam II, except affects all mechanisms in the area of effect.

spells.name: Item Vision
spells.description: Caster gets a vision of a significant event in the item’s past.

spells.name: Mental Operation
spells.description: Allows caster to operate and control one machine. This not only provides basic motive power (as an Operate spell), but allows full control of an entire machine by the caster alone. Treat as if as many individuals as needed for normal operations (up to a maximum equal to the caster’s level) were working under the perfect command of the caster. Treat the skill of this crew as either +0 or the caster’s own bonus in the appropriate skill.

spells.name: Acumen True
spells.description: As Acumen I, except the bonus is +100.

spells.name: Mass Operate
spells.description: As Operate II, except affects all machines in the area of effect. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED

spells.name: Shadow

spells.name: Façade I
spells.description: Target has a limited illusion on himself that allows him to look like any humanoid race within 20% of his own size. The illusion moves as the target moves.

spells.name: Signs
spells.description: [RR Mod: -50] Caster can communicate simple ideas to the target through sign language (yes, no, hungry, good-bye, etc.); to the target it will seem as if the caster were using the gestures appropriate to the target’s culture.

spells.name: Displacement I
spells.description: Target’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.

spells.name: Façade II
spells.description: As Façade I, except one of the other senses can be added.

spells.name: Impersonation Façade
spells.description: As Façade I, except that a specific person can be impersonated with regards to looks. The person must have been observed for at least 1 minute of concentration. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Façade III
spells.description: As Façade I, except two of the other senses can UNIFIED  be added. Spell Law

spells.name: Impersonation Façade
spells.description: As Impersonation Façade I, except that one other aspect (or trait) of the person is impersonated. Additionally, the caster must study the target for at least two minutes of concentration. For example, the target’s voice can be impersonated. 148

spells.name: Displacement II
spells.description: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 20%.

spells.name: False Image
spells.description: Creates a duplicate of the caster, which the caster may concentrate to control and otherwise does exactly what the caster does. The image includes visual and audio components, but no other senses.

spells.name: Impersonation Façade

spells.name: Mass Blur *

spells.name: Façade V

spells.name: Impersonation Study
spells.description: The caster may cast this spell and instantly know everything necessary for Impersonation Façade spells (i.e., this spell replaces time spent studying the subject to be impersonated).

spells.name: Mass Shadow

spells.name: Displacement VI
spells.description: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 60%.

All parts of the area of effect must be in the caster’s field of vision at the time of casting.

spells.name: Phantasms I

When the caster stops concentrating, the image remains but stops moving. The caster may resume concentration again later and make the image move again (presuming that the duration has not expired). The image can be of any size that would fit within the area of effect.

spells.name: Illusion III
spells.description: As Illusion II, except any two of the options may be chosen. See notes above for guidelines on how to combine the options.

spells.name: Waiting Illusion
spells.description: As Illusion II, except it can be delayed by up to 24 hours. At the time of casting, the caster chooses the trigger for the spell. The caster can choose one of the following: a) time period, b) specified movements, c) specified sounds, d) specified smells, e) specified tastes, f) specified touch, or g) a Presence spell cast in the area of effect. If a sense is used to trigger the spell, that sense must be included in the illusion.

spells.name: Phantasms II
spells.description: As Phantasms I, except one of the following options may also be chosen: a) an extra sense can be added (the corresponding Mirage spell must be known); b) the duration can be doubled; c) the range can be doubled; d) the radius of the area of effect can be doubled; e) another image can be created and moved independently of the first (all separate images must be within the caster’s field of vision and within area of effect of the spell); f) the phantasm can cast 7.5 light; or g) limited repetitious sounds, movements, etc., can be added that do not require concentration of the caster (the proper Illusionist senses involved must also be included within the illusion). Base Lists See notes for guidelines on how to combine the options.

spells.name: Illusion V
spells.description: As Illusion II, except any four of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Waiting Phantasm
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except it can be delayed as in Waiting Illusion II. The Phantasm will move and act if given a simple command (e.g., attack, run, etc.) or it can deliver a short speech if sound was one of the senses.

spells.name: Waiting Illusion
spells.description: As Waiting Illusion II, except any two of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Phantasms III
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except any two of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Illusion VII
spells.description: As Illusion II, except any six of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Waiting Phantasm
spells.description: As Waiting Phantasm II, except any two of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Phantasms IV
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except any three of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Waiting Illusion
spells.description: As Waiting Illusion II, except any four of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Phantasms V
spells.description: As Phantasm II, except any four of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Illusion X
spells.description: As Illusion II, except any nine of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Waiting Phantasm
spells.description: As Waiting Phantasm II, except any four of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Phantasms VII
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except any six of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Waiting Illusion
spells.description: As Waiting Illusion II, except any six of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Illusory Terrain
spells.description: All of the terrain in the area of effect will appear differently than it actually is (the corresponding Terrain spell for each sense incorporated must be known). The caster may designate in what way the terrain is different. For example, a clearing in the woods could be disguised as a heavily wooded area of the woods.

spells.name: Phantasms X
spells.description: As Phantasms II, except any nine of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Waiting Illusion
spells.description: As Waiting Illusion II, except any nine of the options may be chosen.

spells.name: Illusion True
spells.description: As Illusion X, except its duration is permanent (until dispelled).

spells.name: Illusion XX
spells.description: As Illusion II, except any nineteen of the opROLEMASTER tions may be chosen. UNIFIED 

spells.name: Phantasms XX

spells.description: As Phantasms X, except its duration is permanent (until dispelled); Phantasm can move only if caster concentrates, except for simple programmed repetitions, if that option is chosen. 149 7.5 7.5 - - Illusionist Illusionist Base Base LIGHT MOLDING

spells.name: Light Mirage
spells.description: Creates any simple immobile image or scene up to an area of 10'R. The visual aspects of the scene are really created (no RR) and detecting that it is an illusion can normally be accomplished only through spells or the use of a sense other than sight.

spells.name: Projected Light
spells.description: Beam of light (like a flashlight) springs from the caster’s palm; it has a 50' effective range. If the caster’s hand is closed into a fist, the light will “turn off”. If the caster’s fist is reopened before the end of the duration, the light will turn on again.

spells.name: Light Control I
spells.description: Caster can control the intensity of light within the area of effect; the intensity can vary from daylight to darkness (natural), and it can be different in different parts of the radius. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED

spells.name: Sudden Light
spells.description: Causes a 10'R burst of intense light, all  within are stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure. Spell Law

spells.name: Shock Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of intense, charged light is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Lightning Bolt attack table, as a Miniscule attack. 150

spells.name: Light Glamour
spells.description: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its visual appearance altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes. This spell cannot make an object cast light (although it may alter the appearance of light given off, whether naturally or from another spell).

spells.name: Light V
spells.description: Lights a 50'R area about the point touched.

spells.name: Darkness V
spells.description: Reduces illumination to a dark night in a 50'R area about the point touched.

spells.name: Blind
spells.description: Creates an area of darkness about the target’s head that blinds him unless dispelled or canceled by Utterlight.

spells.name: Light Control V
spells.description: As Light Control I, except area affected is 50'R.

spells.name: Utterlight
spells.description: Nullifies all magically created darkness in a 100'R and lights that area as full daylight.

spells.name: Light Terrain
spells.description: All of the terrain in the area of effect will look differently than it actually is. The caster may designate in what way the terrain appears different. For example, a group of small trees could be made into a group of tall trees. Note that only the visual aspect of the terrain changes.

spells.name: Beacon I
spells.description: A ray of light (any color) springs from caster’s palm; it can be up to 1 mile long.

spells.name: Utterdark
spells.description: Darkens a 100'R area, no light (except Utterlight) can exist within this radius.

spells.name: Light Control X
spells.description: As Light Control I, except area affected is 100'R.

spells.name: Shock Bolt V
spells.description: As Shock Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.

spells.name: Mass Light
spells.description: As Light Glamour, except that it affects up to one item per caster level, with the duration and range listed.

spells.name: Beacon V
spells.description: As Beacon I, except the ray of light can be up to 5 miles long.

spells.name: Light Control XX
spells.description: As Light Control I, except area affected is 200'R.

spells.name: Lightning Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of lightning is shot from the caster’s palm; resolve attack on the Lightning Bolt Attack Table as a Medium-sized attack.

spells.name: Greater Light
spells.description: As Light Control I, except area affected is 500'R.

spells.name: Lightning Bolt
spells.description: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.

spells.name: Lightning Bolt V
spells.description: As Lightning Bolt I, except range increment is 125'.

spells.name: Shocking Bolts
spells.description: As Shock Bolt V, except the caster can make one Shock Bolt attack each round the spell remains in effect.

spells.name: Light Control
spells.description: As Light Control I, except area affected is 100'R/level. 7.5 7.5 - - Illusionist Illusionist Base Base MIND SENSE MOLDING

spells.name: Detect Illusion
spells.description: Caster can check one object or place (up to a 5'R) and tell if it is an illusion or has an illusion on it.

spells.name: Detect Invisible
spells.description: Detects any invisible objects or beings; one 5'R area can be checked each round. All attacks against detected targets are modified by -50.

spells.name: Misfeel Kind
spells.description: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, target appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.

spells.name: Misfeel Power I
spells.description: As Misfeel Kind, except target’s level may be misrepresented by 1 level (up or down).

spells.name: Presence Mirage
spells.description: Creates a false “presence” of a being for the purposes of magical detection.

spells.name: Misfeel Calling
spells.description: As Misfeel Kind, except target’s profession may be misrepresented.

spells.name: Misfeel Power III
spells.description: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level may be misrepresented by up to 3 levels.

spells.name: Unpresence Self
spells.description: Caster becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.

spells.name: Reduce Power Emanations
spells.description: Conceals an item’s magical power. Any attempt to detect its magical power (an appropriate Detect spell while it is active, an Attunement attempt, or other means) is hindered. If the item has a single power, then if the detection attempt fails an RR against this spell, it does not register the item’s magic. In the case of multiple powers, a magical detection attempt gains a +5 to its RR for each power beyond the first, while someone attempting Attunement (and failing the RR) will be unable to discover one ability (chosen at the time this spell is cast), but may discover and use the remaining powers normally.

spells.name: Misfeel Power V
spells.description: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s 7.5 level may be misrepresented by up to 5 levels. Illusionist

spells.name: Disillusion I
spells.description: While this spell is in effect, one illusion Base Lists within the caster’s range ceases to exist for the caster only. The caster must specify the illusion to be negated (unless caster knows something is an illusion, this spell would be gambling PP).

spells.name: Detect Illusion
spells.description: As Detect Illusion, except caster can concentrate on one object or place per round.

spells.name: Misfeel Power X
spells.description: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level can be misrepresented by up to 10 levels.

spells.name: Disillusion III
spells.description: As Disillusion I, except up to three illusions within the area of effect cease to exist for the caster.

spells.name: Misfeel Multiple
spells.description: Applies all the lower level Misfeels to a target at once (only one Misfeel Power can be applied).

spells.name: Unpresence I
spells.description: Target becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.

spells.name: Disillusion V
spells.description: As Disillusion I, except up to five illusions within the area of effect cease to exist for the caster. 18 Reduce Power Emanations V – As Reduce Power Emanations, except affects up to some combination of five powers/targets, i.e. the caster could use this to conceal up to five powers of a single item, three powers of one item and two powers of another item, one power each of five items, etc. .

spells.name: Mass Shadow
spells.description: As Shadow, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.

spells.name: Disillusion True
spells.description: As Disillusion I, except all illusions within the area of effect cease to exist for the caster.

spells.name: Unpresence V
spells.description: As Unpresence I, except affects up to five targets.

spells.name: Misfeel Multiple
spells.description: As Misfeel Multiple, except duration is 1 hour/level.

spells.name: Lord Unpresence
spells.description: As Unpresence, except 20 targets may be affected.

spells.name: Misfeel Power
spells.description: As Misfeel Power I, except target’s level may be misrepresented by any number of levels.

spells.name: Presence Mirage
spells.description: Creates one false “presence” of a being for the purposes of magical detection for each level of the caster and these will be located as the caster desires within the area of effect. Caster may move these presence mirages by concentrating at a rate of 10'/round each.

spells.name: Mass Misfeel
spells.description: As Misfeel Multiple, except as ROLEMASTER many targets as the caster’s level can be affected. UNIFIED  Spell Law 151 7.5 7.5 - - Illusionist Illusionist Base Base SENSE MOLDING

spells.name: Smell Mirage
spells.description: Fills the area of effect with a set of immobile smells. The smells are real (no RR) and detecting that it is an illusion can normally only be accomplished through spells or the use of a sense other than smell.

spells.name: Strike I
spells.description: Target is struck with the equivalent of a (Medium-sized) bare fist (Martial Arts Striking attack table). The attack is invisible unless combined with the visual sense in an Illusion or Phantasms. Directed Spells: Illusionary Attacks skill may be developed for this attack.

spells.name: Taste Mirage
spells.description: As Smell Mirage, except a set of tastes can be created in a 10'R.

spells.name: Tear Cloud I
spells.description: Creates a 10'R cloud of noxious gas that will stun anyone failing to resist (all targets must make RRs each round they are in the cloud). Cloud drifts with the wind. Targets are stunned (-50) 1 round per 10 failure.

spells.name: Feel Mirage I
spells.description: As Smell Mirage, except all the objects and surfaces in a 10'R can be made to feel differently than they really are. Striking an object or surface will cancel the spell for that object or surface only.

spells.name: Feel/Taste/Smell
spells.description: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has either its taste, its smell, or the way its surface feels altered for the duration of the spell. Note that the visual aspects of ROLEMASTER the object do not change. UNIFIED

spells.name: Strike III
spells.description: As Strike I, except range increment is 75'.  Spell Law

spells.name: Numb
spells.description: Creates a dulling of tactile sensations in the target, especially the hands. Fumble ranges of weapons are increased by 2, applied after any reduction for number of ranks, and all tasks requiring tactile feedback (e.g., picking a lock, dancing with a partner, playing the piano) are at -25, while Perception rolls for the sense of touch are at -50. 152

spells.name: Tear Cloud II
spells.description: As Tear Cloud I, except radius is 20'.

spells.name: Feel Mirage III
spells.description: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck three times before the feeling is cancelled.

spells.name: Strike V
spells.description: As Strike I, except range increment is 125'.

spells.name: Feel Terrain
spells.description: All of the terrain in the area of effect will feel differently than it normally would. The caster may designate in what way the terrain feels differently. For example, flat ground could be made to feel rocky or rocky ground could be made to feel flat.

spells.name: Mass Smell/Taste
spells.description: As many objects as the caster’s level can be given individual smells and tastes.

spells.name: Tear Cloud V
spells.description: As Tear Cloud I, except radius is 50'.

spells.name: Feel Mirage V
spells.description: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck five times before the feeling is cancelled.

spells.name: Mass Feel Mirages
spells.description: As many objects as the caster’s level can be given individual feelings to the touch. Striking an object or surface will cancel the spell for that object only.

spells.name: Striking I
spells.description: As Strike I, except one attack can be made each round. Target may be changed from one round to another.

spells.name: Feel Mirage VIII
spells.description: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck eight times before the feeling is cancelled.

spells.name: Tear Cloud X
spells.description: As Tear Cloud I, except radius is 100'.

spells.name: Feel Mirage X
spells.description: As Feel Mirage I, except objects with a feel mirage on them must be struck ten times before the feeling is cancelled.

spells.name: Smell Glamour
spells.description: Caster can give one object (up to 10 pounds per level) a permanent scent.

spells.name: Taste Glamour
spells.description: Caster can give one object (up to 10 pounds per level) a permanent taste.

spells.name: Tear Cloud True
spells.description: As Tear Cloud I, except radius and range are as listed.

spells.name: Feel Glamour True
spells.description: Caster can give one object (up to 10 pounds per level) a permanent feel to its surface.

spells.name: Mirage True
spells.description: As Smell Mirage, Taste Mirage, and Feel Mirage I, except all changes are permanent on every affected object and surface unless it is moved beyond the area of effect. 7.5 7.5 - - Illusionist Illusionist Base Base SOUND MOLDING

spells.name: Sound Mirage
spells.description: Creates a set of immobile sounds in an area of up to 10'R. The sounds are real (no RR) and detecting that it is an illusion can normally only be accomplished through spells or the use of a sense other than hearing.

spells.name: Sphere of Silence
spells.description: Creates a 10'R area into and out of which sound cannot travel. As noted above, this spell can be cast on an individual target, which the spell will then follow; this target gains +25 to Stalking attempts, but likewise automatically fails any Perception attempts based on hearing alone and suffers a -50 to Perception rolls where hearing is a factor (e.g., to detect someone Stalking the target). Note that this does not interfere with spell-casting (the target can still speak, even yell, the sound is simply unable to leave the radius of the Sphere of Silence - certain spells like Ultrasonics may have their range limited to the interior of the Sphere of Silence radius, however).

spells.name: Sound Control I
spells.description: Caster can control the intensity of sounds within the range; the intensity can vary from none to a very loud shout, and it can be different in different parts of the radius.

spells.name: Sudden Sound
spells.description: Causes a very loud, sudden sound next to the target’s ears; target is stunned (-50) for 1 round per 10 failure.

spells.name: Sound Control V
spells.description: As Sound Control I, except radius is 50'.

spells.name: Sound Glamour
spells.description: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its sound altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes.

spells.name: Muffle
spells.description: Target cannot hear sounds occurring more than 6" from the target’s ears (or other organs of hearing), and the target’s own voice cannot be heard from more than 6" away from the target’s mouth (or other organ of speech).

spells.name: Sphere of Silence
spells.description: As Sphere of Silence I, except radius is 50'.

spells.name: Minor Ultrasonics
spells.description: All beings (except the caster) within range capable of hearing ultrasonic sounds are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; an RR must be made once per round while in the radius. Even if they are not stunned, animals capable of hearing ultra7.5 sonics will have to make a second, non-magical RR against Fear Illusionist (level of Fear is half the caster’s level). Base Lists

spells.name: Sound Control X
spells.description: As Sound Control I, except radius is 100'.

spells.name: Sudden Sound
spells.description: As Sudden Sound, except everyone in a 20'R is affected.

spells.name: Sound Terrain
spells.description: All of the terrain in the area of effect will have sounds that are different than the terrain’s normal sounds. The caster may designate in what way the terrain sounds different. For example, an open plain could be made to sound like a rustling forest. Note that only the audio aspect of the terrain changes. This can provide between a -30 to a +30 modification to all Stalking attempts within the terrain, depending on how “noisy” the illusionary environment is.

spells.name: Sphere of Silence
spells.description: As Sphere of Silence I, except radius is 100'.

spells.name: Muffle Sphere
spells.description: As Muffle, except everyone in a 10'R is affected.

spells.name: Greater Sound
spells.description: As Sound Control I, except radius is 10'R/lvl.

spells.name: Long Silence
spells.description: As Sphere of Silence I, except range is 10' per level.

spells.name: Long Sound Control
spells.description: As Sound Contol I, except range is 10' per level.

spells.name: Long Sudden
spells.description: As Sudden Sound, except for range.

spells.name: Long Muffle
spells.description: As Muffle, except range is 10' per level.

spells.name: Mass Muffling
spells.description: As Muffle, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected.

spells.name: Mass Quiet
spells.description: As many targets as the caster’s level can have a 1'R silence on them. Except for the radius hugging closer to the body of the target, this functions as described in Sphere of Silence I.

spells.name: Sound Control
spells.description: As Sound Control I, except radius is 20'/lvl and the caster does not have to concentrate except to make changes.

spells.name: Sudden Sound
spells.description: As Sudden Sound Sphere II, except everyone in a 50'R is affected.

spells.name: Sudden Sound Sphere
spells.description: As Sudden Sound Sphere II, except everyone in a 100'R is affected and the range is 500'.

spells.name: Major Ultrasonics
spells.description: All beings (except the caster) within range capable of hearing ultrasonic sounds are stunned (-50) 1 rnd/10 failure; an RR must be made once per round while in the radius. Anyone failing to resist by 40-75 falls unconscious, while anyone failing the RR by more than 75 dies. Even if they are not stunned, animals capable of hearing ultrasonics will have to make a second, non-magical RR against Fear (level of Fear is equal to ROLEMASTER the caster’s level). UNIFIED  Spell Law 153 7.6 7.6 - - Magician Magician Base Base EARTH LAW

spells.name: Bake Clay
spells.description: Caster transforms up to one cubic foot per level of clay into baked pottery. Firing clay in this manner will always be successful. This can also cook glazes onto earthenware. Quality of product depends on the skill of whomever shapes the clay.

spells.name: Loosen Earth
spells.description: Loosens 100 cubic feet of earth to the consistency of plowed ground.

spells.name: Stoneworking
spells.description: Caster receives a +30 bonus on any maneuvers involving the working of stone.

spells.name: Earthwall
spells.description: Creates a wall of packed earth up to 10'x10'x (3' at base, 1' at top); it can be dug through only (20 man-rounds at top).

spells.name: Cracks Call
spells.description: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.

spells.name: Repair
spells.description: Caster may mend a single break in a small (up to 2 pounds) inorganic object or multiple breaks, rips, or cracks in a larger (up to 10 pounds) organic object. All component parts of the object must be within a 10' radius.

spells.name: Stonewall
spells.description: Creates a wall of non-precious stone up to 10'x10'x1'; it can be chipped through or toppled if not against a wall.

spells.name: Earthen Spikes
spells.description: This spell causes the area of effect to bristle with hundreds of 6" to 12" sharp spikes and points. The exact composition of these spikes will depend on the material the ground is made of. The area of effect must be placed horizontally on a ROLEMASTER surface (i.e., it cannot be placed on walls). Most animals and creaUNIFIED tures cannot be induced to walk through the area. Anyone moving  through the area must make a maneuver roll (at least Extremely Spell Law Hard difficulty) for every 5' moved through. Anyone who falls inside the area of effect receives an A Puncture critical strike.

spells.name: Stone/Earth
spells.description: Changes 100 cubic feet of stone to packed earth. This change is gradual, taking three rounds to complete. 154

spells.name: Earthwall True
spells.description: As Earthwall, except duration is permanent.

spells.name: Earth/Mud
spells.description: As Stone/Earth, except changes earth to soft mud.

spells.name: Earth/Stone
spells.description: As Stone/Earth, except changes packed earth to solid stone and loose earth to gravel.

spells.name: Stonewall True
spells.description: As Stonewall, except duration is permanent and dimensions may be up to 20'x20'x1'.

spells.name: Mud/Earth
spells.description: As Stone/Earth, except changes mud to packed earth.

spells.name: Unearth
spells.description: Disintegrates 100 cubic feet of earth.

spells.name: Meld Walls
spells.description: Fuses two touching walls together (seam can be up to 20' long) or fuses a section of stone blocks (up to 100 cubic feet).

spells.name: Stone/Mud
spells.description: As Stone/Earth, except changes stone to mud.

spells.name: Curved Stonewall
spells.description: As Stonewall True, except wall may be curved to a semicircle.

spells.name: Unflaw
spells.description: Removes a flaw from a single gem, mineral, or stone. This can greatly enhance the value of some jewels.

spells.name: Unstone
spells.description: Disintegrates 100 cubic feet of stone.

spells.name: Unmetal
spells.description: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot of metal.

spells.name: Tremors
spells.description: Causes a very minor earthquake which could cause shoddy construction to collapse (and can be very terrifying). This is approximately 5.5 on the Richter scale.

spells.name: Great Wall of
spells.description: As Stonewall True, except wall may be curved as desired so long as total length does not exceed 10' per level and wall is two feet thick.

spells.name: Animate Stone
spells.description: Transforms one stone into a stone golem (sophistication of shape depending on caster’s artistic skills) for the duration of the spell, during which it will obey the caster faithfully unless control is wrested by another (in which case the caster can always cancel the spell and return the golem to boulder form before duration runs out). Size of the golem is based on the stone’s weight (which may be modified up to 50% in order to achieve desired Size) and may be up to Large.

spells.name: Earth Mastery
spells.description: Caster can use one lower level spell (on this list) each round. 7.6 7.6 - - Magician Magician Base Base FIRE LAW

spells.name: Boiling Heat
spells.description: For each level of the caster, one cubic foot of liquid can be heated to boiling at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round. Once the caster ceases concentration, the liquid (if any remains) will cool normally.

spells.name: Warm Solid
spells.description: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material (up to 1 cubic foot per level) can be warmed to 100°F (38°C) at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round.

spells.name: Woodfires
spells.description: Causes any wood to ignite and burn. All wood ignited must be within 1' of caster’s palm.

spells.name: Wall of Fire
spells.description: Creates an opaque wall of fire (up to 10'x10'x6"). Anyone passing through it takes an A Heat critical (no RR).

spells.name: Heat Solid
spells.description: As Warm Solid, except for duration and material can be heated to 500°F (260°C) at a rate of one cubic foot heated by 100°F (56°C) per round. The caster must concentrate to increase the temperature (without concentration, the material will retain its current temperature for the duration of the spell).

spells.name: Fire Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of fire is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Fire Bolt Table.

spells.name: Call Flame I
spells.description: Creates a cube of flame (up to 10'x10'x10'); it takes 1 complete round for the cube to form and be effective. Anyone passing through (or caught within) the cube takes an A Heat critical each round spent in the cube.

spells.name: Fire Ball
spells.description: A 1' diameter ball of fire is shot from the palm of the caster. Upon reaching the selected target/location, it expands to affect a 10'R area. Resolve attack on the Fire Ball Table.

spells.name: Ignite
spells.description: The caster may designate a type of light source to ignite or extinguish (e.g., candles, torches, fireplaces, or lanterns). This spell will affect all of the light sources of the specified type(s) within the area of effect.

spells.name: Circle Aflame
spells.description: As Wall of Fire, except “wall” is 10' high and forms a 10'R circle (6" thick) with the caster at the center (it is non-mobile).

spells.name: Fire Bolt III
spells.description: As Fire Bolt I, except range increment is 75'.

spells.name: Call Flame II
spells.description: As Call Flame I, except cube is up to 20'x20'x20' in size and the range is 20'.

spells.name: Waiting Flame
spells.description: As Call Flame I, except the effect can be delayed for up to 24 hours. The effect can be triggered by one of the following (decided by the caster at time of casting): time 7.6 period, certain movements, certain sounds, touch, violent actions Magician in the area, or any other trigger so long as the caster would be able Base Lists to sense it if the caster remained at the area of effect.

spells.name: Firestorm

If the object is on a being, it gets an RR, and if it fails, the being takes an automatic Heat critical of a severity to be determined by its location on the being’s body. If the object is touching exposed flesh, the being takes a C Heat critical; if the object is touching through cloth, the being takes a B Heat critical; any being within a 1'R takes an A Heat critical.

Once the liquid begins to freeze it will not continue to cool, and will begin to warm naturally if the caster stops concentrating. (Temp. cannot be lower then -20°F[-29°C]).

Following the initial formation round, the cloud delivers a C Electricity critical to all within the area of effect on the first and second rounds, a B critical on the third and fourth rounds, and an A critical on the fifth and sixth rounds, after which it dissipates. It drifts with the wind.

spells.name: Familiar
spells.description: The caster can attune himself to a small creature of animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw) to serve as his familiar. The caster must obtain the creature (which can be no more than 10% of caster’s own mass) through normal means and cast the spell on the animal once each day for 1 week (associating with the animal for 2 hours each day). The caster can then control the familiar and view the world through its senses by concentrating on it (must be within 50' per caster level). If the animal is killed, the caster will have a -25 modification to all actions for 2 weeks.

spells.name: Summons I
spells.description: Caster can instantly summon a first level non-intelligent (i.e., possessing the Animalistic or Mindless flaw) living creature, which appears somewhere with the spell’s range. The caster can specify the general type of creature, but exactly what the creature is should be determined randomly (e.g., the caster could specify “four-legged, hoofed” and could get a zebra, horse, camel, etc.). If the creature can understand the caster’s communication, the creature can be controlled by normal communication. Otherwise, control is maintained through concentration of the caster. If the creature summoned is used for a task that is not dangerous (in its own perception), the duration for this spell is 10 minutes per level. However, if the creature is ever put into a dangerous situation, the duration immediately drops to 1 round per level. At the end of the spell’s duration, the summoned creature will disappear back to whence it came.

spells.name: Investiture I
spells.description: This spell is cast upon the caster’s familiar. However, the familiar must be a type of animal that is usually 7.6 Evil associated with evil in the caster’s culture. This spell transfers part Essence of the caster’s “life” into the familiar. This spell must be cast upon the familiar every day for a month (associating with the animal Lists for 2 hours a day). The familiar retains all of its old abilities and it gains special abilities. The familiar will become a malformed version of the animal it once was (more grotesque). The caster can view the world through the senses of the creature at a range of 250' per level. The creatures AT changes to 3 (if not already higher), and it gains a +10 to its DB and OB. The caster can cast spells through the familiar as if the caster were actually where the familiar is (this includes casting “self” spells on the familiar): range for this ability is 50' per level. The creature will make all of its RRs at half the caster’s level or its own level, whichever is higher. The familiar can be sent on long-range missions, although it will be limited in its ability to interpret ambiguous instructions or adapt to unexpected events. It can always find its way back to the caster through their bond unless the caster is magically hidden. If the familiar dies, the caster will lose one quarter of his Power Points and Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks (and these cannot be restored by any means until the time has passed).

spells.name: Summons II
spells.description: As Summons I, except the caster chooses one of the following options: a) the level of the summoned creature is increased by one, b) an extra 1st level creature can be summoned, or c) the duration can be doubled. When applying options to the higher level Summons spells, option “a” can only be applied to one creature at a time (i.e., two 2nd level creatures takes three options: one for the second creature and one each to raise the creatures to 2nd level).

spells.name: Binding II
spells.description: Caster can bind one creature (up to second level) with animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw). The bound creature must follow the commands of the caster (the caster does not have to concentrate). The caster can only have as many creatures bound as he has levels. If the creature is ever outside the range of the spell, the spell is dispelled.

spells.name: Summons III
spells.description: As Summons II, except two options are chosen.

spells.name: Order Bound II
spells.description: The target of this spell is any creature with animal intelligence (i.e., with the Animalistic flaw) that has been bound (with a Binding spell). The caster can give a simple, straight-forward task for the creature to attempt to complete. The creature will do its best to complete the task (within the duration of the spell). When the duration of this spell expires, the creature returns to its normal “unbound” state. Once this spell is cast, the creature can leave the range of the spell without ending its duration.

spells.name: Summons V
spells.description: As Summons II, except four options are chosen.

spells.name: Binding V
spells.description: As Binding II, except binds up to a 5th level creature.

spells.name: Summons VII
spells.description: As Summons II, except six options may be chosen. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED

spells.name: Investiture II
spells.description: The target of this spell is a familiar that has  had Investiture I successfully cast on it. This spell puts more of Spell Law the caster into his familiar. The caster must cast this spell once per day for six months (spending two hours each day). The caster can use the familiar’s senses up to 1,000 feet per level away and can cast spells through the familiar up to 200' per level away. The creature’s AT improves to 5, the DB bonus improves to +20 and 161 the OB bonus improves to +30, and it makes RRs at the higher of its own level or three fourths of the caster’s level. It also gains venomous attack that is used in conjunction with its normal attack (poison type of the caster’s choice, equal level to the familiar). The appearance of the familiar becomes even more grotesque, beginning to assume aspects of the caster’s appearance. The change 7.7 Evil may be extreme enough to make it unrecognizable as the animal Essence was. If the familiar dies, the caster loses half his Power Points and Lists Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks.

spells.name: Summons X
spells.description: As Summons II, except nine options are chosen.

spells.name: Binding X
spells.description: As Binding II, except binds up to a 10th level creature.

spells.name: Summons XII
spells.description: As Summons II, except eleven options are chosen.

spells.name: Order Bound X
spells.description: As Order Bound II, except maximum duration is 10 hours.

spells.name: Summons XV
spells.description: As Summons II, except fourteen options are chosen.

spells.name: Binding XV
spells.description: As Binding II, except binds up to a 15th level creature.

spells.name: Summons XVII
spells.description: As Summons II, except sixteen options are chosen.

spells.name: Investiture III
spells.description: The target of this spell is a familiar that has had Investiture II successfully cast on it. This spell puts even more of the caster into his familiar. The caster must cast this spell once per day for one year (spending two hours each day). The caster can use the familiar’s senses up to 1 mile per level away and can cast spells through the familiar up to 1000' per level away. The creature’s AT improves to 7, the DB bonus improves to +30 and the OB bonus improves to +50, and it makes RRs at the higher of its own level or the caster’s level. It retains its venom from Investiture II. Its form becomes completely grotesque and could not be associated with the animal it once was. If the familiar dies, the caster loses three quarters of his Power Points and Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks.

spells.name: Lord Summons
spells.description: As Summons II, except nineteen options are chosen.

spells.name: Binding True
spells.description: As Binding II, except binds a creature of any level.

spells.name: Order Bound True
spells.description: As Order Bound II, except maximum duration is 1 hour per caster’s level.

spells.name: Mass Summons
spells.description: As Summons II, except a number of options equal to the caster’s level are chosen.

spells.name: Investiture IV
spells.description: The target of this spell is a familiar that has had Investiture III successfully cast on it. This spell puts even more of the caster into his familiar. The caster must cast this spell once per day for two years (spending two hours each day). The caster can use the familiar’s senses up to 10 miles per level away and can cast spells through the familiar up to 1 mile per level away. The creature’s AT improves to 9, the DB bonus improves to +50 and the OB bonus improves to +75, and it continues to make RRs at the higher of its own level or the caster’s level. It retains its venROLEMASTER om from Investiture II, but potency is the higher of the familiar’s UNIFIED  level or the caster’s level. It cannot really become more grotesque. Spell Law If the familiar dies, the caster loses all of his Power Points and Concussion Hits for 1-5 weeks, spending that period in a coma.

spells.name: Treacherous Essence
spells.description: Any time an attempt to cast a spell from the Realm of Essence within the area of effect results in spell failure, +30 is added to the spell failure roll.

spells.description: Any attempt to cast a spell of the 7.6 Evil Realm of Essence will be made with a -20 penalty to the Essence Spellcasting roll. Lists

spells.name: Chaotic Mentalism
spells.description: Any attempt to cast a spell of the Realm of Mentalism will be made with a -20 penalty to the Spellcasting roll.

spells.name: Chaotic Essence

spells.name: Darken Essence
spells.description: All spells cast that are from an Evil spell list are resisted at -10, while those from other lists suffer a -25 to their Spellcasting rolls and are resisted with a +10 bonus.

spells.description: As any of the above spells of less than 40th level, except for area of effect and duration. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 163 7.7 7.7 - - Evil Evil Essence Essence FOUL TRANSFORMATIONS

spells.name: Bulging Eyes
spells.description: Caster’s eyes enlarge. Caster gains a +20 to Perception rolls involving vision exclusively and a +10 to Perception rolls involving vision along with other senses. Any vision enhancement spells cast on the caster while this spell is active will last until this spell expires or their normal duration, whichever is longer.

spells.name: Armoring I
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 2. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.

spells.name: Prehensile Tail
spells.description: Caster grows a prehensile tail strong enough to support his own weight.

spells.name: Tentacles
spells.description: Caster grows several tentacles, which can be used to make an attack on the Grapple Attack Table without occupying the caster’s hands. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.

spells.name: Armoring II
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 3. This covers the entire body and constitutes ROLEMASTER a full set of armor. UNIFIED 

spells.name: Wings I
spells.description: Caster grows wings. Caster can now fly at a Spell Law rate equal to his normal walking speed. He can fly at faster paces, but must make Endurance rolls as if moving one pace faster than actual, with movement at Dash pace requiring an Endurance roll every round at a -20 penalty. 164

spells.name: Horns
spells.description: Caster grows horns, which can be used to make an attack on the Horn Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.

spells.name: Armoring III
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 4.

spells.name: Claws
spells.description: Caster grows clawed hands, which can be used to make an attack on the Claw Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.

spells.name: Wings II
spells.description: As Wings I , except Fatigue checks are as normal for the pace of movement and any maneuver rolls related to flight are made with a +20 bonus.

spells.name: Armoring IV
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 5. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.

spells.name: Bite
spells.description: Caster grows fangs or teeth suitable for attacks, which can be used to make an attack on the Bite Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.

spells.name: Spines
spells.description: Caster grows spiny projections. Any time the caster is attacked unarmed or with a weapon less than onehalf the caster’s height in length, the attacker will suffer an attack resolved on the Stinger Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is half the caster’s skill in this list. This attack is automatic and does not require an action by the caster.

spells.name: Wings III
spells.description: As Wings II, except base speed is twice the caster’s normal walking pace.

spells.name: Armoring V
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 6. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.

spells.name: Killer Instincts
spells.description: When using the attacks provided by spells on this list, the caster can substitute his skill in this list for any one of the following Combat Expertise skills: Disarm, Multiple Attacks, Restricted Quarters, or Reverse Strike. The skill to which this applies may be different each round.

spells.name: Envenom
spells.description: The attack provided by Horns, Claws, Bite, or Spines will deliver a poison upon doing a critical. The level of the poison is the caster’s level. Only one attack becomes poisonous, but this spell can be cast multiple times if the caster has cast more than one of the listed spells active. Caster determines the type of poison. This spell lasts as long as the spell whose attack it is enhancing.

spells.name: Armoring VI
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster a natural AT of 7. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.

spells.name: Spinnerets
spells.description: Caster grows a group of spinnerets somewhere on his body, which can be used to make webs. This can be used to create up to 100' of silk rope per round, capable of withstanding a thousand pounds of weight. Webs can be sticky in some parts and not in others, allowing the creation of anchored bridges made of webbing. The webs can also be launched with the ranges of a javelin, with the attack resolved on the Grapple Attack Table. The size of the attack is the same as the size of the caster. The OB of the attack is the caster’s skill in this list.

spells.name: Armoring VII
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster natural AT of 8. This covers the entire body and constitutes a full set of armor.

spells.name: Foul Breath
spells.description: Caster begins to exhale a toxic gas (caster is immune to the effects). Any living thing coming within 10' of the caster must make an RR versus poison each round or suffer a cumulative -5 penalty and take 1d10 damage from the effects of the gas. Treat the penalty as organ damage (lungs) for treatment/healing purposes and the final penalty as a single injury. Level of the poison is the level of the caster. Caster can also exhale a 50' long cone, reaching a width of 25' at its furthest extent, but this requires such effort that an Endurance roll is called for.

spells.name: Armoring VIII
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster natural AT of 9.

spells.name: Adapted Form
spells.description: Caster’s body adapts to survive in environmental conditions. This can involve submersion in freezing water at depths with crushing pressure, traversing molten lava, enduring exposure to a raging sandstorm, or withstanding the bizarre conditions of an alien dimension. This does not cover any form of deliberate attack (even while comfortable naked in a blizzard, the caster will still suffer normal damage from a Cold Ball).

spells.name: Armoring IX
spells.description: Caster’s skin and subcutaneous fat are changed into a more protective form, granting the caster natural AT of 10.
