spells.name: Fit Special
spells.description: As Fit Wood; allows caster to attach a “special” (of enchanted or exotic material) prosthetic.

spells.name: Mechanical Contrivance
spells.description: Allows incorporation of simple mechanisms into a prosthetic (e.g., a small retractable knife blade hidden in a finger), which can then be operated by the will of the wearer when an Animation spell is active on the prosthetic. The mechanism must be constructed normally.

spells.name: Animation IV
spells.description: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 month.

spells.name: Repair Prosthetic
spells.description: Repairs all damage to a single prosthetic.

spells.name: Artificial Organ
spells.description: Allows an artificial organ (created with one of the Mold spells on this list and/or through normal crafting) to be incorporated into a living target. Typically, this will be a glass eye that can provide sight when an Animation spell is cast on it, but theoretically any organ could be replaced with a suitable artificial organ. If an internal organ is replaced, surgery will be required for the insertion of the artificial organ.

spells.name: Mechanical Contrivance
spells.description: As Mechanical Contrivance, except that a complex mechanism (e.g., a dart gun) can be included in a prosthetic.

spells.name: Animation V
spells.description: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 3 months.

spells.name: Artificial Flesh True
spells.description: As Artificial Flesh, except perfectly realistic synthetic flesh can be made and worked. The process will require days instead of hours, with the same numbers as in Artificial Flesh.

spells.name: Animation VI
spells.description: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 year.

spells.name: Animation VII
spells.description: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will for 1 decade.

spells.name: Implant Spell
spells.description: Caster can emplace one spell of any level into a prosthetic. Within one minute of casting this spell, the spell to be emplaced must also be cast (within 10'). Once the spell is implanted, it will remain waiting in the prosthetic until used. When the prosthetic is attached and an Animation spell is in effect, the prosthetic wearer may trigger the spell at any time (treat as an innate power).

spells.name: Prosthetic Addition
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to attach an ROLEMASTER artificial part that is not a replacement. The recipient of the part UNIFIED  could be given wings (two spells and two prosthetics required to actually fly), an extra arm, or the like. An Animation spell is reSpell Law quired for the added part to actually function.

spells.name: Animation True
spells.description: Allows target to operate any artificial limbs (and other prosthetics) at will permanently. 197 8.4 8.4 - - Magent Magent Base Base ASSASSINATION MASTERY

spells.name: Concealed Object
spells.description: Target object (no larger than a dagger) is camouflaged to appear as part of the caster’s clothing or skin. This is an illusion to all senses (including feel), and can be detected as an illusion. The object will remain concealed until taken from the location, or the duration expires.

spells.name: Study Patsy
spells.description: [RR Mod: -50] When this spell is cast on a person, it remains active for 24 hours. After 24 hours has passed (but before another 24 hours has passed), the caster must cast this spell again on the same person. During the time the spell is active, it is recording information concerning the activities of the target. When the spell is cast the second time, the information about those activities is revealed to the caster. This information is used for spells later on this list and spells on other lists (see the Misdirections and Disguise Mastery lists). The caster can only have one “patsy” studied at a time.

spells.name: Well-Aimed Attack
spells.description: The next attack made by the caster (within one minute of casting this spell) receives a bonus of +25.

spells.name: Open Ambush I
spells.description: If the caster’s next attack (made within one minute of casting this spell), gets a “flank” or “rear” bonus, he may use his Ambush skill even if the target is aware of the caster. However, the maximum change to the critical roll from Ambush is ±2.

spells.name: Improvised Attack
spells.description: The caster is able to make use of ROLEMASTER UNIFIED whatever weapons are available, including non-standard weapons  such as a candlestick. Whatever penalties are applied due to the Spell Law use of “similar skill”, limitations of the item when employed as a weapon, or unsuitability for use due a mismatch between the caster and the user for which a weapon is designed (as from Size) are reduced by 20. 198

spells.name: Create Evidence I
spells.description: Caster may cast this spell to create one piece of physical evidence that implicates the currently studied patsy (see Study Patsy). This could include a fingerprint, a footprint, etc., but must be something that cannot normally be moved from the crime scene. The evidence should be treated as circumstantial (i.e., a good alibi should still get the patsy off the hook).

spells.name: Concealed Object
spells.description: As Concealed Object I, except will affect an object up to the size of a one-handed sword.

spells.name: Well-Aimed Attack
spells.description: As Well-Aimed Attack I, except bonus is +50.

spells.name: Open Ambush II
spells.description: As Open Ambush I, except the limit on critical roll modification is ±4.

spells.name: Improvised Attack
spells.description: As Improvised Attack I, except penalties are reduced by 40.

spells.name: Lightning Reactions
spells.description: Caster gains a +20 to his initiative roll this round. This must be the caster’s first action and the +20 applies to the casting of this spell.

spells.name: Create Evidence II
spells.description: As Create Evidence I, except two pieces of evidence are created; one piece of evidence is incriminating (i.e., patsy will need more than a good alibi).

spells.name: Well-Aimed Attack
spells.description: As Well-Aimed Attack I, except bonus is +75.

spells.name: Open Ambush III
spells.description: As Open Ambush I, except the limit on critical roll modification is ±6.

spells.name: Improvised Attack
spells.description: As Improvised Attack I, except penalties are reduced by 60.

spells.name: Concealed Object
spells.description: As Concealed Object I, except object may be of any size, so long as the largest dimension of the object is less than the caster’s height.

spells.name: Study Victim
spells.description: As Study Patsy, except the information it gathers is required for use with the Slaying Attack spells later on this list.

spells.name: Create Evidence
spells.description: As Create Evidence I, except three pieces of evidence are created; two pieces of evidence are incriminating.

spells.name: Well-Aimed Attack
spells.description: As Well-Aimed Attack I, except bonus is +100.

spells.name: Slaying Attack
spells.description: The caster’s next attack (within one minute of casting this spell) against the currently studied victim (see Study Victim) will be resolved as a Slaying IV attack (+20 to the critical roll).

spells.name: Open Ambush V
spells.description: As Open Ambush I, except the limit on critical roll modification is ±10.

spells.name: Improvised Attack
spells.description: As Improvised Attack I, except penalties are reduced by 90.

spells.name: Open Ambush True
spells.description: As Open Ambush I, except the limit on critical roll modification is ±14, the caster has 10 minutes in which to make the attack, and he does not have to have a positional bonus.

spells.name: Improvised Attack
spells.description: As Improvised Attack I, except penalties are reduced by 120.

spells.name: Slaying Attack True
spells.description: As Slaying Attack, except all of the caster’s attacks made against the victim are resolved as Slaying IV attacks, and the duration is 1 minute per level. 8.4 8.4 - - Magent Magent Base Base DISGUISE MASTERY

spells.name: Study Disguise
spells.description: Caster studies a person for use with disguise spells later on this list. The caster can only have as many disguises studied as he has ranks in this list. He may forget a studied disguise at any time to make room for replacements.

spells.name: Light Glamour
spells.description: One object (up to 10 pounds per level) has its visual appearance altered for the duration of the spell. Note that no other aspect of the object changes. This spell cannot make an object cast light (although it may alter the appearance of light given off, whether naturally or from another spell).

spells.name: Façade I
spells.description: Caster has a limited illusion on himself that allows him to look like any humanoid race within 20% of his own size. The illusion moves as the caster moves.

spells.name: Shadow Assassin
spells.description: All deeds performed while this spell is active are “shadowed” to divination spells. All related visions gained through divination effect are shrouded in shadows. For example, a Death’s Mastery cast on the scene of a murder might reveal a shadowy figure performing the deeds (but no details about the identity of the figure).

spells.name: Misfeel Kind • *
spells.description: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, caster appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.

spells.name: Façade II
spells.description: As Façade I, except one extra sense is added.

spells.name: Disguise I
spells.description: Caster physically changes to take on the form and visual appearance of any one studied person (using the Study Disguise spell). This is strictly a physical change.

spells.name: Unknown Assassin
spells.description: As Shadow Assassin, except absolutely no information about the caster will be revealed. For example, a Death’s Memory cast on the scene of a murder might show the victim collapsing with a knife in his chest (but no sign of the assassin).

spells.name: Façade III
spells.description: As Façade I, except two extra senses are added. 8.4 Magent

spells.name: Disguise II
spells.description: As Disguise I, except caster also takes on the Base Lists vocal characteristics of the person mimicked.

spells.name: Misfeel Calling
spells.description: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s profession may be misrepresented.

spells.name: Psychic Tongue
spells.description: Caster may mentally speak with one target; to the target it will seem as if they are communicating normally (i.e., orally).

spells.name: Disguise III
spells.description: As Disguise II, except caster also takes on the obvious mannerisms of the person mimicked (e.g., left-handedness, limp).

spells.name: Façade V
spells.description: As Façade I, except all five basic human senses (sight, hearing, smell, taste, touch) are included.

spells.name: Misfeel Power •
spells.description: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s level may be misrepresented (to an upper limit of twice the caster’s actual level).

spells.name: Disguise IV
spells.description: As Disguise III, except caster also takes on the subtle mannerisms of the person mimicked. This makes him virtually undetectable to all but the closest of associates.

spells.name: Unpresence Self
spells.description: Target becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.

spells.name: False Assassin
spells.description: As Shadow Assassin, except all deeds performed while this spell is active will appear as if the currently studied patsy is performing the caster’s actions (see Study Patsy on Assassination Mastery list).

spells.name: Disguise V
spells.description: As Disguise IV, except even very subtle mannerisms are gained.

spells.name: Misfeel • *
spells.description: Allows caster to use all of the “Misfeel” spells at once.

spells.name: Nondetect
spells.description: Caster and items on his person cannot be detected by any Detect. . spell.

spells.name: Misfeel True
spells.description: As Misfeel, except it has a ten minute/level duration and does not require concentration.

spells.name: Accomplice Façade
spells.description: As Façade III, except duration is only ten minutes per level and it may be used on another person.

spells.name: Shifting Façade
spells.description: As Façade V, except with one round of concentration, the caster can change the façade to another.

spells.name: Disguise Mastery
spells.description: Caster may use any lower level spell on this list, once per round. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 199 8.4 8.4 - - Magent Magent Base Base ESCAPES

spells.name: Locklore
spells.description: Gives the caster +20 bonus for picking the lock analyzed. If the caster takes a round to explain the lock to someone else, that person gets a +10 bonus to pick the lock.

spells.name: Ungag
spells.description: Any minor covering on the head of the caster made of organic material is loosened. This includes any gags or blindfolds.

spells.name: Phantom Tools I
spells.description: This spell allows the caster to perform one maneuver using a Craft or Technical skill as if the caster had a complete set of the appropriate tools with a bonus of +10.

spells.name: Silenced Leave
spells.description: Caster quietly “teleports” a single inanimate object to a point up to 100' away from its current location. There can be no intervening barriers in a direct line between the target and the point (for these purposes, a barrier is anything the object could not physically go through). The object has a maximum weight limit of 1 pound per level of the caster.

spells.name: Untie
spells.description: Any ropes (or similar bindings) that are binding the caster are untied.

spells.name: Phantom Tools II
spells.description: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools ROLEMASTER is +20. UNIFIED

spells.name: Silenced Leaving
spells.description: Caster quietly “teleports” to a point up to  100' away, but there can be no intervening barriers in a direct line Spell Law between the target and the point (for these purposes a barrier is anything the caster could not physically go through; a closed door is a barrier, a pit is not). 200

spells.name: Silenced Long Door
spells.description: As Silenced Leave Item I, except the object can pass through barriers by specifying exact direction and distance. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the object does not move (though the caster still loses the PP).

spells.name: Unbind
spells.description: Each lock or shackle binding the caster has a 75% chance of opening (modified by the sophistication of the lock, see Section 10.14).

spells.name: Silenced Leave
spells.description: As Silenced Leave Item I, except target can be moved 500'.

spells.name: Passing
spells.description: Caster may pass through any inanimate material up to 1 foot/level. It takes 1 round to pass through 2 feet of material.

spells.name: Silenced Long Door
spells.description: As Silenced Leaving I, except caster can pass through barriers by specifying exact distances. If the arrival point coincides with solid material, the caster does not move, instead being stunned (-50) 1-10 rounds, and the PP for this spell are expended.

spells.name: Phantom Tools III
spells.description: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools is +30.

spells.name: Silenced Long Door
spells.description: As Silenced Long Door Item I, except object can be moved up to 500'.

spells.name: Unbind True
spells.description: As Unbind, except will also work on fixed shackles (i.e., those that have been rivetted shut).

spells.name: Silenced Leaving
spells.description: As Silenced Leaving I, except caster can move 300'.

spells.name: Phantom Tools IV
spells.description: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools is +30.

spells.name: Flattening
spells.description: Caster is flattened until becoming virtually two-dimensional. This allows the caster to slide through cracks and makes the caster effectively invisible when viewed from the side.

spells.name: Silenced Long Door
spells.description: As Silenced Long Door I, except caster can move 300'.

spells.name: Passing True
spells.description: As Passing, except caster can pass through as much material as the duration allows at a rate of 10'/rnd.

spells.name: Portal
spells.description: Opens a portal 3'x6'x3' in any solid surface, through which anyone can pass. At the end of the duration, the surface is returned to its previous state. Anyone in the area of effect when the spell ends will be forced back out to the point of entry.

spells.name: Phantom Tools V
spells.description: As Phantom Tools I, except bonus for tools is +50.

spells.name: Silenced Long Door
spells.description: As Silenced Long Door I, except caster can move 1000'.

spells.name: Tracklessness
spells.description: Caster leaves no physical trace of having passed: no footprints, no broken twigs, no scent. Non-magical tracking is impossible. No protection against magical tracking is provided, nor does the caster gain any unusual abilities (the caster will still sink in snow normally, it just will be left unmarked after the caster passes). Deliberate actions will still leave traces (e.g., the caster will not snap a twig off a tree in passing, but if the caster reaches out and breaks a twig, it will stay broken),

spells.name: Silenced Teleport
spells.description: As Silenced Long Door I, except movement range is 10 miles per level. Making a precise appearance is difficult, especially if unfamiliar with the destination. The following chances for error exist: Never seen place (only described) = 50%; been briefly (1 hr) = 25%; Studied (24 hr) = 10%; Studied carefully (1 week) =1%; Lived in (1 yr) = .01 %. In case of failure: first determine direction of error (randomly), then determine amount of error (make an open-ended d100 roll for the number of feet). 8.4 8.4 - - Magent Magent Base Base GATHERING SECRETS

spells.name: Copy I
spells.description: This spell copies a document that the caster is examining. The caster merely concentrates and touches the document with one hand and a blank sheet with his other hand. The copy is not exact (i.e., not a forgery). Note that the caster does not have to be able to read the document. Alternatively, this spell can transcribe (in monochromatic black ink) one scene that he is viewing onto a page, creating the equivalent of a well-drawn, detailed realistic drawing. The process occurs at the rate of 1 minute per page or scene.

spells.name: Listen
spells.description: Caster can pick a point up to 10'/level away and will hear as if physically at that point (there can be intervening objects such as walls). Note that this means that he cannot hear where his ears are.

spells.name: Duplicate I
spells.description: This spell makes a hardened clay copy of a small, 3-dimensional object (up to 6"x6"x6"). This spell requires that the caster have enough clay to make the copy (at a one to one scale). The clay must start off soft (the spell will harden it). This process requires one minute of concentration.

spells.name: Sensory Marker
spells.description: One location is “marked” so that the caster can find it again later with a Far Sense spell. The marker is automatically located when the caster casts a Far Sense spell if the marker is within range of the Far Sense spell. A marker can be placed on any inanimate object. The caster can only have as many markers as he has ranks in this list; a Sensory Marker spell can be cancelled even if the caster is no longer within range of the marker.

spells.name: Copy II
spells.description: As Copy I, except copies 1 page/scene every four rounds.

spells.name: Far Sense I
spells.description: Caster may designate any one of his senses to be used at a “marked” location within range (see Sensory Marker). The caster can use any perception abilities he has at the time (e.g., if he is currently under the effects of Nightvision, he will have Nightvision at the marked location). The remote sensory point may be rotated to face any direction. While he is sensing at the marked location, the caster must remain immobile and concentrate (and does not have that sense available at his own location).

spells.name: Locate Evidence
spells.description: After casting this spell, the caster will know what (if any) evidence exists within the range which would implicate the currently studied patsy if found at the scene of the crime (see Study Patsy on the Assassination Mastery list). The evidence (if any exists) will be circumstantial, but will imply that the patsy was at the scene when a crime occurred. This spell pro8.4 Magent vides only the direction, distance, and description of the piece of evidence. It does not provide any details as to why the patsy will Base Lists be implicated by the evidence.

spells.name: Watch
spells.description: As Listen, except caster sees from the fixed point (it can rotate).

spells.name: Duplicate II
spells.description: As Duplicate I, except will work on an object up to 3' x 3' x 3' in dimensions.

spells.name: Copy III
spells.description: As Copy I, except copies 1 page/scene per 2 rounds.

spells.name: Far Sense II
spells.description: As Far Sense I, except caster may designate up to two senses for use at the marked location.

spells.name: Woodsight
spells.description: Caster can see through wood (up to a maximum thickness of 1"/level).

spells.name: Locate Evidence
spells.description: As Locate Evidence I, except two pieces of evidence are located (providing such exist) and the evidence will be incriminating (not just circumstantial).

spells.name: Duplicate III
spells.description: As Duplicate I, except will work on an object up to 6' x 6' x 6' in dimensions.

spells.name: Copy IV
spells.description: As Copy I, except copies 1 page/scene per round.

spells.name: Greater Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense I, except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).

spells.name: Stonesight
spells.description: Caster can see through stone (up to a maximum thickness of 1"/level).

spells.name: Locate Evidence
spells.description: As Locate Evidence I, except three pieces of evidence are located and the evidence will “prove” that the patsy is guilty.

spells.name: Greater Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense II, except caster can move while the spell is active (though he must maintain concentration).

spells.name: Ironsight
spells.description: Caster can see through iron or steel (up to a maximum thickness of 1"/level).

spells.name: Long Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense I, except the range is 500 feet/level.

spells.name: Copy V
spells.description: As Copy I, except copies 2 pages/scenes per round.

spells.name: Synchronized
spells.description: Caster touches two similar writing surfaces (e.g., two parchments, two bound volumes of like dimensions, or two clay tablets) and so long as the spell remains in effect, anything written on one of these documents will also appear written on the other.

spells.name: Long Far Sense
spells.description: As Far Sense II, except the range is 500 feet/level.

spells.name: Copy True
spells.description: As Copy I, except will copy any bound volume onto an equal number of pages (not necessarily bound). Also, the caster need not examine the material or maintain touch contact after the spell begins (he simply casts the spell and then he can leave the spell running). The spell will copy pages at the rate of 5 ROLEMASTER pages per round (and will even copy color images). UNIFIED  Spell Law 201 8.4 8.4 - - Magent Magent Base Base MISDIRECTIONS

spells.name: False Credentials
spells.description: [RR Mod = -20] Target will think that the caster has the proper credentials for the current situation (this could include passwords or documents).

spells.name: Unseen I *
spells.description: A single object is made invisible (1 garment, 1 naked body, etc.); until 24 hrs pass or the object is struck by a violent blow (being hit by a weapon, falling, etc.) or the object makes a violent move (i.e., an attack) or casts a spell of type E or F.

spells.name: Eyewitness III
spells.description: [RR Mod = -20] Up to three targets will incorrectly identify the caster as someone else (all three will describe the caster in the same fashion).

spells.name: Quick Cloaking
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] Affects up to three targets. Each target makes an RR. Failure results in the caster being invisible to the target; others see the caster normally. If the caster makes an obvious action (e.g., talking, moving an object, etc.), any target that would normally sense that action may make another RR (the GM may deem it appropriate to apply modifiers to this RR based upon the action the caster takes). If the caster attacks a target, that target may make another RR with a +50 modifier. If the caster begins to concentrate, he can extend the duration of this ROLEMASTER spell indefinitely (as long as he can concentrate). UNIFIED

spells.name: Misleading I
spells.description: Target’s perception is skewed. All Awareness  maneuvers are modified by -10. Spell Law

spells.name: Unseen II *
spells.description: As Unseen I, except affects two objects.

spells.name: Quick Cloaking
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except affects up to five targets.

spells.name: Eyewitness V
spells.description: As Eyewitness III, except affects five targets. 202

spells.name: Quick Cloaking Sphere I *
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except every target in the area of effect must make an RR. New targets entering the sphere while the spell is still in effect will need to make an RR or be affected as well. Once a target makes a successful RR, he need not make another RR against the spell, however once affected, leaving the sphere does not cancel the effect.

spells.name: Misleading III
spells.description: As Misleading I, except penalties are -30.

spells.name: Quick Cloaking
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except affects up to ten targets.

spells.name: Displacement I
spells.description: Caster’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.

spells.name: Eyewitness X
spells.description: As Eyewitness III, except affects ten targets.

spells.name: Quick Cloaking Sphere II *
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking Sphere I, except area of effect is a twenty foot radius.

spells.name: Misleading V
spells.description: As Misleading I, except penalties are -50.

spells.name: Quick Cloaking
spells.description: As Quick Cloaking III, except affects up to twenty targets.

spells.name: False Trail
spells.description: Creates a false trail with the same physical markings (typically footprints) and scent(s) as the actual trail left by the caster, but Routine (+30) to follow (without appearing to be so). The trail can be laid out as the caster wishes, within the range of the spell. This can be especially effective when combined with some form of teleportation to break the real trail.

spells.name: Mass Eyewitness
spells.description: As Eyewitness III, except affects one target per level of the caster.

spells.name: Quick Cloaking Sphere III *
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking Sphere I, except area of effect is a thirty foot radius.

spells.name: Misleading True
spells.description: As Misleading I, except penalties are -100.

spells.name: Displacement III
spells.description: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 30%.

spells.name: True Quick Cloaking
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking III, except the caster can affect as many targets as he has levels and the range is only limited to the caster’s field of vision.

spells.name: Quick Cloaking Sphere X *
spells.description: [RR Mod = -50] As Quick Cloaking Sphere I, except area of effect is a hundred foot radius.

spells.name: Displacement IV
spells.description: As Displacement I, except chance of missing is 40%.

spells.name: Eyewitness True
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -50] As Eyewitness III, except affects everyone who sees the caster. 8.4 8.4 - - Magent Magent Base Base POISON MASTERY

spells.name: Poison Lore
spells.description: The caster knows what type of poison(s) are needed to achieve a particular effect. The GM may deem it appropriate to give the caster a bonus of +25 to any maneuver directly involving poison(s).

spells.name: Poison Resistance
spells.description: The caster receives an additional RR versus poisons (i.e., if the caster is poisoned, the caster has two chances to roll a successful RR).

spells.name: Apply Poison I
spells.description: This spell causes one dose of a poison to adhere to an inanimate, solid surface. The poison will wear off normally.

spells.name: Work Poison V
spells.description: This spell magically prepares a poison (up to 5th level in potency). This spell normally requires d100 minutes before it is completed. Note that the caster must have the poison in its raw form before casting this spell (i.e., the spell does not create the poison; it merely prepares it).

spells.name: Poison Analysis
spells.description: Caster may analyze a poison to determine the exact effects and what antidotes are necessary. Poison may be on an item or in a being.

spells.name: Delay Poison *
spells.description: For the duration of this spell, all effects from a single poison are suspended. Note that the poison will still be in the caster and takes effect normally after the duration (though the spell may be recast to continue delaying the effects, and some poisons may leave the caster’s system before the spell expires).

spells.name: Transfer Poison I
spells.description: Caster can transfer a single dose of a poison from one target to the caster (who can then use the self-only poison negation spells on this list to deal with it).

spells.name: Apply Poison II
spells.description: As Apply Poison I, except affects two doses (which can be applied to different surfaces).

spells.name: Neutralize Poison
spells.description: Any poison inside the caster must make an RR with a -20 modifier or be neutralized. In any case, it delays the effects of the poison for the duration of the spell.

spells.name: Work Poison X
spells.description: As Work Poison V, except affects poisons up 8.4 Magent to a potency of 10th level. Base Lists

spells.name: Venom

The target must be no further away than 5' and the caster’s mouth must be capable of delivering the poison (either through a kiss, a whisper, spitting, etc.). The target gets an RR to avoid the effects of this spell (in addition to the RR to resist the poison).

The poison will linger in the area normally until the duration of the spell expires (i.e., the mist will be subject to the wind, etc.). When the duration expires, the poison disperses.

spells.name: Transfer Poison IV
spells.description: As Transfer Poison III, except caster need not touch the target from which the poison is transferred, both targets need merely be within the spell’s range.

spells.name: Venom’s Persistence

If in or on an object (e.g., goblet of a poisoned ROLEMASTER UNIFIED drink, poison on a blade, but not a person), then the poison will  linger for 24 hours, even if normally effective washing procedures Spell Law are done. This residual poison will continue to affect anyone who would normally be affected by the poison there, but it is diluted from the original potency and will allow a normal single RR, with a +10 bonus.

When UNIFIED cast, this spell acts as a filter and interpreter which allows a caster  to understand and tolerate these mental differences. This can be Spell Law as mundane as catching cultural implications in someone’s Inner Thoughts or as exotic as understanding interdimensional fish monsters which directly sense forces of gravitation, magnetism, essence, and luck. 206

The following are not allowed as shared skills: Body Development, Power Point Development, Spell Lists. The skill ranks are not lost to the individual who originally had them. spells.name: Unpain IV *

spells.name: Meditative Sleep

spells.name: Self Keeping *

Target then remains ‘studied’ until spell duration ends.

spells.name: Swing I *
spells.description: Allows caster to leap up to 10' vertically or laterally, grasp and swing from a fixed object, and land perfectly (up to 20' away from the object). The object could be a tree, branch, rafter, rope, chandelier, etc. This swing is included as part of this instantaneous spell action (i.e., takes no additional AP).

spells.name: Perfect Stance *
spells.description: Caster is immune to stagger effects (includes “prone” or “knocked back” results) for the duration of this spell.

spells.name: Dodging I *
spells.description: If the caster performs a Full Dodge for a round, he gains +20 to his DB. If he uses a Partial Dodge, he gets half that bonus.

spells.name: Swing III *
spells.description: As Swing I, except 3 swings may be executed in rapid succession.

spells.name: Flip I *
spells.description: Allows caster to “flip” in any direction, landing up to 10' away (facing in any direction). This flip is included as part of this instantaneous spell action (i.e., takes no additional AP).

spells.name: Soft Step I
spells.description: Caster can tread very quietly, providing a +25 bonus to Stalking maneuvers when moving.

spells.name: Dodging II *

spells.name: Moving Dodge I *
spells.description: Caster may move at up to a Running pace and Dodge without suffering a penalty due to pace. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Defensive Contortion
spells.description: Caster reacts to an attack to take some control of the placement of an unavoidable blow. This allows him to change the result rolled on a critical taken by himself by up to ±5. This only applies to criticals that result from attacks using a Combat Training skill (e.g., it could be used against a sword attack or a crossbow bolt, but not a Fire Bolt or a Wall of Fire). This effect applies to the first applicable critical taken within the spell’s duration, after which the spell is cancelled.

spells.name: Weightless
spells.description: Caster is all but weightless when standing or treading on a surface of some kind (but falling is normal). This allows the caster to be supported easily by tiny twigs at the ends of tree branches, unsafe roofs, and other surfaces that would normally give under the pressure of his weight. The spell also greatly reduces the impact of the caster’s passing, giving a -75 penalty to attempts to track him by looking for signs (but not by scent or by magic).

spells.name: Dodging IV *

spells.name: Freedom *

spells.name: Shield Instinct *

spells.name: Shattering Blow
spells.description: Caster can strike an inanimate object and deliver damage to it (taking no damage himself). Caster makes a normal Unarmed attack against the object. In addition, the object must make a normal Breakage Check.

Only one wound at a time can be ignored in this fashion (i.e., the caster cannot have more than one Lion’s Heart spell active at any given time).

spells.name: Physical Discipline

spells.description: As Shattering Blow I, except object receives a -100 penalty to its Breakage Check.

spells.name: Leaping I *
spells.description: Allows caster to make one leap up to 50' laterally or 20' vertically (as a separate 1 AP action, with no Pace penalty for the leaping movement) in the round that the spell was cast.

spells.name: Landing *
spells.description: This spell may be cast when the caster begins to fall, taking effect immediately, allowing the caster to land safely in a fall up to 20' per level of the caster and to take that distance off the severity of any longer fall.

spells.name: Traction
spells.description: Caster can walk or run on even, unstable surfaces (sand, ice, etc.) as if on a hard, stable surface.

spells.name: Edgerunning
spells.description: Caster can walk or run on even, narrow (at least 2" wide) surfaces as if on normal ground.

spells.name: Leaping III *
spells.description: As Leaping I, except the caster can execute 3 “leaps” in succession. Each leap must be within 90° of the last leap’s direction. Each leap is a 1 AP action.

spells.name: Cornering I *
spells.description: Allows caster to execute a turn up to 180° with no deceleration or unbalancing (can be used with any “running” spells). ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Levitation
spells.description: Allows caster to move up and down vertiUNIFIED  cally 10' per round. This spell does not confer any ability to Spell Law control horizontal movement, which must be accomplished by other means.

spells.name: Landing True *
spells.description: As Landing, except caster can land safely from any fall 99% of the time, and still takes the normal 20'/level off any fall for the 1% of the time that safety 214 is not complete.

spells.name: Wallwalking

spells.name: Windrunning
spells.description: Caster can walk or run on air if there is a wind blowing. However, running directly into the wind is not possible, and when running in any other direction, the wind’s speed will modify the caster’s movement rate (i.e., like the effects of wind on a sail boat).

spells.name: Liquidrunning
spells.description: Caster can walk on any liquid surface as if on level ground.

spells.name: Scent *
spells.description: Caster gains extremely acute sense of smell (e.g., +50 to Perception involving only smell, +25 to Perception involving smell and other senses).

spells.name: Watervision *
spells.description: Caster can see 100' in any water (including murky water) as if through air in daylight.

spells.name: Fogvision *
spells.description: As Nightvision, plus caster sees 100' in any precipitation (including thick fog) as if the air were clear.

spells.name: Touch *
spells.description: Caster gains extreme tactile sensitivity. This provides a bonus to any action requiring a sense of touch. For example, caster might receive +25 to picking locks, disarming traps, opening secret doors, etc. and +50 to Perception involving only touch, +25 to Perception involving touch and other senses).

spells.name: Darkvision *
spells.description: As Nightvision, except any darkness can be seen through. No light at all is needed for this spell to work.

spells.name: Detect Invisible
spells.description: Detects any invisible objects or beings; one 5'R area can be checked each round. All attacks against detected targets are modified by -50.

spells.name: Detect Illusion
spells.description: Caster can check one object or place (up to a 5'R) and tell if it is an illusion or has an illusion on it. 8.6 Monk Base Lists

spells.name: Scent True *

spells.description: While this spell is in effect, one illusion within the caster’s range ceases to exist for the caster only. The caster must specify the illusion to be negated (unless caster knows something is an illusion, this spell would be gambling PP).

spells.name: Woodsight
spells.description: Caster can see through wood (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: See Invisible
spells.description: Caster can see all invisible things anywhere that would normally be visible to the caster. In addition, the caster suffers no penalties against invisible targets (as they are not invisible to the caster).

spells.name: Fabricsight
spells.description: Caster can see through any woven organic substance (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Long Vision
spells.description: As any of the lower level Vision spells on this list but without a range limit (i.e., it will function out to normal limits of sight).

spells.name: Illusionsight
spells.description: Caster cannot see any visual illusions. This does not affect any of the other senses.

spells.name: Stonesight
spells.description: Caster can see through stone (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Monkvision
spells.description: As all lower level Vision spells functioning at the same time.

spells.name: Ironsight
spells.description: Caster can see through iron or steel (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Metalsight
spells.description: Caster can see through any metal (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Vigilance
spells.description: Caster gains a +100 bonus to any Perception roll to detect danger.

spells.name: Substancesight
spells.description: Caster can see through any one chosen substance (up to 1" per level).

spells.name: Mental Block I
spells.description: The target’s memory of a 20 minute period is temporarily eliminated. After a period of up to one hour (specified by the caster at the time of casting), the target will remember all that transpired during the period.

spells.name: Forget I
spells.description: Target forgets 10 contiguous minutes totally. The caster determines which ten minute period is forgotten.

spells.name: Mind Blank I
spells.description: Target’s mind is temporarily blank and he can do nothing. The target will remember nothing that occurs during the period. His body stops (all voluntary actions cease).

spells.name: Skill Amnesia I
spells.description: Any time the target attempts to use (roll a maneuver using) a skill with a strong mental component (e.g. one for which Memory or Reasoning stat bonus is used), there is a 20% chance that the victim will not be able to recollect the skill at all and cannot use it again until having slept.

spells.name: Forget X

spells.name: Mind Blank III

spells.name: Lord Forget
spells.description: As Forget I, except 200 minutes can be erased.

spells.name: Mind Death I

spells.name: Lost Experience

spells.name: Mental Block
spells.description: As Mental Block I, except memories of up to one hour/level can be delayed for up to one day per level.

spells.name: Mind Blank V
spells.description: As Mind Blank I, except duration is 5 rounds.

spells.name: Forget True

spells.name: Mind Death II

spells.name: Skill Amnesia III

spells.name: Mind Death III
spells.description: As Mind Death I, except the target blanks out three times per day.

spells.name: Mind Blank X
spells.description: As Mind Blank I, except duration is 10 rounds.

spells.name: Lost Experience
spells.description: As Lost Experience I, except percentage of experience lost is 10%.

spells.name: Lost Experience

spells.name: Mind Death X

spells.description: As Lost Experience I, except percentage of experience lost is 20%.

spells.name: Lord Mind Blank
spells.description: As Mind Blank I, except duration is 20 rounds.

spells.name: Mind Blank True
spells.description: As Mind Blank I, except the target has a 5% chance of blanking out each round. 8.7 8.7 - - Evil Evil Mentalism Mentalism MIND DISEASE

spells.name: Insomnia
spells.description: Target has trouble sleeping. Treat any sleep time as half as effective (e.g., count eight hours “sleep” as four hours). This hinders hit and PP recovery. See RMCL Section 5.5 for effects of inadequate sleep on fatigue. Drugs/herbs or spells may allow for temporary relief by forcing sleep.

spells.name: Neurosis
spells.description: Target has an extreme dislike for any specific thing the caster chooses. Target has a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) of overcoming the neurosis when given a choice (e.g., neurosis concerning horses would mean that every time the target tried to get on or near a horse the target would need to make a roll in order to do so).

spells.name: Guilt
spells.description: Target experiences chronic guilt over some action in his past. The GM should choose some significant event in the target’s past. The target will not perform the same or similar actions again and must take steps to alleviate the guilt (acting to rectify the past wrong or, if that is impossible, similar wrongs).

spells.name: Mild Paranoia
spells.description: Target believes everyone other than his close associates is out to get him.

spells.name: Panic
spells.description: Target must make an RR against Fear (use the highest level foe, or GM’s judgment for other perils, as level of Fear) at -10 in any personally dangerous situation.

spells.name: Phobia
spells.description: Target has a fear of a specific type of thing the caster chooses. The target has a 25+(three times his SD stat bonus)% chance of maintaining control around the feared thing. If the target fails to control himself, he has a 25% chance of fleeing from the object of fear (moving at maximum pace to get away from it), otherwise (75% of the time) he will avoid the object of fear at all costs.

spells.name: Hallucinate
spells.description: Target develops a tendency to hallucinate and see things as they are not. For each major event in a day, the target has a 10% chance of hallucinating and seeing major threats as nothing major and minor things as major threats (similar to the syndrome that Don Quixote suffered from).

spells.name: Split Personality
spells.description: Target develops a second personality with 8.6 Evil different values. There is a 10% chance per day that the second personality will become active (at a random time during the day). The Mentalism GM should select d10 character traits that must be extremely different Lists from the target’s.

spells.name: Psychosis
spells.description: Target has a psychosis about a specific type of thing of the caster’s choosing. The target has a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) of maintaining control around the object of his psychosis. If the target fails to control himself, he will blindly attack the object (attempting to destroy it at all costs) 20% of the time, avoiding it the rest of the time. If avoidance is not possible, the reaction will be violent.

spells.name: Catatonia
spells.description: Target drifts in and out of a catatonic state. Every hour there is a 25% chance he will become catatonic for one hour.

spells.name: Insomnia True
spells.description: As Insomnia, except target can only sleep if he uses drugs or spells to induce sleep. He adds +100 to his RRs against all sleep spells. If he gets no sleep, he will slowly go mad (GM’s discretion on the specifics).

spells.name: Neurosis True
spells.description: As Neurosis, except that the base chance of overcoming the neurosis is 25%.

spells.name: Guilt True
spells.description: As Guilt, except every day that he fails an RR (against the original level of this spell), the target must take actions to atone for his guilt (or sink into a deep depression).

spells.name: Kleptomania
spells.description: Target becomes tempted to steal things at every opportunity. It does not matter if the target even actually wants the things. The target has a chance of maintaining control equal to 25+(three times his SD stat bonus)%. If the target does not maintain control, he will steal something every time there is an opportunity. (Check no more often than once per hour.)

spells.name: Paranoia
spells.description: Target will trust no one absolutely. When in a life threatening/ dangerous situation, there is a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) that the target will not trust the protection of others and will act unilaterally for self-preservation.

spells.name: Pyromania
spells.description: Target becomes tempted to set fires at every opportunity. He will also take great pleasure in watching things burn, so this may tip off his companions. The target has a chance of maintaining control equal to 25+(three times his SD stat bonus)%. If the target does not maintain control, he will set something on fire. Check once per day; additional checks may be necessary if exceptional fire-setting opportunities arise.

spells.name: Paranoia True
spells.description: As Paranoia, except target will not associate with anyone for longer than one hour per day.

spells.name: Greater Hallucinate

spells.name: Psychosis True
spells.description: As Psychosis, except that the base chance of overcoming the psychosis is 10%.

spells.name: Split Personality
spells.description: As Split Personality, except the second personality is active 50% of the time.

spells.name: MPD
spells.description: As Split Personality, except target develops d10+1 extra personalities (Multiple Personality Disorder). There is a 10% chance per day that a non-active personality will become active.

spells.name: Catatonia True

spells.name: Mind Distraction
spells.description: Target is distracted by an imaginary object. For the duration of this spell, the target wastes 1 AP on this distraction each round.

spells.name: Mind Lock
spells.description: Caster and target are locked in mental contact until: the caster cancels the spell or the spell is dispelled or either the caster or the target has an RR failure of 30 or greater (both the caster and the target must make RRs against each other every round). The one failing by 30 becomes unconscious. While this spell is active, neither the caster nor the target can take any action.

spells.name: Mind Invasion
spells.description: As Mind Lock, except while target cannot take any action during the mental struggle, the caster may act as normal, within the restrictions of maintaining concentration.

spells.name: Dominance II
spells.description: The next Type “Fm” spell cast by the caster of this spell (cast within one minute of completing this spell) will have a special RR penalty of -10.

spells.name: Demonic Possession
spells.description: Target is controlled by a Type I Demon. The target may make an additional RR once per day (attack level equals Demon level) to regain control. The Demon is random, maniacal, and homicidal.

spells.name: Transferral
spells.description: Target’s “soul” and the caster’s “soul” are interchanged; the caster can operate at half of his normal activity (double all AP costs as with concentration) while ROLEMASTER in the body of the target. The target (in the caster’s body) is UNIFIED inactive. The caster can cancel the spell at any time (takes  one round). The target can cancel the spell if he makes a Spell Law successful RR (he gets one every 10 minutes). If either body is killed, both “souls” are destroyed.

spells.name: Lesser Object
spells.description: An intelligent magic item becomes submissive to the caster’s will and will obey the caster completely. If the item successfully resists, another attempt to dominate the item cannot be made until the caster gains a rank in this spell list.

spells.name: Dazed
spells.description: Target is mentally bewildered. Each round, he has only a 50% chance of making a decision. He will always defend himself from direct attack.

spells.description: All text that the target attempts to read will appear jumbled (and is indecipherable).

spells.name: Hypochondria
spells.description: The target believes that he is very ill (or sicker than he actually is). The GM should base the severity of the “imaginary” illness upon the amount the target fails his RR by (e.g., if he fails his RR by more than 50, he might think he is terminally ill).

spells.name: False Sense I
spells.description: Target will perceive something incorrectly through one of his senses. For example, he might see something out of the corner of his eye, or hear something that is not there. The sensory effect cannot last longer than one round.

spells.name: Misleading I
spells.description: Target’s perception is skewed. All Awareness maneuvers are modified by -10.

spells.name: Lesser Phantom
spells.description: The target is attacked by a creature of shadow. The creature is actually a hallucination and only the target sees the creature. The creature will immediately attack the target (in melee). The creature has the same DB, OB, and Hits as the target (use the target’s highest melee OB as the creature’s OB), but AT is always 1. If the caster casts spells upon himself, the creature acts as if affected by the same spell. The creature is immune to spell ROLEMASTER attacks. If the creature is slain (by killing or incapacitating it), the UNIFIED spell is cancelled. Creature uses same attack table as target, but  damage is not real. However, if damage would render target dead Spell Law or unconscious if real, target falls unconscious for the remainder of the spell’s duration.

spells.name: Waking Dream I
spells.description: Target will view things as if he were in a dream. His reactions are slowed (he suffers a -10 to all actions). In addition, he will sometimes perceive things differently than they actually are. The GM should roll for each event that occurs with 220 a 10% chance that the event is mistaken. For example, the target might see an approaching horse as a large wild animal; or he might see the charging Orc as a messenger bringing news. Note that the caster has no control over what the target perceives or how he perceives it.

spells.name: Mistaken Identity
spells.description: Target will believe that the next person he sees is someone else. At the time of casting, the caster chooses what type of person the target sees. For example, he might see a servant entering the palace gate instead of the skulking figure. If the next person attacks the target, the effects of this spell are cancelled.

spells.name: Suspicion
spells.description: Target suspects the actions of his associates (GM may give descriptions that arouse the player’s own suspicions).

spells.name: Trait Subversion I
spells.description: One of the target’s personality traits is perverted (that trait is taken to an extreme). If the target already has a tendency in one direction, it will be taken to an extreme: if spending habits were affected, a thrifty fellow would become a miser, while a generous spender would become a spendthrift. However, someone may still have a middle of the road trait affected, in which case it might go to either extreme (e.g., someone not particularly brave or cautious could become cowardly or reckless).

spells.name: Lying
spells.description: Target has a 20% chance of lying anytime he makes a statement.

spells.name: Trait Subversion II
spells.description: As Trait Subversion I, except 2 traits may be perverted.

spells.name: Cheating
spells.description: Target will attempt to cheat on all matters (e.g., splitting loot, card games, etc.).

spells.name: Vandal
spells.description: Target has urges to commit minor property damage. When such an opportunity arives, there is a 60% chance the target must attempt to commit such damage.

spells.name: Stealing
spells.description: Target becomes a sneak thief and has a 10% chance of attempting to steal anything that catches his fancy (if it does not involve violence).

The effects may wait years until being triggered; the triggered spell’s duration is based on the RR failure to this spell, where applicable.

spells.name: Transference
spells.description: Caster transfers one injury, wound, state of death, soul departure (not soul destruction), disease, or poison from the target to himself. With an Easy Spell Mastery maneuver, the caster may elect to only transfer certain components of an injury to himself, but he cannot break up an individual component of the injury (e.g., if an injury has done 18 hits, a round of Stun (-50), 2 hits/round of bleeding, and inflicted a -30 penalty, the caster might elect to transfer the Stun or bleeding as the most urgent components, and leave the target with 18 hits of damage and a -30 penalty; the caster could not, however, choose to only transfer 12 of the hits or 1 hit/round of bleeding, or -20 worth of penalty). The caster immediately suffers all effects transferred, while the target is immediately and completely cured of all such effects. However, if the caster would be killed by the transferred damage, they go into a coma and death is delayed for one minute (during which time subconscious healing spells may be cast to prevent death from occurring at all).

spells.name: Psychotranference
spells.description: Caster transfers one mental illness or addiction from the target to himself.

spells.name: Detoxification
spells.description: One poison or disease is totally removed from ROLEMASTER the caster’s system. This does not undo any damage already done UNIFIED  by the poison or disease, which must be cured separately. Spell Law

spells.name: Long Diagnostics
spells.description: As Diagnostics, except for range.

spells.name: Lose Addiction I
spells.description: Caster cleanses his system of one addiction. There is a recovery period of 1-10 weeks during which minor withdrawal symptoms will cause the caster a -10 penalty to all actions. 226

spells.name: Transfer Curse
spells.description: Caster transfers one curse from the target to himself.

spells.name: Instant Transference*
spells.description: As Transference, except that it this is an instantaneous spell.

spells.name: Curse Cure
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -10] Caster rids himself of one curse. If the curse makes its RR, the caster cannot try again to remove it until he has gained a rank in this list. (Note that if the caster has a different curse removal spell from a different list, then he does get a second chance with that spell.)

spells.name: Mind Disease Cures
spells.description: Caster is cured of one mental illness or malady. This will require a 1-10 day recovery period to take effect.

spells.name: Long Transference
spells.description: As Transference, except for range.

spells.name: Alter Transference
spells.description: For the caster’s next Transference spell, cast within one round/level of casting this spell, he may instead transfer the affliction to another (willing) target with a range of touch.

spells.name: Suspend Life
spells.description: Caster may suspend all life functions from his body, effectively entering suspended animation for the duration of the spell or until a fixed specified time has passed or a specified triggering event occurs (e.g., body brought to a specific person). While suspended the caster does not age, require food, water, or air, nor will any herbs work on his body although healing spells may continue to be used, but no further harm will come to him from any injury, toxin, curse, etc. until life resumes. The caster is not protected from further harm by external causes.

spells.name: Mass Diagnostics
spells.description: As Diagnostics, except each round caster may target any one individual within range of this spell.

spells.name: Transference True
spells.description: Caster may transfer any number of afflictions from 1 target.

spells.name: Lose Addiction II
spells.description: Caster cleanses his system of one addiction. There is a recovery period of 1-10 days during which minor withdrawal symptoms will cause the caster a -10 penalty to all actions.

spells.name: Complete Detoxification
spells.description: All poisons and diseases are totally removed from the caster’s system. This does not undo any damage already done by a poison or disease, which must be cured separately.

spells.name: Mind Disease Cures
spells.description: Caster is instantly cured of one mental illness or malady.

spells.name: Suspend Life True
spells.description: As Suspend Life, except for duration.

spells.name: Curse Cure True
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -50] As Curse Cure, except curse’s RR is at a -50 penalty.

spells.name: Lose Addiction True
spells.description: Caster cleanses his system of one addiction. There is a recovery period of 1-10 hours during which minor withdrawal symptoms will cause the caster a -10 penalty to all actions.

spells.name: Mass Transference
spells.description: Caster may transfer up to 1 condition/ level to himself from any number of targets within range. A condition in this context is a wound, injury, disease, poison, curse, mental illness, or addiction.

spells.name: Transfer Possession
spells.description: Caster transfers one possessing demon, spirit, soul, or like entitity from the target to himself.

spells.name: Expel Intruder
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -35] Caster expels one possessing demon, spirit, soul, or like entity from his person. If this fails, the caster cannot cast this spell again for 1-10 days, though he will get whatever normal RRs may be allowed to resist/expel the possessing entity.

spells.name: Transference Mastery
spells.description: Caster may use 1 spell from this list per round. 9.1 - Healer Base MUSCLE WAYS

spells.name: Severe Tendon Relief
spells.description: An injury to tendons incurring any penalty is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.

spells.name: Severe Muscular Relief
spells.description: As Severe Tendon Relief, except an injury to a muscle.

spells.name: Light Tendon Healing
spells.description: An injury to tendons incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Light Muscular Healing
spells.description: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a muscle incurring a penalty up to -20.

spells.name: Medium Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a tendon incurring a penalty up to -40.

spells.name: Medium Muscular
spells.description: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a muscle incurring a penalty up to -40.

spells.name: Joining ‡ *
spells.description: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the other 3 Healer Joining spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

spells.name: Severe Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a tendon incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Severe Muscular
spells.description: As Light Tendon Healing, except an injury to a muscle incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Light Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Light Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Light Muscular Healing
spells.description: As Light Muscular 9.1 Healer Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds. Base Lists

spells.name: Regenerate Tendon
spells.description: Caster can regenerate any 1 tendon. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Medium Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Medium Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Medium Muscular
spells.description: As Medium Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Joining True ‡ *
spells.description: As Joining, except caster must cast the other 3 Healer Joining True spells and recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Regenerate Muscle
spells.description: Caster can regenerate any one muscle (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Severe Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Severe Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Severe Muscular
spells.description: As Severe Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Mass Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Severe Tendon Healing, except it affects all tendon injuries.

spells.name: Mass Muscular Healing
spells.description: As Severe Muscular Healing, except it affects all muscle injuries.

spells.name: Mass Regenerate
spells.description: Caster can regenerate all of the muscles and/or tendons in his body (other than the heart). The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Regrow Limb ‡ *
spells.description: Caster can regrow 1 lost limb; requires use of the other 3 Healer Regrow Limb spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

spells.name: Mass Tendon Healing
spells.description: As Mass Tendon Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Mass Muscular Healing
spells.description: As Mass Muscular Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Mass Repairs True
spells.description: Caster can repair or regenerate a number of damaged muscles and/or tendons equal to his level. Recovery is instantaneous. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 227 9.1 - Healer Base ORGAN WAYS

spells.name: Light Organ Relief
spells.description: An injury to an organ (e.g. eye, ear, liver, but not the brain or nerves) incurring a penalty up to 20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, and the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll.

spells.name: Medium Organ Relief
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, but an injury incurring a penalty up to -40. This can be used to cure most causes of infertility (if the requisite organs are still present).

spells.name: Severe Organ Relief
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, but an injury incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Restart Heart
spells.description: Caster’s heart will start beating after having stopped. This does not repair any heart disease or damage that caused the initial stoppage.

spells.name: Sense Restoration
spells.description: Caster regains function in one sensory organ, so long as that sensory organ has not been completely destroyed (in which case Organ Regeneration is required). ROLEMASTER The recovery time is 1-10 hours. UNIFIED 

spells.name: Light Organ Healing
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, except the Spell Law recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Joining ‡ *
spells.description: Caster can reattach 1 severed limb; it requires the use of the other 3 Healer Joining spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

spells.name: End Coma
spells.description: Caster comes out of a coma or unconscious228 ness caused by injury, disease, poison, spell, or spell failure.

spells.name: Medium Organ Healing
spells.description: As Medium Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Sense Restoration
spells.description: As Sense Restoration, except recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Severe Organ Healing
spells.description: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Paralysis Cures
spells.description: Removes any paralysis problem suffered by the caster. The recovery time is 1-10 days. Treat as -100 injury for progress of recovery.

spells.name: Light Organ Healing
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds rather than days.

spells.name: Light Nerve Healing
spells.description: As Light Organ Relief, except an injury to nerves incurring a penalty up to -20, and the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Joining True ‡ *
spells.description: As Joining, except caster must cast the other 3 Healer Joining True spells and recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Medium Organ Healing
spells.description: As Medium Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds rather than days.

spells.name: Medium Nerve Healing
spells.description: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury to nerves incurring a penalty up to -40.

spells.name: Severe Organ Healing
spells.description: As Severe Organ Relief, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds rather than days.

spells.name: Severe Nerve Healing
spells.description: As Light Nerve Healing, except an injury to nerves incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Brain Repair
spells.description: Caster can repair brain damage (such as that caused by a skull fracture, etc. at the discretion of the GM). Recovery time is 1-10 hours. This includes damage from the Mind Death spell list.

spells.name: Organ Regeneration
spells.description: Caster can regenerate any 1 organ (external or internal), except the brain. The recovery time is 1-10 hours.

spells.name: Regrow Limb ‡ *
spells.description: Caster can regrow 1 lost limb; requires use of the other 3 Healer Regrow Limb spells in order to make limb fully functional. The recovery time is 1-10 days.

spells.name: Paralysis Cures True
spells.description: As Paralysis Cures, except recovery is instantaneous.

spells.name: Instant Brain Repair
spells.description: As Brain Repair, except instantly provides complete recovery, taking data stored in caster’s or original victim’s aura to reconstruct the brain exactly.

spells.name: Reuniting
spells.description: Caster may reunite his soul with his body, provided the latter is functional. To restore a dead target to life, a Healer would first have to heal all of the latter’s wounds (without “killing” himself) and then restore the target’s soul by giving up his own (for the target this process operates like the 19th level Lifegiving II spell on the Cleric Base list, Life Mastery). While technically alive, but without his soul, the Healer would be affected as if he had been “absolved” (see Cleric Base list, Channels); thus, he could take no conscious actions and he would be at -75 for all subconscious actions. The caster may regain his own soul through the use of this spell (which does not suffer the -75 penalty) or through someone else casting an appropriate spell. 9.1 - Healer Base SURFACE WAYS

spells.name: Heal II
spells.description: Caster is healed of 10 concussion hits.

spells.name: Fatigue Relief
spells.description: Removes up to -30 in penalties from fatigue.

spells.name: Stun Relief I *
spells.description: Caster is relieved of 1 round’s worth of accumulated stun effects.

spells.name: Regeneration I *
spells.description: Reduces damage by 2 hits every round as long as caster concentrates; if caster is unconscious, this spell will operate without concentration.

spells.name: Light Burn/Frostbite
spells.description: An injury from burns, chemical burns, scalding, or frostbite incurring a penalty up to -20 is cleaned, protected, and bound as effectively as if a successful Medicine maneuver was performed, the target receives a +50 bonus on their recovery roll and the recovery period is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Awakening *
spells.description: Target is instantly awake.

spells.name: Heal V
spells.description: Caster is healed of 25 concussion hits.

spells.name: Medium Burn/Frostbite
spells.description: As Light Burn/ Frostbite Healing, except an injury incurring a penalty up to 40.

spells.name: Stun Relief III *
spells.description: Caster is relieved of 3 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.

spells.name: Regeneration II *
spells.description: As Regeneration I, except 4 hits are healed every round.

spells.name: Severe Burn/Frostbite
spells.description: As Light Burn/Frostbite Healing, except an injury incurring any penalty.

spells.name: Heal X
spells.description: Caster is healed of 50 concussion hits. 9.1 Healer

spells.name: Stun Relief V *
spells.description: Caster is relieved of 5 rounds’ worth Base Lists of accumulated stun effects.

spells.name: Light Burn/Frostbite
spells.description: As Light Burn/ Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Regeneration III *
spells.description: As Regeneration I, except 6 hits are healed every round.

spells.name: Medium Burn/Frostbite Healing True
spells.description: As Medium Burn/Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Heal XV
spells.description: Caster is healed of 75 concussion hits.

spells.name: Severe Burn/Frostbite Healing True
spells.description: As Severe Burn/ Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to hours rather than days.

spells.name: Stun Relief X *
spells.description: Caster is relieved of 10 rounds’ worth of accumulated stun effects.

spells.name: Lord Heal
spells.description: Caster is healed of 100 concussion hits.

spells.name: Heal True
spells.description: Caster is healed of all concussion hit damage.

spells.name: Mass Burn/Frostbite
spells.description: As Severe Burn/Frostbite Healing, except affecting all such injuries.

spells.name: Mass Burn/Frostbite
spells.description: As Mass Burn/ Frostbite Healing, except the recovery time is reduced to rounds.

spells.name: Stun Relief True *
spells.description: Caster is relieved of all accumulated stun effects.

spells.name: Surface Healing True
spells.description: Caster is instantly relieved of all accumulated stun effects, concussion hit damage, fatigue penalties, and wounds due to burn, chemical burn, scalding, or frostbite. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 229 9.2 9.2 - - Mystic Mystic Base Base CONFUSING WAYS

spells.name: Distraction
spells.description: Target is at -30 for all actions.

spells.name: Confusion
spells.description: Target is incapable of making decisions or initiating action, but may continue to fight current foes or in self-defense. The target may also follow orders from the target’s leader (if any), but will only be able to follow them so long as no ambiguities arise.

spells.name: Blur Vision
spells.description: Target has a -100 OB modification for missile attacks and a -50 penalty for all other actions involving sight.

spells.name: Fear
spells.description: Target fears caster and attempts to flee. (This does not involve a Fear RR or the usual fear mechanics. Use a normal Essence or Mentalism RR and the result is flight for the specified duration.)

spells.name: Stumble
spells.description: Target becomes unbalanced. If the target is moving, the target trips and falls (prone and stunned (-50) for 1-5 rounds); any maneuver that the target was performing when affected by this spell automatically fails.

spells.name: Fumble
spells.description: As Stumble, except target also fumbles any weapon or item currently held; roll on the appropriate RMCL Chapter 11 Fumble Table.

spells.name: Hallucination
spells.description: Target sees a nonexistent foe and must fight this foe until it is “defeated” (i.e., takes damage that would drop the target). Foe has same capabilities as target but does no damage (i.e., always misses).

spells.name: Spin
spells.description: Target is spun about a few times, ending up facing 180° away from his original facing, then spends the rest of the round recovering from the disorientation. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Weapon Alteration
spells.description: Target perceives the target’s weapon(s) as UNIFIED  changed into some other similar weapon(s), which lacks the right Spell Law balance even if familiar to the target. Target will only have half normal OB with the weapon(s) so long as this delusion remains in effect.

spells.name: Mirages
spells.description: Target sees all moving things clearly, but the caster can control the fixed scene that the target “sees”. 230

spells.name: Blinding
spells.description: Target is blinded.

spells.name: Shifting Sight
spells.description: Target sees moving things shifted between 6" and 18" from where they really are; any attack the target makes against a moving foe has a 50% chance of having no effect.

spells.name: Mass Distraction
spells.description: A number of targets equal to the caster’s level can be distracted as in Distraction; they must all be in the caster’s field of vision.

spells.name: Word of Fear *
spells.description: As Fear, except the spell is instantaneous and the duration is 1 minute per 5 failure.

spells.name: Amnesia
spells.description: Target has amnesia, but loses no skills or abilities. This is a loss of personal memories and knowledge of own identity and background.

spells.name: Shout of Confusion
spells.description: As Confusion, except the spell is instantaneous and all beings (other than the caster) within an area encompassed by a radius of 50' are targets.

spells.name: Long Bewilderment
spells.description: As any single spell from this list of no more than 10th level, except the range is increased to 300'.

spells.name: Shout of Fear *
spells.description: As Fear, except the spell is instantaneous and the duration is 1 minute per 5 failure and all beings (other than the caster) within a fifty foot radius are targets.

spells.name: Rebellious Weapon
spells.description: As Weapon Alteration, except target must also make an RR each round or fumble his weapon. If the target is using two weapons, he must make a separate RR for each weapon and may fumble twice in a round due to the spell. He may still fumble normally if he gets to make an attack.

spells.name: Lord Bewilderment
spells.description: As any single spell from this list of no more than 10th level, except affects up to 20 targets.

spells.name: Amnesia True
spells.description: As Amnesia, except duration is permanent (unless dispelled).

spells.name: Parallel Reality
spells.description: Target sees and hears a world in which everything real has an image, but the image is changed or distorted (e.g., a Dwarf might seem to be an Elf, a house might seem to be a cave, etc.).

spells.name: Shout of Amnesia
spells.description: As Amnesia, except the spell is instantaneous and all beings (other than the caster) within an area encompassed by a radius of 50' are targets.

spells.name: Mass Long
spells.description: As any single spell from this list of no more than 10th level, except the range is increased to 300' and it affects up to one target per level of the caster.

spells.name: Bewilderment True
spells.description: As any single spell from this list of no more than 20th level, except affects up to one target per level of the caster and the range is 300'. 9.2 9.2 - - Mystic Mystic Base Base GAS ALTERATION

spells.name: Condensation
spells.description: Condenses 1 cubic foot of water from the surrounding air. Condensation can be channeled into any suitable receptacle, including caster’s cupped hands.

spells.name: Airwall
spells.description: Creates a wall of dense churning air up to 10'x10'x3'. This wall requires 1 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by one (e.g, a Run going in would be a Jog coming out), and attacks through the wall suffer a -50 penalty. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.

spells.name: Thick Fog II
spells.description: Creates very dense fog in a 20' radius; normal visibility is 1'.

spells.name: Vacuum A
spells.description: Creates a near vacuum with a five foot radius; all within this radius take an A Impact critical (if they fail an RR), as air leaves and rushes back in.

spells.name: Air Stop I
spells.description: Stops all generalized air movement up to winds of 30 mph and cuts higher winds by 30 mph in a 10' radius.

spells.name: Thick Fog X
spells.description: As Thick Fog II, except radius is 100'.

spells.name: Airwall True
spells.description: As Airwall, except has a set duration of 1 min/ level and does not require concentration.

spells.name: Bolt of Fire I
spells.description: A bolt of fire is shot from a fire source within 20' of the caster; results are determined on the Fire Bolt Table. Range modifier is based on the distance from the fire source to the bolt’s target.

spells.name: Vacuum B
spells.description: As Vacuum A, except results in a B Impact critical.

spells.name: Air Stop V
spells.description: As Air Stop I, except radius is 50'. 9.2 Mystic

spells.name: Thick Fog XXX
spells.description: As Thick Fog II, except radius is 300'. Base Lists

spells.name: Gas/Air
spells.description: All gas within 10' of caster is converted to breathable air.

spells.name: Vacuum C
spells.description: As Vacuum A, except results in a C Impact critical.

spells.name: Oxygenation
spells.description: Enriches a 50' radius of air with extra oxygen; within this radius all animal life receives a +20 bonus to their OBs and fire attacks deliver double the normal number of hits.

spells.name: Whirling Winds
