spells.description: Creates a whirlwind about caster; it moves with the caster and has a 10'R. No missile attacks can penetrate it and any movement (except for caster’s) into the whirlwind costs 2 AP and slows movement to a Walk pace, melee or elemental attacks on the caster through the whirlwind suffer a -50 penalty to OB.

spells.name: Bolt of Fire III
spells.description: As Bolt of Fire I, except range increment is 75'.

spells.name: Vacuum D
spells.description: As Vacuum A, except causes a D Impact critical.

spells.name: Greater Vacuum
spells.description: As Vacuum B, except radius is 10'.

spells.name: Reverse Winds
spells.description: Allows the caster to change the direction of the wind within the radius.

spells.name: Thick Fog True
spells.description: As Thick Fog II, except duration is 1 hour per level and the radius is 100' per level.

spells.name: Vacuum E
spells.description: As Vacuum A, except causes an E Impact critical.

spells.name: Cloud Mastery
spells.description: Caster has complete control of clouds within area of effect (includes fog); storm clouds can be gathered and made to rain (or snow or hail in suitable conditions), but they cannot be created.

spells.name: Greater Vacuum
spells.description: As Vacuum E, except radius is 10'.

spells.name: Toxic Cloud
spells.description: Creates a cloud of toxic gas. Any living thing coming within the cloud must make an RR versus poison each round or suffer a cumulative -10 penalty and take 2d10 damage from the effects of the gas. Treat the penalty as organ damage (lungs) for treatment/healing purposes and the final penalty as a single injury. Level of the poison is the level of the caster. The cloud takes one round to form, but the gas is barely visible (Very Hard Perception maneuver to notice by sight). Those in the cloud during the formation round will have their breathing irritated but suffer no damage if they leave the cloud before the next round.

spells.name: Gas Transmutation
spells.description: May transmute 1,000 cubic feet of gas (no part can be more than 20' away) into another non-magical gas that the caster has a sample of. This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 231 9.2 9.2 - - Mystic Mystic Base Base HIDING

spells.name: Blur *
spells.description: Causes caster to appear blurred to attackers, granting a +10 to the caster’s DB.

spells.name: Unseen I
spells.description: A single object is made invisible (1 garment, 1 naked body, etc.); until 24 hrs pass or target is struck by a violent blow (being hit by a weapon, falling, etc.) or target makes a violent move (i.e., an attack) or target casts a spell of type E or F.

spells.name: Shadow
spells.description: Caster and objects on the caster’s person appear to be in shadow and thus are almost invisible in dark areas (in many situations this could be handled with a Stalking bonus of between +25 and +75).

spells.name: Quiet I
spells.description: Creates a 1'R area into and out of which sound cannot travel. As explained in Section 10.17, this spell can be cast on an individual target, which the spell will then follow; this target gains +25 to Stalking attempts, but likewise automatically fails any Perception attempts based on hearing alone and suffers a -50 to Perception rolls where hearing is a factor (e.g., to detect someone Stalking the target). Note that this does not interfere with spell-casting (the target can still speak, even yell, the sound is simply unable to leave the radius of the Quiet - certain spells like Ultrasonics may have their range limited to the interior of the Quiet radius, however).

spells.name: Resilient Invisibility
spells.description: As Unseen I, except everything within 1' of the target is invisible as long as it is within the 1'R and none of the Unseen I termination conditions occur. The first time an Unseen I termination condition occurs, the target will become visible for one round and then become invisible again. If an Unseen I condition happens again, the spell ends. A target rendered invisible by this spell therefore can make one violent move ROLEMASTER (an attack) or cast one spell of type E or F or be struck once by a violent UNIFIED blow without losing the invisibility. 

spells.name: Resilient Invisibility
spells.description: As Resilient Invisibility I, except the caster Spell Law can vary the radius up to 1'. This makes the invisible target harder to see by reducing the “fringe effect”.

spells.name: Screens
spells.description: Creates a 1,000 square foot screen on which the caster can place any static scene; this scene seems 3-dimensional and normal. 232

spells.name: Displacement I
spells.description: Caster’s apparent location is offset from the actual location; all attacks have no effect 10% of the time. Each time a foe misses due to this effect, the chance of that foe missing again goes down 5%.

spells.name: No Sense
spells.description: As Resilient Invisibility I, except target is also undetectable by smell and sound.

spells.name: Shadow Mystic
spells.description: Creates a duplicate of the caster; if the caster concentrates, it will move as willed; otherwise, it does exactly as the caster does. The duplicate cannot move more than 100’ away from the caster. If the caster concentrates, he can “merge” the duplicate with himself and then split back apart again (making it difficult to discern which one is the “real” caster).

spells.name: Resilient Invisibility Sphere I
spells.description: As Resilient Invisibility I, except the constant radius is 10'. Note that anything that exits the radius will become visible again.

spells.name: Displacement II
spells.description: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 20%.

spells.name: Flattening
spells.description: Caster is flattened until becoming virtually two-dimensional. This allows the caster to slide through cracks and makes the caster effectively invisible when viewed from the side.

spells.name: Merging
spells.description: Caster can merge into any solid, inanimate material. While merged, the caster cannot move, perceive, or cast spells. When the caster emerges from the material, it is possible to emerge from any side or face of the material, although this is limited to moving “through” no more than the caster’s own body size plus two feet worth of material. The caster can exit at any time up to the end of spell duration (at which time the caster automatically emerges at the entrance point).

spells.name: Greater Resilient Invisibility
spells.description: As Resilient Invisibility II, except the invisibility can survive (level/5) termination actions. For example, if cast by a 15th level caster, the invisibility will survive three (15/5) termination events, e.g. a total of three violent moves, spell casting attempts or being struck violently, thus the caster could make an attack, be hit by an opponent, and cast a spell without losing the invisibility, but if the caster made another attack or cast another E or F spell or suffered another hit, the invisibility would end .

spells.name: Displacement III
spells.description: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 30%.

spells.name: Passing
spells.description: Caster may pass through any inanimate material up to 1 foot/ level. It takes 1 round to pass through 2 feet of material.

spells.name: Mystic Manner
spells.description: Caster’s actions are extremely subtle for the duration of this spell. Any form of non-magical detection or observation regarding the Mystic and his activities suffers a -50 penalty. The caster also may hide spell-casting without the normal Subtlety penalty to his spell-casting roll (and the -50 is applied to attempts to detect this casting as well).

spells.name: Great Merge
spells.description: As Merging, except caster can turn within the material and can perceive the outside world if within 6" of the surface.

spells.name: Displacement IV
spells.description: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 40%.

spells.name: Nondetect
spells.description: Caster and objects on his person cannot be detected by any “Detect...” spells.

spells.name: Displacement V
spells.description: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 50%.

spells.name: Dimensional
spells.description: Caster disappears into a private world. This place is largely featureless, but by concentrating, the caster can view (and listen) from the point of departure. The caster may also create any material objects desired, although anything not consumed by the caster will disappear upon leaving the dimensional closet (so the caster may eat, drink, take an antidote to a poison, etc., but cannot arm himself or emerge with great wealth). The caster will reappear in the same place when the duration of the spell expires or when the caster chooses to cancel the spell. If the point of departure is now occupied, the caster will be teleported D10x100 feet in a random direction.

spells.name: Displacement VI
spells.description: As Displacement I, except base chance of missing is 60%.

spells.name: Hiding True
spells.description: Caster can “Merge” into a material and may observe surrounding activity, and is affected by Unpresence, Nondetect, and Greater Resilient Invisibility all with this one spell. 9.2 9.2 - - Mystic Mystic Base Base LIQUID ALTERATION

spells.name: Boil/Freeze Liquid
spells.description: The caster may alter the temperature of any inanimate body of liquid of up to 1 cubic foot per level by 50°F [28°C] per round of concentration. Once the liquid begins to boil or freeze, it will not continue to change temperature, but will begin to warm or cool naturally if the caster stops concentrating. Caster is immune to the heat/cold thus generated.

spells.name: Clear/Desalinate Liquid
spells.description: Removes all sediment and dissolved substances from a liquid.

spells.name: Evaporate Liquid
spells.description: All targeted liquid (up to a maximum of 1000 cubic feet per level) is evaporated (i.e., boiled away and turned into heated gas) at a rate of one hundred cubic feet of liquid per round of concentration.

spells.name: Waterwall
spells.description: Creates a wall of water up to 10'x10'x1'. This wall requires 2 AP to cross, the pace of anyone moving through it at more than a Walk comes out with his pace reduced by two (e.g, a Run going in would be a Walk pace coming out) to a minimum of a Walk, and attacks through the wall suffer a -80 penalty. See Section 10.20 for additional details on Wall spells.

spells.name: Bolt of Water I
spells.description: A bolt of water is shot from a water source within 20' of the caster; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table. Range modifier is based on the distance from the water source to the bolt’s target.

spells.name: Water Corridor I
spells.description: Creates a corridor through liquid up to 100' long, 3' wide, and 10' deep (must be open at the top).

spells.name: Call Rain
spells.description: If there are clouds in the sky, it rains outdoors for the duration of the spell.

spells.name: Calm Water
spells.description: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.

spells.name: Wave
spells.description: Creates a wave moving away from the caster; wave is 1' high per level in the center and 10' per level wide.

spells.name: Waterwall True
spells.description: As Waterwall, except caster need not concentrate and the duration is 1 minute per level.

spells.name: Water Corridor
spells.description: As Water Corridor I, except limit is 300'x4'x50' deep.

spells.name: Liquid/Water
spells.description: Changes any liquid into fresh water.

spells.name: Lesser Whirlpool
spells.description: Creates a whirlpool with a 20 foot radius in any liquid body that will draw in any objects in the liquid up to 200' away. Objects capable of moving under their own power may 9.2 Mystic make a Very Hard maneuver to resist the pull of the whirlpool. Base Lists

spells.name: Water Tunnel
spells.description: Creates a tunnel through liquid that is 5' in diameter and 100' long.

spells.name: Calm Water True
spells.description: As Calm Water, except radius is 100'/ lvl and waves are cut by 50' in the center and caster need not concentrate.

spells.name: Command Current
spells.description: The area of effect for this spell is the water immediately surrounding the boat that the caster is on (where “boat” should be interpreted loosely; a log is a “boat” for purposes of this spell if the caster is riding it on water). For as long as the caster concentrates, the speed of the boat will be increased by 25' per round (approximately 3 miles per hour).

spells.name: Water Bubble
spells.description: Creates a bubble of air (10'R) around the caster and others, which will carry them in up to 100' depth of water (air is replenished). Caster can direct the motion of the bubble at a rate of 30'/round (may be adjusted by the presence of (un) favorable water currents. If/when the caster stops concentrating, the bubble will rise to the surface at the same rate (30'/round). If the caster resumes concentrating before the bubble surfaces, the bubble may be controlled again. All objects inside the bubble will rise with it. After reaching the surface, the bubble will burst.

spells.name: Bolt of Water V
spells.description: As Bolt of Water I, but range increment is 125'.

spells.name: Acidify Liquid
spells.description: Caster can cause up to one cubic foot per level of liquid into a magical acid strong enough to corrode an equal volume of metal or cause an A Acid critical to someone splashed with the acid or a D Acid critical each round immersed in the acid (with a C, a B, and an A critical coming in that order during the three rounds after getting out).

spells.name: Water Corridor
spells.description: As Water Corridor I, except limit is 100' per level long, 6' wide and 100' deep and the caster need not concentrate.

spells.name: Water Tunnel True
spells.description: As Water Tunnel, except limit is 6' in diameter and 100' per level long and the caster need not concentrate.

spells.name: Stream Diversion
spells.description: Caster can divert a water source or part of a water source into a stream which can be up to 15'x6' and as long as the range of this spell.

spells.name: Water Bubble
spells.description: As Water Bubble, except that caster need only concentrate on the desired route for one round and the bubble will “pilot” itself until the destination is reached and the radius of the bubble may be up to 20' in radius and the maximum speed of the bubble is 50'/round and there is no depth limit.

spells.name: Command Rain
spells.description: As Call Rain, except for area of effect and duration.

spells.name: Liquid Transmutation
spells.description: May transmute 1 cubic foot of liquid into another non-magical liquid that the caster has a sample of. ROLEMASTER This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work. UNIFIED  Spell Law 233 9.2 9.2 - - Mystic Mystic Base Base MYSTICAL CHANGE

spells.name: Study Individual
spells.description: Caster studies and memorizes the appearance and mannerisms of a being, for later use with higher level spells on this list. The caster must observe the target for five minutes after casting the spell in order to complete the study. The caster can only have as many forms studied as he has ranks in this list. Caster may choose to forget a studied form at any time to make room for replacements.

spells.name: Face Shifting True
spells.description: Allows caster to alter the form of his face. He can take on the exact face of another being if he has used Study Individual on the being he wishes to copy.

spells.name: Minor Changing
spells.description: Caster can alter his own form to the form of any desired humanoid race within his own Size category. The form can be that of a particular individual that has been the target of Study Individual; this does not include mannerisms, but only appearance. Note that only the physical form is gained (i.e., a target that becomes a Dwarf would not receive a Dwarf’s ability to see in the dark). Non-magical clothing will refit itself to the new form, but other equipment will not change, with the exception of prosthetics added by means of the Lay Healer Base list Prosthetics.

spells.name: Misfeel Kind • *
spells.description: For the purposes of mental or magical detections, caster appears to be of any race with which the caster is familiar.

spells.name: Deceptive Aura
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -50] Caster fools a magic item ROLEMASTER into reacting to the caster as if the caster were of another race, sex, UNIFIED  Profession, religion, etc. in order to make use (or better use) of the Spell Law item. This does not allow the caster to use a PP multiplier or Spell Adder for a realm he does not actually use, although it will allow use of a PP multiplier that works for only one of his realms.

spells.name: Major Changing
spells.description: As Minor Changing, except form need not be humanoid, but must be a living organic thing. 234

spells.name: Misfeel Calling
spells.description: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s profession may be misrepresented.

spells.name: Changing Lungs
spells.description: Caster can breathe water, air, or any gas at will.

spells.name: Shrinking Change
spells.description: As Minor Changing, except form may be one Size category smaller than the caster’s.

spells.name: Mystical Tongue
spells.description: [RR Mod: -50] Caster can speak mentally with any sentient target, and to the target, it will seem as if the caster was speaking in the target’s primary language.

spells.name: Misfeel Power •
spells.description: As Misfeel Kind, except caster’s level may be misrepresented (to an upper limit of twice the caster’s actual level).

spells.name: Impersonation
spells.description: As Major Changing, except a specific being who has been the target of Study Individual can be duplicated in both appearance and mannerisms.

spells.name: Unpresence Self
spells.description: Caster becomes undetectable by Presence spells or related abilities.

spells.name: Misfeel • *
spells.description: Allows caster to use all of the “Misfeel” spells at once.

spells.name: Changing True
spells.description: As Major Changing, except caster can alter forms at will, by concentrating for one round/change.

spells.name: Growing Change
spells.description: As Minor Changing, except form may be one Size category larger than the caster’s.

spells.name: Impersonation
spells.description: As Impersonation, except caster can alter forms at will, by concentrating for one round/change.

spells.name: Long Major
spells.description: As Major Changing, except for duration.

spells.name: Long Impersonation
spells.description: As Impersonation, except for duration.

spells.name: Unpresence True
spells.description: As Unpresence Self, except caster does not have to concentrate.

spells.name: Misfeel True •
spells.description: As Misfeel, except it has a ten minute/level duration and does not require concentration.

spells.name: Holy Presence
spells.description: As Misfeel, except caster can misrepresent his presence and power, so that he seems to be a minor deity. Even those viewing with normal senses will be given an impression of superhuman power and majesty.

spells.name: Shapechanging
spells.description: Caster can alter his own form to the form of any desired living thing. The form can be that of a particular individual that has been the target of Study Individual, in which case the caster may have the mannerisms of that individual as well. The caster gains all natural abilities of the form taken, but not magical abilities. Non-magical clothing will change form to fit the caster’s new body, or may join the rest of items worn or carried, which merge into the caster’s body for the duration of the spell. If the caster dies, the spell immediately ends.

spells.name: Shapechanging True
spells.description: As Shapechanging, except caster can alter forms at will, by concentrating for one round/change.

spells.name: Submerge Self
spells.description: Caster develops an entire false persona and places his own persona deep in his subconscious. To all detection attempts (except, perhaps, Mind Probe), the caster seems to be the false persona. The caster has no control over his actions (except for having designed the persona now in control), until the spell ends. The false persona can be a specific individual if Study Individual has been cast once/day (for 30 days) on that individual. The caster must determine a set period after which the spell will end and may also specify one triggering event that can cancel the spell before that period expires. The caster will remember everything that happened while the false persona was in control. 9.2 9.2 - - Mystic Mystic Base Base SOLID ALTERATION

spells.name: Enchanted Rope
spells.description: If the caster holds one end of a rope, he can cause the rope to move up to its length in any direction and to tie itself in knots (it cannot attack or tie up a living being).

spells.name: Heat Solid I
spells.description: Warms 1 cubic foot of any solid, inanimate material per level up to 500°F (260°C) at a rate of 1 cubic foot heated by 100°F (56°C) per round of concentration (without concentration, the material will remain at its current temperature for the remainder of the duration). The caster is immune to this heat.

spells.name: Cool Solid
spells.description: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -20°F (-29°C), at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round (cooling spreads evenly from initial point of cooling unless caster concentrates to direct spread).

spells.name: Cracks Call
spells.description: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.

spells.name: Door
spells.description: Can cause a door to expand or contract due to subtle temperature changes; door can be jammed or loosened (a special modification of +/- 50 to maneuvers to force the door can be granted).

spells.name: Woodfires
spells.description: Causes any wood to ignite and burn. All wood ignited must be within 1' of caster’s palm.

spells.name: Strengthen Material
spells.description: Causes any one object of up to 1 cubic foot per level of the caster to be much more resistant to damage. It will resist destructive spells as if the RR was being made by the caster and it will have a +50 bonus to any normal rolls against breakage.

spells.name: Heat Solid II
spells.description: As Heat Solid I, except the range is 50'.

spells.name: Chill Other Solid
spells.description: Any solid, inanimate, non-metal material can be cooled to -200°F (-129°C), at a rate of (1 cubic foot and -100°F/56°C)/round. The caster must concentrate to decrease the temperature (without concentration, the material will retain its current temperature for the duration of the spell).

spells.name: Wall of Ice
spells.description: Creates a wall of ice up to 10'x10'x (2' at base, 1' at top); it can be melted through or chipped through or toppled if not against a wall. Requires a source of water 9.2 Mystic or ice within 10'. See Section 10.20 for additional details Base Lists on Wall spells.

spells.name: Solid Door I
spells.description: Creates a doorway through any solid, inanimate material; it can be up to 3'x6'x1'.

spells.name: Stone/Earth/Mud
spells.description: Will turn 100 cubic feet of stone to packed earth, 100 cubic feet of earth to mud, 100 cubic feet of mud to earth, or 100 cubic feet of earth to stone. The transformation takes 3 rounds to complete.

spells.name: Shatter
spells.description: Causes a non-metal, inanimate object to shatter (up to 1 cubic foot in size). All within 5' radius take an A Impact critical; anyone holding the object takes a C Impact critical.

spells.name: Solid Door II
spells.description: As Solid Door I, except size is 4'x8'x5'

spells.name: Mold Solid
spells.description: Caster’s hands can mold up to one cubic foot of any solid, inanimate material as if it were putty or clay. The material hardens to normal consistency when the caster is finished. Quality of results depends on caster’s skill and time invested.

spells.name: Break Solid
spells.description: One cubic foot of any solid, inanimate material becomes very brittle. This causes the object to break if struck (the GM may rule that certain objects get an RR to resist breakage).

spells.name: Unstone
spells.description: Disintegrates (i.e., destroys without a residue) a target of up to 100 cubic feet of stone.

spells.name: Strengthen Material
spells.description: As Strengthen Material, except object can be up to 100 cubic feet per level in size.

spells.name: Unmetal
spells.description: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot of metal.

spells.name: Solid Tunnel
spells.description: Creates a tunnel through solid, inanimate material that is 4' in diameter and 5'/level long.

spells.name: Solid Door True
spells.description: As Solid Door, except size is 6'x12'x10'.

spells.name: Solid Tunnel True
spells.description: As Solid Tunnel, except that it is permanent and size is 3'x6'x (1'/level).

spells.name: Mold Solid True
spells.description: As Mold Solid, but working on a larger scale, suitable for monuments, architectural components, etc.

spells.name: Living Forge
spells.description: Caster becomes able to work non-magical metals with his bare hands, needing no other tools to give it the shape and qualities desired (gaining a +20 to any appropriate maneuvers rolled). This includes being able to create alloys if the necessary components are available. If this spell is used in conjunction with proper equipment, a ROLEMASTER UNIFIED +35 bonus may be gained to appropriate maneuvers. 

spells.name: Solid Transmutation
spells.description: Transmutes up to 1 ounce of Spell Law any solid material into another non-magical solid material that the caster has a sample of. This transmutation requires eight hours of uninterrupted work. 235 9.3 9.3 - - Sorcerer Sorcerer Base Base FLESH DESTRUCTION

spells.name: Sprain Limb
spells.description: A random part of a random limb is sprained. If it is part of a leg: movement is cut by 25%, combat is at -10. If it is part of an arm, combat is modified by -20.

spells.name: Limb Pain
spells.description: A random limb is in intense pain. If it is a leg, the target cannot walk (target may crawl at 10% of walking movement rate). If it is an arm, that arm cannot be used.

spells.name: Touch of Disruption
spells.description: The bones and tissues of the target’s body are disrupted as if the target had fallen a long distance. If the target’s RR fails by 1-10, the target takes an A Impact critical; increase the severity of the critical for each 10 by which the RR is failed (i.e. 11-20 = B, 21-30 = C, 31-40 = D, 41+ = E).

spells.name: Lock Joint
spells.description: One of the target’s joints locks. Target suffers as in Limb Pain, and in addition a moving target that is afflicted with leg lock will fall down; if a target with arm lock is carrying anything, it is dropped.

spells.name: Break Limb
spells.description: A random limb is broken. A broken arm incurs a -40 penalty, while a broken leg results in -25 and movement is reduced by 50%.

spells.name: Disruption II
spells.description: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 20'.

spells.name: Skin Death
spells.description: Target’s skin will flake and peel (on entire body). There is no tactical penalty, but it ruins the appearance ROLEMASTER of the target, resulting in a -25 to -75 on social interactions, deUNIFIED pending on the importance of appearance. Lasts until dispelled  and 1 month passes (recovery time may be reduced through Spell Law magical healing).

spells.name: Disruption V
spells.description: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 50'.

spells.name: Ear Disruption
spells.description: One of the target’s ears (randomly chosen) is disrupted. Hearing is at 50% of normal until cured (-25 to Perception involving hearing, -50 to Perception only involving hearing). If both ears go, the target will be rendered deaf. 236

spells.name: Limb Death
spells.description: One of the target’s limbs (random) will wither and die. Target loses 5% of the use of the limb per day; when the total reaches 100%, the limb falls off. Can be cured through flesh and nerve repair spells suitable for the degree of lost use (e.g., at 25% loss, treat as a -25 injury penalty, thus requiring Medium Flesh Relief and Medium Nerve Relief) before reaching 80%, after which it must be regenerated. If the limb is a leg, movement is reduced by half the percentage lost (e.g., if a leg is 60% withered, movement is cut by 30%). If the limb is an arm, combat suffers a -1 penalty per 2% withered (e.g., if an arm is 80% withered, all combat with that arm will suffer a -40 penalty).

spells.name: Eye Disruption
spells.description: As Ear Disruption, except an eye is affected and the penalties are to Perception involving sight.

spells.name: Collapse Lung
spells.description: One of the target’s lungs collapses. This restricts the target to half of normal activity for all physical activity (treat as 2 AP per round and half normal BMR) until the lung is repaired.

spells.name: Muscle Death
spells.description: As Limb Death, except a random muscle is affected (not muscle organs, e.g., not the heart).

spells.name: Bone Death
spells.description: As Limb Death, except a random bone (not part of spine or skull) gradually disintegrates.

spells.name: Black Channel I
spells.description: One of the Black Channel I spells may be used (see Section 10.7); the Gamemaster must decide which one(s) may be used on the basis of culture and world system.

spells.name: Rupture
spells.description: Target’s skin splits and begins to bleed. One bleeding wound opens for every 10 by which the target’s RR was failed, with each bleeding 1-5 hits per round (roll randomly for each wound).

spells.name: Long Flesh
spells.description: As one of Sprain Limb, Limb Pain, Lock Joint, Break Limb, Skin Death, or Ear Disruption, except with a range of 300'.

spells.name: Corpse Disposal
spells.description: Target must be dead; the spell causes a body to decompose almost instantaneously, including the bones or other hard parts, crumbling into a fertile dust.

spells.name: Mass Flesh
spells.description: As any lower level spell other than Disruption or Black Channel spells, except that it may be cast upon as many targets as the caster has levels.

spells.name: Petrifaction
spells.description: The target’s bones will gradually petrify; a loss of activity (as in Limb Death) occurs due to loss of blood production. Treat the loss of use as a penalty to all physical maneuvers and all Endurance rolls. At 100%, this effect becomes fatal.

spells.name: Disruption X
spells.description: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 100'.

spells.name: Black Channel II
spells.description: As Black Channel I, except one of the Black Channel II spells may be used.

spells.name: Disruption True
spells.description: As Touch of Disruption, except range is 300' and delivers an E Impact critical if the target fails to resist.

spells.name: Multichannels
spells.description: As Black Channel I, except that it may be cast upon as many targets as the caster has levels.

spells.name: Black Channel
spells.description: As Black Channel I, except one of the Black Channel III spells may be used. 9.3 9.3 - - Sorcerer Sorcerer Base Base FLUID DESTRUCTION

spells.name: Vaporize Fluid
spells.description: Vaporizes 10 cubic feet of liquid/level.

spells.name: Freeze Fluid
spells.description: Freezes 10 cubic feet of liquid/level.

spells.name: Evaporation I
spells.description: Causes 1000 cubic feet of liquid to rapidly evaporate over the course of 1 hour.

spells.name: Unwater I
spells.description: Instantly disintegrates 100 cubic feet of water.

spells.name: Calm Water
spells.description: All water within a 100'R is calmed; waves are cut by 20' in the center and less towards the perimeter. This will negate the effects of currents in the area of effect.

spells.name: Water Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of water is shot from the palm of the caster; results are determined on the Water Bolt Table.

spells.name: Unrain
spells.description: No precipitation will occur in the area of effect for the duration of the spell.

spells.name: Dehydrate
spells.description: Removes all the liquid (usually water) from 1 cubic foot of inanimate material.

spells.name: Evaporation II
spells.description: As Evaporation I, except 10,000 cubic feet are affected.

spells.name: Unwater II
spells.description: As Unwater I, except 1,000 cubic feet are affected.

spells.name: Unrain True
spells.description: As Unrain, except affects a 500'/level radius for 1 day/level.

spells.name: Dehydrate True
spells.description: As Dehydrate, except affects 10 cubic feet.

spells.name: Evaporation III
spells.description: As Evaporation I, except affects 100,000 cubic feet.

spells.name: Unwater III
spells.description: As Unwater I, except affects 10,000 cubic feet.

spells.name: Calm Water True
spells.description: As Calm Water, except radius is 100'/lvl and waves are cut by 50' in the center and caster need not concentrate.

spells.name: Acidic Bolt I
spells.description: A bolt of acid is shot from the palm of the caster; results determined on the Fire Bolt Table, but replacing Heat criticals with Acid criticals and also doing Impact criticals one severity level less than the primary critical.

spells.name: Long Fluid Destruction
spells.description: As any single spell on this 9.3 list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300', or Sorcerer a 75' range increment in the case of Water Bolt. Base Lists

spells.name: Blood Destruction
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -25] This spell takes advantage of any bleeding wounds on a target to draw out and destroy additional blood. Target suffers the equivalent of ten rounds bleeding from his wounds as a result of this spell (e.g., if the target were bleeding at a rate of 3 hits per round, this spell would inflict 30 hits of damage).

spells.name: Desiccation
spells.description: The target immediately begins to dehydrate and wither as if in a wind-blown sandy desert. Target is immediately at a -10 penalty to all actions and loses 1% of his total hits. For each round that the caster concentrates, the target takes another -5 penalty and 1% of total hits. If the caster maintains concentration for 10 consecutive rounds, animate targets will be reduced to dried out husks, dying. If the target gets out of range or the caster stops concentrating, there is no further damage, but the penalty and hits loss remain. The hits can heal normally, but the penalty recovers at a rate of 10 per day, assuming regular hydration. A Cure Disease spell can remove the penalty, once the hit loss is healed.

spells.name: Dehumidify
spells.description: For every minute that the caster concentrates, the humidity drops 1% of the original humidity (up to 50%). The humidity will gradually increase at a rate of 5% (of original humidity) per day until the original humidity is reached. Note that this change in humidity levels could affect an area’s climate (in turn affecting the area’s vegetation).

spells.name: Evaporation True
spells.description: As Evaporation I, except 1,000,000 cubic feet can be affected.

spells.name: Calm Sea
spells.description: As Calm Water True, except radius is 1000'R/level.

spells.name: Acidic Bolt V
spells.description: As Acidic Bolt I, but with a range increment of 125'.

spells.name: Mass Desiccation
spells.description: As Desiccation, except caster may affect up to one target/level.

spells.name: Dehumidify True
spells.description: Every minute that the caster concentrates, the humidity drops 5% of the original humidity (up to 95%). The humidity later increases as per the Dehumidify spell. Note that this change in humidity levels could affect an area’s climate (in turn affecting the area’s vegetation). ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 237 9.3 9.3 - - Sorcerer Sorcerer Base Base GAS DESTRUCTION

spells.name: Foglessness
spells.description: Disperses all fog in a 10'/level radius and prevents new fog from forming within that radius for 10 minutes per level. If a fog-creating spell makes its RR against this spell, it can create fog within the area of effect.

spells.name: Air Stop I
spells.description: Stops all generalized air movement up to winds of 30 mph and cuts higher winds by 30 mph in a 10' ROLEMASTER radius. UNIFIED 

spells.name: Vacuum I
spells.description: Creates a 5' radius of near vacuum; all within Spell Law the radius take a B Impact critical as the air leaves and rushes back in (if they fail an RR).

spells.name: Deoxygenation I
spells.description: Caster can remove the oxygen in a 10' radius at the rate of 2% of the original oxygen each round. 238

spells.name: Air Stop II
spells.description: As Air Stop I, except affects a radius of 20'.

spells.name: Vacuum II
spells.description: As Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 10'.

spells.name: Deoxygenation II
spells.description: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20'.

spells.name: Gas to Air
spells.description: Changes any gas to normal, breathable air at a rate of 1 cubic foot per round as long as the caster concentrates.

spells.name: Air Stop X
spells.description: As Air Stop I, except affects a radius of 100'.

spells.name: Vacuum IV
spells.description: As Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 20'.

spells.name: Great Vacuum I
spells.description: As Vacuum I, except delivers a D Impact critical in a 5' radius.

spells.name: Fast Deoxygenation
spells.description: As Deoxygenation I, except removal rate is 5% per round.

spells.name: Deoxygenation V
spells.description: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 50'.

spells.name: Great Vacuum II
spells.description: As Great Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 10'.

spells.name: Fast Deoxygenation
spells.description: As Fast Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20'.

spells.name: Air Stop True
spells.description: As Air Stop I, except affects a radius of 10'/level.

spells.name: Long Gas Destruction
spells.description: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.

spells.name: Great Vacuum III
spells.description: As Great Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 15'.

spells.name: Major Deoxygenation
spells.description: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20' and removal rate is 10% per round.

spells.name: Major Vacuum I
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -10] As Vacuum I, except delivers an E Impact critical in a 5' radius.

spells.name: Deoxygenation
spells.description: As Deoxygenation I, except affects a radius of 20' and removal rate is 20% per round.

spells.name: Major Vacuum II
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -10] As Major Vacuum I, except affects a 10' radius.

spells.name: Vacuum X
spells.description: As Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 50'.

spells.name: Major Vacuum IV
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -10] As Major Vacuum I, except affects a radius of 20'.

spells.name: Implosion
spells.description: Destroys gases in a single enclosure (usually a building) up to a size of 100'x100'x100' (or 1,000,000 cubic feet). The building has a chance of collapsing (dependent on construction) and everyone inside takes an E Impact critical from falling debris. The implosion will make a very loud noise. If the building collapses everyone within a distance from the building equal to the building’s height takes a C Impact critical from flying debris. 9.3 9.3 - - Sorcerer Sorcerer Base Base MIND DESTRUCTION

spells.name: Minor Pain
spells.description: Causes loss of 25% of target’s remaining concussion hits (i.e., those not already taken); these hits are healed when the duration is up, provided target has not died.

spells.name: Jolts III
spells.description: Target is stunned (-50) for 3 rounds.

spells.name: Disorientation
spells.description: Target must subtract 25 from all Perception maneuvers and subtract 5 from initiative rolls.

spells.name: Unbalance
spells.description: Target must subtract 25 from all maneuvers involving movement and subtract 5 from initiative rolls.

spells.name: Falter
spells.description: Target must subtract 25 from all combat rolls and subtract 5 from initiative rolls.

spells.name: Jolts VI
spells.description: Target is stunned (-50) for 6 rounds.

spells.name: Major Pain
spells.description: As Minor Pain, except 50% of remaining hits are taken.

spells.name: Forget
spells.description: Target forgets a period (of up to one minute per level) of the past, as desired by the caster.

spells.name: Word of Pain *
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -10] As Major Pain, except hits must be cured or heal normally.

spells.name: Mind Shock
spells.description: As As Disorientation, Unbalance, and Falter (all at once), except initiative penalty is 10.

spells.name: Mind Death
spells.description: Target mind blanks out for a period of 10 minutes each day at random. Target is incapable of activity or thought during this period.

spells.name: Jolts X
spells.description: Target is stunned (-50) for 10 rounds.

spells.name: Unminding
spells.description: Target’s mind is completely blank for the duration. The target cannot perceive events, time, or activity; when the target’s mind does come back, this produces one round of stun (-50) due to reorientation. 9.3

spells.name: Mass Disorientation
spells.description: As Disorientation, except for Sorcerer range, duration, and that it affects a number of targets up to the caster’s level. Base Lists

spells.name: Mind Break
spells.description: Target is a blathering idiot. The target can be led around, but can take no action.

spells.name: Mass Unbalance
spells.description: As Unbalance, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.

spells.name: Long Mind Destruction
spells.description: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.

spells.name: Mass Falter
spells.description: As Falter, except affects a number of targets equal to the caster’s level.

spells.name: Horror
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Target hallucinates an attack by a creature drawn from the target’s worst fears. The target will be attacked each round by the creature (doing real damage with a +100 Martial Arts Strikes attack). Regardless of the damage done by the target (to the creature), the creature will only be defeated when the target either loses consciousness or makes an RR (one RR each 6 rounds).

spells.name: Lost Experience
spells.description: Target loses 5% of collected experience (usually experience points).

spells.name: Mass Pain
spells.description: As Minor Pain, except as many targets as the caster’s level are affected, the duration is until healed, and the range is 300'.

spells.name: Memory Loss
spells.description: Target forgets completely about any one particular subject (e.g., the caster, the Fire Ruby of Romju, the Battle of Xon’s Crossing).

spells.name: Mass Mind Shock
spells.description: As Mind Shock, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected and the range is 300'.

spells.name: Mass Major Pain
spells.description: As Major Pain, except as many targets as the caster’s level are affected, the duration is until healed, and the range is 300'.

spells.name: Mass Unminding
spells.description: As Unminding, except as many targets as the caster’s level can be affected and the range is 300'. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 239 9.3 9.3 - - Sorcerer Sorcerer Base Base SOLID DESTRUCTION

spells.name: Melt Ice
spells.description: Melts up to 10 cubic feet/level of ice.

spells.name: Loosen Earth
spells.description: Loosens 100 cubic feet of earth to the consistency of plowed ground.

spells.name: Erosions
spells.description: Causes a 100 cubic feet/level section of inorganic material to erode and deteriorate at 1000 x normal rate.

spells.name: Cracks Call
spells.description: Any previous cracks or flaws in material up to 10'x10'x10' section will extend to their limit.

spells.name: Undoor I
spells.description: Will vaporize a non-magical door up to 6" thick, 10' high, and 10' wide (if the door is thicker than 6" it will vaporize the closest 6 inches).

spells.name: Stone/Earth
spells.description: Changes 100 cubic feet of stone to packed earth. This change is gradual, taking three rounds to complete.

spells.name: Splinter
spells.description: Causes one non-living wooden object to burst apart in a spray of splinters. All within a 5' radius take an A Puncture critical and holder takes a C Puncture critical.

spells.name: Earth/Mud
spells.description: As Stone/Earth, except changes earth to soft mud.

spells.name: Loosen Earth True
spells.description: Loosens 43,560 cubic feet of earth (1 acre-foot) to the consistency of plowed ground.

spells.name: Corridor
spells.description: Creates a 3'x6' corridor that is up to 1'/level long; it can be cut through any non-metal, inorganic material. The corridor can be cut at a rate of 1' (length) per round as the caster concentrates. ROLEMASTER

spells.name: Shatter
spells.description: Causes a non-metal, inanimate object to shatUNIFIED ter (up to 1 cubic foot in size). All within 5' radius take an A  Impact critical; anyone holding the object takes a C Impact Spell Law critical.

spells.name: Unearth
spells.description: Disintegrates 100 cubic feet of earth.

spells.name: Unstone
spells.description: Disintegrates 100 cubic feet of stone.

spells.name: Undoor True
spells.description: As Undoor I, except any single door is vaporized. 240

spells.name: Unmetal
spells.description: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot of metal.

spells.name: Solid Destruction
spells.description: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot of any inanimate solid.

spells.name: Long Solid Destruction
spells.description: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.

spells.name: Metal Corridor
spells.description: As Corridor, except that it can cut through non-magical metal.

spells.name: Crevasse
spells.description: Causes a large crack to open in the ground. The size of the crack will be up to 10' wide, up to 100' deep, and up to 200' long. The crack takes 2 rounds to open.

spells.name: Solid Destruction
spells.description: Disintegrates 1 cubic foot per level of any inanimate solid.

spells.name: Tremors
spells.description: Causes a very minor earthquake which could cause shoddy construction to collapse (and can be very terrifying). This is approximately 5.5 on the Richter scale.

spells.name: Great Crack
spells.description: Causes a large crevasse to open in the ground (up to 10' per level deep, 1' per level wide and 20' per level long). The crevasse takes 2 rounds to open. The caster can concentrate for 3 rounds to close the crevasse.

spells.name: Mass Shatter
spells.description: As Shatter, but affects up to 1 target per level (each individual target restricted in size as per Shatter). If someone is within attack radius of more than one shattered object, multiple criticals are possible.

spells.name: Metal Corridor True
spells.description: As Metal Corridor, except that it can cut through even magical metal. Note that this is a Force spell so the magical metal will normally get an RR.

spells.name: Quake
spells.description: Caster can cause an earthquake; with the caster’s location at the time of casting as the epicenter. The quake can be delayed up to 1 round/level. The severity of the quake on the Richter scale is determined by a roll: (0120) = 5.5, (21-45) = 6, (46-65) = 6.5, (66-80) = 7.0, (81-90) = 7.5, (91-95) = 8.0, (96-98) = 8.5, (99-100) = 9.0. 9.3 9.3 - - Sorcerer Sorcerer Base Base SOUL DESTRUCTION

spells.name: Question
spells.description: Target must answer a single concept question (presuming that the target can understand the question).

spells.name: Possession I
spells.description: Target is possessed by a Type I Demon; caster has no control over the target or Demon. The Demon will force the target to do random (not necessarily destructive) things. Target gets an RR every other round to attempt to get rid of the Demon.

spells.name: Neurosis
spells.description: Target has an extreme dislike for any specific thing the caster chooses. Target has a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) of overcoming the neurosis when given a choice (e.g., neurosis concerning horses would mean that every time the target tried to get on or near a horse the target would need to make a roll in order to do so).

spells.name: Guilt
spells.description: Target experiences chronic guilt over some action in his past. The GM should choose some significant event in the target’s past. The target will not perform the same or similar actions again and must take steps to alleviate the guilt (acting to rectify the past wrong or, if that is impossible, similar wrongs).

spells.name: Paranoia
spells.description: Target will trust no one absolutely. When in a life threatening/ dangerous situation, there is a 50% chance (modified by three times Self Discipline stat bonus) that the target will not trust the protection of others and will act unilaterally for self-preservation.

spells.name: Possession II
spells.description: As Possession I, except Demon is Type II and the possessed only gets an RR once every minute.

spells.name: Word of Panic *
spells.description: Target flees in total panic from caster. Fleeing equates to movement away at maximum pace for 1 round per 5 failure of the target’s RR.

spells.name: Transferal
spells.description: Target’s “soul” and the caster’s “soul” are interchanged; the caster can operate at half of his normal activity (double all AP costs as with concentration) while in the body of the target. The target (in the caster’s body) is inactive. The caster can cancel the spell at any time (takes one round). The target can cancel the spell if he makes a successful RR (he gets one every 10 minutes). If either body is killed, both “souls” are destroyed.

spells.name: Spirit Burn
spells.description: This spell torments a spirit, typically a conjured being like an elemental or demon. These spirits may be targeted even if in an incorporeal form or if possessing another’s body. The GM may wish to include some undead among potential targets. The pain caused by this 9.3 spell reduces the target to 2 AP per round and the target takes 1 hit per 1 failure on the RR as well. These hits do not return at the end of the spell Sorcerer duration; that aspect of the spell is “permanent”. Base Lists

spells.name: Possession III
spells.description: As Possession I, except Demon is Type III and the possessed target only gets an RR once every 10 minutes.

spells.name: Subjugation
spells.description: Part of the target’s “soul” is taken and placed in an organic object on the caster’s person. The body of the target will act as the caster directs (caster must concentrate).The spell ends when: the caster cancels it or the object is destroyed or the body is more than 100' from caster or the object leaves the caster’s person. If the caster is not concentrating the target may act freely, though at -30 to all activities. While being commanded, the target is aware of all the actions performed, but is helpless to stop them.

spells.name: Unsoul Curse
spells.description: Part of the target’s soul is transferred to a random object somewhere within 100 miles; the target is at -30 to all activities until the spell is dispelled or the target touches the object (which returns the missing piece of soul). The target will always know the direction of the lost “soul”.

spells.name: Possession IV
spells.description: As Possession I, except Demon is Type IV and the possessed target only gets an RR once every hour.

spells.name: Shout of Panic *
spells.description: As Word of Panic, except affects everyone within 20’ except the caster.

spells.name: Chronic Panic
spells.description: [RR Mod.: -10] Target must make an RR against Fear (use the highest level foe or GM’s judgment for other perils as level of Fear) at -25 in any personally dangerous situation.

spells.name: Spirit Repulsion
spells.description: [RR Mod.: +10] As Spirit Burn, except if target fails RR it must retreat to its own plane of existence, if applicable, or it will be transported instantly to its “home” location on this plane and is reduced to zero hits.

spells.name: Long Soul Destruction
spells.description: As any single spell on this list of no more than 10th level, but with a range of 300'.

spells.name: Possession V
spells.description: As Possession I, except Demon is Type V and the possessed target only gets an RR once every day.

spells.name: Dark Slumbers
spells.description: Target is put into a state of deep sleep and will neither awaken nor age. The target will only die if killed (does not need food, water, etc.). This sleep will continue until dispelled or until a single, non-magical event (specified by the caster at the time of casting) is accomplished. Note that the caster must specify the non-magical condition (i.e., it is not optional).

spells.name: Soultheft
spells.description: As Subjugation, except the “soul” is only released if the caster cancels the spell or the object is destroyed. The target is always aware of the direction of the object holding the stolen “soul”.

spells.name: Transferal True
spells.description: As Transferal, except target only gets his additional RRs once per month and every time he fails his chance to successfully resist decreases by 1 and the caster may operate normally in the target’s body.

spells.name: Long Absolution
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Target’s “soul” is torn from the target’s body (or other physical location if the soul is otherwise housed); it remains in whatever place “souls” go for 30 days per 10 failure. The soul can be returned early only through Lifegiving or Reuniting. Target ROLEMASTER is unconscious until soul is restored; even subconscious activity (i.e., UNIFIED healing, etc.) suffers a -75 penalty. 

spells.name: Possession VI
spells.description: As Possession I, except Demon is Type VI and the Spell Law possessed target only gets an RR once every week.

spells.name: Absolution Pure
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] As Long Absolution, except “soul” can only be brought back through Lifegiving.

spells.name: Dark Absolution
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] As Absolution Pure, except “soul” is utterly destroyed. 241 Chapter Chapter ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law Specific Specific Spell Spell 242 10: 10: Notes Notes 10. 10. Specific Specific Spell Spell This chapter focuses in on particular groups of spells that can prove more difficult than most to interpret or apply to specific situations, especially if the Gamemaster and players are coming to Rolemaster from a system that handles certain elements (e.g., illusions) differently. Still, it is impossible to cover every case or anticipate all potential questions. The general guide in interpreting spells is to use common sense and keep game balance in mind. Be especially wary of assuming abilities which are not actually granted to the spell by the text; this is an especially common source of 5) unbalancing effects. 10.1. B aRdic s ongs The Bard’s Base lists Controlling Songs and Inspiring Songs are somewhat more elaborate in their casting than most spells are. A number of special rules apply to them: 1) Normally, Controlling Songs affect all targets in the radius of effect except for the caster. If the caster is using Song Mastery (see Sound Projection spell list), he may elect to include himself as a target of the Controlling Songs spell. Inspiring Songs work as explained in that list. In either case, someone entering the effect radius while the song is still active becomes a target for the spell. 2) All of the spells on these lists require that the Bard provide some form of entertainment for the targets; this is the “performance”. This could take the form of singing, tale telling, juggling, etc. Since the performance is part of the spell-casting technique, use of the voice or hands for the performance counts as using them for spell-casting. 3) The area of effect listed is the base area of effect, used for a normal success on the maneuver roll for the performance. The duration is also determined by the maneuver roll for the performance, with a base duration in rounds equal to the number of ranks that the caster has in the skill used for the performance. If the caster uses two skills for a performance (he sings while playing the lute or dances while telling stories), the skill with the most ranks is used. The maneuver roll for the performance is made after the Controlling Songs or Inspiring Songs spell has been successfully cast. Stopping the performance will end the spell (unless it has been augmented with an Echoes spell), regardless of how much time is left on the basic duration. 4) After the duration given by the result of the initial performance maneuver roll is over, the caster may Notes Notes make a “continuation roll” (consisting of an Absolute Maneuver using the performance skill) each round in order to maintain the concentration needed to perform. Each additional continuation roll after the first one has a cumulative -5 modifier. These rolls never change the parameters set up for the spell effects by the initial performance roll. They succeed on a roll over 100 and fail on a roll under 101. They are simply to maintain the performance as is for one more round per continuation roll. The maneuver roll for the performance uses the following table. “Ranks” means the number of ranks the caster has in the skill used for the performance. Table 10-1: Bard Songs Roll Result Duration RR Mod. < 1 Absolute Failure - - Loss of confidence as well as the spell. No maneuver with this performance skill can succeed until you have slept. For the same period all Bard Base list spells will suffer a -10 modification to the SCR. 1 - 75 Failure 0 n/a Spell has no effect. UM 66 Unusual Event - - You inadvertently tap into the full power of your art. You cast an additional random Essence spell up to the level of the Song cast. The spell is cast at your level; if target selection is required, you know range, area of effect, and type (Elemental, Force, etc.) of the spell, but not the exact spell or what list it is from. 76 - 100 Partial Success Ranks x 0.5 +10 Spell has half listed radius. 101 - 175 Success Ranks +0 Spell works as listed. 176 + Absolute Success Ranks x 2 -10 Spell has twice listed radius. ROLEMASTER UNIFIED  Spell Law 243

spells.name: Armor Analysis
spells.description: Caster learns the materials of which a shield or armor suit/piece is made, what bonuses it has to DB and breakage rolls, the approximate time and place of manufacture, and whether the armor has any additional magical properties (but not what they are).

spells.name: Resizing Organic
spells.description: Refits an organic armor (full suit or individual piece) to correctly fit any individual in the same Size category as the armor. The adjustment takes one minute.

spells.name: Resizing Inorganic
spells.description: Refits an inorganic armor (full suit or individual piece) to correctly fit any individual in the same Size category as the armor. The adjustment takes one minute.

spells.name: Increase Armor Type
spells.description: Allows caster to create an armor piece which is considered one step heavier for protective purposes only (e.g., caster can create Light greaves that protect as if they were Medium).

spells.name: Armor I
spells.description: Allows caster to create torso armor with a +5 DB bonus or torso armor, limb armor, or helmet with a penalty (maneuvers, missiles, or perception) reduced by 5, or a shield with a +5 bonus to Shield skill. Multiple Armor spells may be used to reduce different penalties or to both increase DB and reduce a penalty.

spells.name: Automatic Sizing Organic #
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an organic armor (full suit or individual piece) so that it will always change its size to correctly fit any individual in the same Size category as the armor. It will alter to fit as it is being put on, so there is no delay for adjustment.

spells.name: Crit Negation I
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an armor piece so that it has a 5% chance of negating any critical hit to the area of the body (chest & groin, head, leg, or arm) protected by the armor piece. On a shield, it will negate crits to the shield arm plus a reduced chance (2% per 5% of normal chance) to negate any other crit by an attacker against whom the shield is defending.

spells.name: Automatic Sizing Inorganic #
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an inorganic armor (full suit or individual piece) so that it will always change its size to correctly fit any individual in the same Size category as the armor. It will alter to fit as it is being put on, so there is no delay for adjustment.

spells.name: Armor II
spells.description: As Armor I, except a bonus or penalty reduction of 10.

spells.name: Crit Negation II
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 10%.

spells.name: Mobility
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant a shield such that it can protect against one additional attack each round. This ability can be added multiple times.

spells.name: Multisize #
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item to fit itself to an additional Size category (e.g., the caster could make an item that will resize itself for either a Small or Medium user). For an armor, this will also require Automatic Sizing. For non-armors, which have less demanding fit requirements, this spell will function on its own. Multiple castings per day will allow an additional Size for each casting. In any case, Sizes must be contiguous; an Armor cannot fit Small and Big users, skipping over Medium.

spells.name: Crit Negation III
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 15%.

spells.name: Armor III
spells.description: As Armor I, except a bonus or penalty reduction of 15.

spells.name: Resilience
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item so that once per day, the item may reroll any failed breakage roll. If this spell is cast multiple times per day while enchanting an item, one reroll may be made each day per casting of this spell.

spells.name: Autofocusing
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant a shield that allows the bearer to perform a full Block by Concentrating (normally requires 4 AP) or a body armor that allows the wearer to do a full Dodge at the cost of Concentrating (rather than 4 AP).

spells.name: Crit Negation IV
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 20%.

spells.name: Defensive Size I
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant body armor so that the wearer is treated as being one Size larger when determining the effects of attacks on the wearer (e.g., a Medium wearer would be treated as Big for defensive purposes).

spells.name: Weapon Bane
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an armor piece (or set of pieces) so that any weapon which inflicts a critical to the area protected by that armor must make a breakage roll. A shield may also be enchanted with this spell. In that case, the breakage roll is made when the shield prevents a hit (i.e., when the attack misses, but it would have hit if not for the DB from the shield) or when the shield is deliberately struck.

spells.name: Crit Negation V
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 25%.

spells.name: Armor IV
spells.description: As Armor I, except a bonus or penalty reduction of 20.

spells.name: Crit Negation VI
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 30%.

spells.name: Defensive Size II
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant body armor so that the wearer is treated as being two Sizes larger when determining the effects of attacks on the wearer (e.g., a Small wearer would be treated as Big for defensive purposes).

spells.name: Crit Negation VIII
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 40%.

spells.name: Armor V
spells.description: As Armor I, except a bonus or penalty reduction of 25.

spells.name: Crit Negation X
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 50%.

spells.name: Armor VI
spells.description: As Armor I, except a bonus or penalty reduction of 30.

spells.name: Crit Negation True
spells.description: As Crit Negation I, except chance is 75%.

spells.name: Bladerune Analysis
spells.description: Allows caster to detect and identify all bladerunes on a single item. (Bladerune rules: bladerunes may be applied only to weapons and shields. The wielder must activate a bladerune via an instantaneous Runes maneuver penalized by the level of the spell that created it; the Anchoring Rune is active whenever its anchored rune is active. When a bladerune "fades" it disappears, its magic spent. Bladerunes are superficial enchantments subject to dispelling and Disenchantments, and count as active spells. They do not make an item count as magical in combat except during a round its effect is active. Most items hold up to four bladerunes; inherently magical materials hold six, Starfall eight. Bladerune effects are not cumulative within a type, but different types may be used together. Bladerunes are Force spells: a magical item must fail an RR to be runed, +1 to item level per existing bladerune; willing/intelligent items give a -50 RR modifier.)

spells.name: Weapon Rune I
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that increases the item's OB by 10. The bladerune fades when the additional +10 OB changes the result of an attack. This OB is in addition to the item's normal OB, magical or non-magical.

spells.name: Runekey
spells.description: Creates a bladerune which "keys" the runed item to a particular individual, who must be touching the item when this spell is cast. The keyed individual gains +30 to Runes maneuvers to activate bladerunes on this item, while all others suffer -30. This bladerune does not fade unless dispelled or, for a magical item, if it becomes attuned to someone other than the keyed individual.

spells.name: Shield Rune I
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that increases the item wielder's DB by 10. The bladerune fades when the +10 changes the result of an attack. This DB is in addition to any normally granted by the item, magical or non-magical.

spells.name: Weapon Rune II
spells.description: As Weapon Rune I, except the OB increase is +20.

spells.name: Runic Surface
spells.description: Allows caster to create an item which accepts bladerunes easily. This is treated as the item having the -50 RR penalty for a "willing target" when a Bladerune spell is cast on it.

spells.name: Resistance Rune I
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that provides the item's wielder with a +10 bonus to RRs. The bladerune fades when the +10 bonus changes the result of an RR.

spells.name: Shield Rune II
spells.description: As Shield Rune I, except the increase is +20.

spells.name: Range Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that increases the range of the weapon on which it is placed. A weapon (including a shield) which cannot normally be thrown may be thrown with a 10' range increment; a thrown weapon's range increment increases by 10'; a missile's by 25'. The bladerune fades when a ranged attack is made.

spells.name: Instant Return Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that will cause the weapon to return to its wielder when thrown or fired. The weapon returns via Long Door (it teleports) and the bladerune fades after returning the weapon.

spells.name: Resistance Rune II
spells.description: As Resistance Rune I, except the RR increase is +20.

spells.name: Slaying Rune I
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that adds +5 to a critical roll made for an attack using the weapon on which it is placed. The bladerune fades after a critical is rolled. This effect is cumulative with other Slaying effects, up to the maximum Slaying bonus of +25.

spells.name: Weapon Rune III
spells.description: As Weapon Rune I, except the OB increase is +30.

spells.name: Anchoring Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune linked to another bladerune on the same item. When that other bladerune would normally fade, the Anchoring Rune attempts to preserve it, drawing energy from the wielder: the wielder makes an Endurance roll at a penalty of twice the anchored rune's level. On success both remain; on failure the wielder suffers as for a failed Endurance roll and both runes fade. The anchored bladerune must still be reactivated between uses.

spells.name: Shield Rune III
spells.description: As Shield Rune I, except the increase is +30.

spells.name: Resistance Rune III
spells.description: As Resistance Rune I, except the RR increase is +30.

spells.name: Lesser Critical Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that adds an additional critical when a critical is scored using the weapon. The additional critical is one level of severity less than the original, of a type selected when the bladerune is cast. The bladerune fades after inflicting one critical.

spells.name: Weapon Rune IV
spells.description: As Weapon Rune I, except the OB increase is +40.

spells.name: Slaying Rune II
spells.description: As Slaying Rune I, except the critical roll is modified by +10.

spells.name: Shield Rune IV
spells.description: As Shield Rune I, except the increase is +40.

spells.name: Major Critical Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that adds an additional critical when a critical is scored using the weapon. The additional critical is of the same severity as the original, of a type selected when the bladerune is activated. The bladerune fades after inflicting one critical.

spells.name: Weapon Rune V
spells.description: As Weapon Rune I, except the OB increase is +50.

spells.name: Slaying Rune III
spells.description: As Slaying Rune I, except the critical roll is modified by +15.

spells.name: Shield Rune V
spells.description: As Shield Rune I, except the increase is +50.

spells.name: Slaying Rune IV
spells.description: As Slaying Rune I, except the critical roll is modified by +20.

spells.name: Resistance Rune V
spells.description: As Resistance Rune I, except the RR increase is +50.

spells.name: Life Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that will negate the next critical taken by the item's wielder. The rune fades after negating one critical.

spells.name: Death Rune
spells.description: Creates a bladerune that requires the first individual (after activation) to suffer a critical scored using the weapon to make an RR (versus a 50th level Essence spell) or instantly die. The bladerune fades after one use, even if the target makes the RR.

spells.name: Analyze Elemental
spells.description: Each round, the caster may learn one of the following about a single elemental: level, element (usually obvious), one talent, one flaw, one spell list, one skill bonus. Skill bonuses are learned largest first; talents and flaws from most DP involved to least; otherwise in random order within the chosen category.

spells.name: Express Elemental Nature
spells.description: Allows caster to bring out the elemental nature of an item into which an elemental has been embedded, giving cosmetic effects and granting the attuned bearer a bonus equal to the embedded elemental's level to Creature Lore: Elemental maneuvers and SCRs casting spells dealing with elementals.

spells.name: Improved Communication
spells.description: Cast once per day, gives an intelligent item the power of touch telepathy or of speech. Cast twice per day (double time and PP) it can provide ranged telepathy with a range equal to its level in feet.

spells.name: Manifest Talent I
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to one of an embedded elemental's talents up to a limit of 6 DP value.

spells.name: Bind Elemental I
spells.description: Allows caster to embed a Grade I elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Manifest Open List
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to one of an embedded elemental's Open spell lists. These spells may be cast using the wielder's PP or the item's PP.

spells.name: Manifest Internal Skill
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to three of an embedded elemental's skills for purposes of the item's own use or the direct use of the item.

spells.name: Tap Element I
spells.description: Enchants an item with an embedded elemental to provide +5 to RRs and DB against all physical elements (Earth, Fire, Ice, Light, Water, Wind) and +10 to all maneuvers and SCRs dealing directly with the embedded element. The wielder may cast up to 10 PP worth of spells from the corresponding Magician base list. A weapon does an additional critical of the appropriate type (Krush for Earth, Unbalancing for Wind, Impact for Water) two severities less than normal. Requires a Grade II or higher embedded elemental.

spells.name: Manifest Power
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to an embedded elemental's normal PP total, for use in casting spells from its lists.

spells.name: Bind Elemental II
spells.description: Allows caster to embed a Grade II or lesser elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Manifest Closed List
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to one of an embedded elemental's Closed spell lists. These spells may be cast using the wielder's PP or the item's PP.

spells.name: Manifest Talent II
spells.description: As Manifest Talent I, but limit is 20 DP.

spells.name: Tap Element II
spells.description: As Tap Element I but +10 to RRs/DB against all physical elements, +15 to maneuvers and SCRs of the embedded element, up to 15 PP of the corresponding Magician list, and a weapon's additional critical is one severity less than normal. Requires a Grade III or higher embedded elemental.

spells.name: Manifest Stat
spells.description: Allows caster to make one of an embedded elemental's stat bonuses available to the wielder, or one of its physical stat bonuses (Ag, Co, Qu, St) available for the item's use.

spells.name: Bind Elemental III
spells.description: Allows caster to embed a Grade III or lesser elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Manifest External Skill
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to three of an embedded elemental's skills for all purposes.

spells.name: Manifest Greater List
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to one of an embedded elemental's spell lists which is neither Open nor Closed. These spells may be cast using the wielder's PP or the item's PP.

spells.name: Manifest Talent III
spells.description: As Manifest Talent I, but limit is 40 DP.

spells.name: Tap Element III
spells.description: As Tap Element I but +15 to RRs/DB against all physical elements, +20 to maneuvers and SCRs of the embedded element, up to 20 PP of the corresponding Magician list, and a weapon's additional critical is of equal severity to normal. Requires a Grade IV or higher embedded elemental.

spells.name: Bind Elemental IV
spells.description: Allows caster to embed a Grade IV or lesser elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Manifest General
spells.description: Allows caster to provide access to general traits of an embedded elemental, such as its level for purposes of the bearer's RRs, a full set of senses (without talents) in item form, etc.

spells.name: Manifest Talent IV
spells.description: As Manifest Talent I, but limit is 75 DP.

spells.name: Bind Elemental V
spells.description: Allows caster to embed a Grade V or lesser elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Tap Element IV
spells.description: As Tap Element I but +20 to RRs/DB against all physical elements, +25 to maneuvers and SCRs of the embedded element, up to 30 PP of the corresponding Magician list, and a weapon's additional critical is one greater severity than normal. Requires a Grade V or higher embedded elemental.

spells.name: Bind Elemental VI
spells.description: Allows caster to embed a Grade VI or lesser elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Manifest Talent True
spells.description: As Manifest Talent I, but there is no limit on the talent's DP cost.

spells.name: Tap Element V
spells.description: As Tap Element I but +25 to RRs/DB against all physical elements, +30 to maneuvers and SCRs of the embedded element, up to 50 PP of the corresponding Magician list, and a weapon's additional critical is two greater severities than normal. Requires a Grade VI or higher embedded elemental.

spells.name: Bind Elemental True
spells.description: Allows caster to embed any elemental into an object as described in Section 5.3.

spells.name: Aura Analysis
spells.description: Caster learns the race, level, and profession of the individual(s) attuned to the target item. (Note: items on this list normally apply to a particular realm or Hybrid profession; restricting scope changes the working days/PP — single non-Hybrid profession halves them, single list one-tenth, Evil only one-third, two realms double, Universal triple. Items must be held in one hand by default; to function while merely worn or carried, double the working time and PP.)

spells.name: Casting Focus I
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +5 bonus to all Spell-Casting Rolls.

spells.name: Complex Aura
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item so that an additional person can be attuned to the item at a given time (e.g., if added twice, three individuals could be attuned at once).

spells.name: Elemental Power I
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +5 bonus to all Ball and Cone OBs.

spells.name: Spell Adder I
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which is a +1 Spell Adder.

spells.name: Power Multiplier I
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which is a x1.25 PP Multiplier.

spells.name: Casting Focus II
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +10 bonus to all Spell-Casting Rolls.

spells.name: Elemental Power II
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +10 bonus to all Ball and Cone OBs.

spells.name: Spell Adder II
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which is a +2 Spell Adder.

spells.name: Concentration Anchor
spells.description: Enchants an item that improves the bearer's ability to concentrate on spells, doubling the base duration of concentration (12 rounds in a stressful environment, 2 minutes in a calm environment, 10 minutes in a meditative trance); thereafter Endurance rolls need only be made at twice the normal interval.

spells.name: Casting Focus III
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +15 bonus to all Spell-Casting Rolls.

spells.name: Power Multiplier II
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which is a x1.5 PP Multiplier.

spells.name: Elemental Power III
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +15 bonus to all Ball and Cone OBs.

spells.name: Spell Adder III
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which is a +3 Spell Adder.

spells.name: Casting Focus IV
spells.description: Allows caster to enchant an item which provides the bearer a +20 bonus to all Spell-Casting Rolls.

spells.name: Increase Power
